## §87 Evidence Artefact Cross-Reference Patch: COST_MODEL §39 (Deal Close Governance, Win/Loss Analytics & Competitive Intelligence)

### §87.1 Purpose

`docs/COST_MODEL.md §39.9` (v2.5, 2026-06-15 — Enterprise Deal Close Governance, Win/Loss Analytics & Competitive Intelligence Model) defined three SOC 2 evidence artefacts as part of the enterprise deal close schema: **WIN-E-001** (CC5.2/CC1.4 — annual `enterprise.deal_closed_won` export with `floor_respected: true` attestation), **WIN-E-002** (CC5.2/CC4.1 — `enterprise.win_loss_analysis_recalibrated` chain export at Deal 10/20/50), and **WIN-E-003** (CC1.4/CC9.2 — quarterly `privacy.no_go_criteria_applied` export with zero-count affirmative ethics attestation). These were flagged in COST_MODEL §39.10 checklist items 5 and 6 (P1/M10) as requiring registration in this document. This section closes that obligation.

The three artefacts span four trust-service criteria: **CC5.2** (technology general controls operating effectively — the `floor_respected: true` Zod invariant and the governed recalibration chain prove commercial controls are technology-enforced, not procedural), **CC1.4** (commitment to integrity and ethical values — the price floor attestation and the no-go ethics audit trail together demonstrate FORM's stated principles are operationally enforced through the DEC-030 HMAC chain), **CC4.1** (monitoring of control activities — recalibration after actual deal data demonstrates FORM actively monitors and updates its commercial model), and **CC9.2** (risk mitigation — customer selection governance through no-go criteria demonstrates entity-level risk management at the customer intake boundary).

**Privacy floor (applies to all artefacts in §87):** No prospect company name, contact email, individual employee `user_id`, health data, biometric values, or coaching content in any exported row or artefact. `deal_id` is a FORM-internal UUID from `enterprise_pipeline_stages` — never shared externally or linked to individual health records. `win_primary_reason`, `loss_primary_reason`, `criteria_triggered`, and `competitor_category` are structured enums only; verbatim deal-call notes and prospect names remain in CRM only. `acv_usd` and `contracted_seats` are aggregate financial metadata with no link to individual health data. `form_api` Supabase role is REVOKED from `enterprise_deal_outcomes` (§39.5 RLS — form_system INSERT only; compliance_reviewer + form_admin SELECT for audit collection).

---

### §87.2 Annual Deal Close Integrity Record (COST_MODEL §39.8.1)

Source of truth for artefact definition: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §39.9` (WIN-E-001).

#### WIN-E-001

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | WIN-E-001 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC5.2 (entity selects and develops general control activities over technology — the `floor_respected: true` Zod invariant in the `emit-audit-event` Worker is a technology control that cannot be bypassed at the application layer); CC1.4 (commitment to competence — FORM's pricing authority matrix and pricing floor discipline require trained, authorised individuals; this artefact confirms no breach occurred) |
| **Description** | Annual export of all `enterprise.deal_closed_won` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events for the calendar year. Every row must carry `floor_respected: true` — the `emit-audit-event` Worker enforces this via a `z.literal(true)` Zod schema constraint: any Worker call attempting to emit `enterprise.deal_closed_won` with `floor_respected: false` (or `floor_respected` absent) returns HTTP 422 `FLOOR_RESPECTED_MUST_BE_TRUE` and does not append to the chain. The artefact therefore provides auditors with a complete, tamper-evident record that every enterprise deal closed during the observation year was priced at or above the contractual floor (`docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.5`). A year with zero `enterprise.deal_closed_won` events is filed as a zero-event attestation confirming the Worker validation gate is active and no deals closed — the absence of rows is not a gap. The artefact also captures `win_primary_reason` enum distribution and `contract_years` breakdown, providing context for the CC5.2 auditor narrative on commercial decision-making discipline. |
| **Collection method** | Annual (calendar year, collected in January following the observation period). Automated query against `audit_log_events` via the §81 evidence cron (`evidence-cron.ts`) for `event_type = 'enterprise.deal_closed_won'` with `created_at BETWEEN '<year_start>' AND '<year_end>'`. Export as CSV: `id`, `sequence_number`, `chain_hash`, `prev_hash`, `created_at`, `payload->>'deal_id'`, `payload->>'tier'`, `payload->>'contracted_seats'`, `payload->>'acv_usd'`, `payload->>'contract_years'`, `payload->>'floor_respected'`, `payload->>'win_primary_reason'`, `payload->>'sales_cycle_days'`. Assertion step: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM above WHERE floor_respected != 'true'` must equal 0 — non-zero count triggers P1 incident and compliance-officer review before filing. Compliance-officer signs off on the artefact with a one-paragraph attestation confirming the floor-respected assertion passed. File at `compliance/evidence/winloss/WIN-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`; append SHA-256 to MASTER-INDEX (§79). |
| **Cadence** | Annual — first collection: January following the first calendar year with at least one enterprise deal close |
| **Retention** | 7 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/winloss/WIN-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |
| **R2 bucket** | `form-soc2-evidence/winloss/` |
| **Privacy floor** | Export contains `deal_id` (FORM-internal UUID — not shared externally), `tier`, `contracted_seats`, `acv_usd`, `contract_years`, `floor_respected` (literal `true` — assertion gate ensures this), `win_primary_reason` (enum — no company name), `sales_cycle_days` (integer); no `user_id`, no prospect name, no employee name, no health data, no IP address, no coaching content; `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_deal_outcomes` at Supabase RLS level |
| **Status** | 🟡 Authored — first collection pending first calendar year with a closed enterprise deal (COST_MODEL §39.10 item 5, P1/M10) |

**Auditor narrative (CC5.2):** CC5.2 requires that the entity selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives. FORM's pricing architecture designates a set of price floors (documented in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.5` and enforced by the pricing authority matrix in §31.6) below which no enterprise contract may be executed. This floor is not merely a policy — it is a technology control: the `emit-audit-event` Worker validates the `floor_respected` field against a Zod `z.literal(true)` schema before appending the `enterprise.deal_closed_won` event to the HMAC chain. Any attempt to emit the event with `floor_respected: false` returns HTTP 422 and the event is not recorded. WIN-E-001 provides auditors with the annual export proving that every deal close recorded in the HMAC chain carried `floor_respected: true` — i.e., the technology control operated as designed throughout the observation year. Combined with the pricing exception audit trail (CC5.2 evidence `ETF-E-003` / `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` in §27.2.5), this establishes that both the floor rule and the exception governance process functioned during the period.

**Auditor narrative (CC1.4):** CC1.4 requires commitment to competence — management defines competency requirements and ensures individuals meet them. FORM's pricing authority matrix (`docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.6`) restricts discount authority by tier and discount depth: the founder approves discounts > 15%; customer-success agents approve ≤ 15%. WIN-E-001 is the evidence that these individuals exercised their pricing authority correctly: every deal close in the annual export has `floor_respected: true`, confirming the trained, authorised individuals who executed the "Close Deal" modal in Admin Console operated within the defined competency framework without exception.

---

### §87.3 Win/Loss Recalibration Chain Export (COST_MODEL §39.8.3)

Source of truth for artefact definition: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §39.9` (WIN-E-002).

#### WIN-E-002

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | WIN-E-002 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC5.2 (technology general control — the `enterprise.win_loss_analysis_recalibrated` event is emitted only when PIPE-CHAIN-02 is satisfied: `decision_log_ref` must be non-null and non-empty, otherwise the Worker returns HTTP 422 `PIPE_CHAIN_02_MISSING_DECISION_REF`; this enforces that recalibration events are always traceable to a decision log entry); CC4.1 (monitoring — FORM monitors its commercial conversion rate model with actual pipeline data and updates assumptions at defined thresholds, per `docs/COST_MODEL.md §37.3`) |
| **Description** | Export of `enterprise.win_loss_analysis_recalibrated` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events, filed at each OQ-PIPE-01 calibration threshold: Deal 10, Deal 20, and Deal 50 (`docs/COST_MODEL.md §39.7`). Each event carries five stage conversion rate actuals (`s0_to_s1_actual_pct` through `s4_to_s5_actual_pct`), `overall_win_rate_pct`, `deals_analysed`, `no_go_excluded_count`, `decision_log_ref` (the slug of the DECISION_LOG entry created before the event was emitted — PIPE-CHAIN-02 blocking invariant), and `cost_model_section_updated: '§37.3'` (a literal confirming the rate table in the Cost Model document was updated as part of the protocol). The PIPE-CHAIN-02 blocking invariant ensures the event cannot be emitted unless a matching DECISION_LOG entry exists — providing CC5.2 evidence that the recalibration decision was formally documented before the DEC-030 chain record was created. |
| **Collection method** | Per-threshold — filed at each OQ-PIPE-01 calibration event (Deal 10, Deal 20, Deal 50). The §39.7 four-step protocol specifies the filing sequence: Step 1 (run §39.6.1 SQL), Step 2 (create DECISION_LOG DEC-06X entry), Step 3 (update §37.3 conversion rate table), Step 4 (emit `enterprise.win_loss_analysis_recalibrated` via Admin Console with `decision_log_ref`). After emission, query: `SELECT id, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, created_at, payload FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'enterprise.win_loss_analysis_recalibrated' AND payload->>'deals_analysed'::int = <N> ORDER BY created_at`. Export as JSON: include the DEC-030 event row plus a three-field attestation: (a) DECISION_LOG entry slug matching `decision_log_ref`, (b) §37.3 git commit SHA showing the rate table update, (c) compliance-officer sign-off confirming PIPE-CHAIN-02 was satisfied. File at `compliance/evidence/winloss/WIN-E-002_deal<N>.json`; append SHA-256 to MASTER-INDEX (§79). |
| **Cadence** | Per-threshold — three planned filings: Deal 10 (est. M18), Deal 20 (est. M24), Deal 50 (est. M36). No annual cadence — event-driven only. |
| **Retention** | 7 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/winloss/WIN-E-002_deal<N>.json` (three files: deal10, deal20, deal50) |
| **R2 bucket** | `form-soc2-evidence/winloss/` |
| **Privacy floor** | Export contains aggregate conversion rates only (five floats, `overall_win_rate_pct`, `deals_analysed`, `no_go_excluded_count`) and operational metadata (`decision_log_ref`, `cost_model_section_updated`, `calibration_date`); no `deal_id`, no prospect names, no `user_id`, no individual deal financial terms, no health data; the `no_go_excluded_count` integer confirms how many no-go rejections were excluded from the conversion rate denominator without identifying any rejected prospect |
| **Status** | 🟡 Authored — first filing pending Deal 10 threshold (OQ-PIPE-01, COST_MODEL §39.11 item 8, P2/M18 est.) |

**Auditor narrative (CC5.2):** PIPE-CHAIN-02 is a blocking technology control on the `enterprise.win_loss_analysis_recalibrated` event: the `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `PIPE_CHAIN_02_MISSING_DECISION_REF` if `decision_log_ref` is null or an empty string. This ensures that no recalibration can be recorded in the DEC-030 chain without a traceable decision log entry. WIN-E-002 is the per-threshold export proving that every recalibration event in the chain satisfied PIPE-CHAIN-02 — the `decision_log_ref` field in each exported event points to a DECISION_LOG entry (step 2 of the §39.7 protocol) that was created before the event was emitted. This is the same chain-guard pattern used in `enterprise.pipeline_conversion_model_recalibrated` (PIPE-CHAIN-01, §37.9.1), confirming that FORM applies consistent technology governance to its commercial modelling lifecycle.

**Auditor narrative (CC4.1):** CC4.1 requires that the entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing evaluations to ascertain whether the components of the entity's system of internal controls are present and functioning. FORM's pipeline conversion rate model (`docs/COST_MODEL.md §37.3`) is a business planning control assumption — if the assumed S2→S3 conversion rate is 55% but actual data shows 30%, the model overstates pipeline output and underestimates required input. WIN-E-002 documents that FORM performs these ongoing model evaluations at defined thresholds (Deal 10/20/50) and updates the control assumption (`§37.3` rate table updated in step 3 of the §39.7 protocol, confirmed by git commit SHA in the artefact attestation). The monitoring loop — actual data → SQL query → DECISION_LOG update → §37.3 revision → HMAC-chained event — is the CC4.1 evidence that the commercial planning control is not static but is actively evaluated and calibrated.

---

### §87.4 Quarterly No-Go Criteria Ethics Attestation (COST_MODEL §39.8.4 / §39.9)

Source of truth for artefact definition: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §39.9` (WIN-E-003).

#### WIN-E-003

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | WIN-E-003 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC1.4 (commitment to integrity and ethical values — FORM's no-go criteria are a constitutive ethical commitment documented in `docs/ENTERPRISE.md §"No-go customers"` and `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-032`; quarterly export proves these criteria are operationally enforced through the DEC-030 chain, not merely stated in policy); CC9.2 (risk mitigation — customer selection governance at the intake boundary is an entity-level risk management control; refusing insurance risk-scoring customers, government-backdoor requesters, and wellness-as-punishment operators reduces regulatory, reputational, and security risk) |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of all `privacy.no_go_criteria_applied` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events for the calendar quarter. This event is auto-emitted as an atomic companion to `enterprise.deal_closed_lost` when `no_go_criteria_triggered = true` — the `form_system` Worker appends it as the second event in the same two-event array payload, guaranteeing the event cannot be omitted from a no-go rejection. The `criteria_triggered` field carries a structured enum value from: `insurance_risk_scoring` / `government_backdoor_request` / `wellness_as_punishment_use_case` / `other_no_go`. The `review_confirmed_by` field is the founder UUID, confirming the rejection decision was reviewed at the highest level. A **zero-count quarter is filed as affirmative positive evidence** — a signed attestation JSON confirming the query ran, returned zero rows, and that the no-go criteria monitoring is operational. A non-zero count is not inherently a problem (it may represent legitimate rejections); every row must be reviewed by the compliance-officer to confirm the `criteria_triggered` value is correctly categorised and the review_confirmed_by is a founder UUID. |
| **Collection method** | Quarterly (Q1: Jan–Mar, Q2: Apr–Jun, Q3: Jul–Sep, Q4: Oct–Dec). Automated via the §81 evidence cron (`evidence-cron.ts`). Collection query: `SELECT id, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, created_at, payload->>'deal_id' AS deal_id, payload->>'criteria_triggered' AS criteria_triggered, payload->>'review_confirmed_by' AS review_confirmed_by FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'privacy.no_go_criteria_applied' AND created_at >= '<quarter_start>' AND created_at < '<quarter_end>' ORDER BY created_at`. Zero-count case: emit a zero-event attestation JSON — `{ "artefact": "WIN-E-003", "quarter": "<YYYY-QN>", "query_run_at": "<ISO-8601>", "event_count": 0, "attestation": "Zero no-go rejections this quarter. No-go criteria monitoring operational. Confirmed by compliance-officer." }`. Non-zero case: export as CSV; compliance-officer reviews each row to confirm (a) `criteria_triggered` is a valid enum value, (b) `review_confirmed_by` is a founder UUID, (c) the companion `enterprise.deal_closed_lost` event (same `deal_id`, preceding this event in the chain) exists and has `no_go_criteria_triggered: true`. File at `compliance/evidence/winloss/WIN-E-003_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (or `.json` for zero-event attestation); append SHA-256 to MASTER-INDEX (§79). |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly — first collection: Q3 2026 (or the first calendar quarter after the Admin Console "Close Deal" modal is deployed, whichever comes first) |
| **Retention** | 3 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/winloss/WIN-E-003_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (or `.json` for zero-event attestation) |
| **R2 bucket** | `form-soc2-evidence/winloss/` |
| **Privacy floor** | Export contains `deal_id` (FORM-internal UUID — not shared externally; not linked to individual health data), `criteria_triggered` (four-value structured enum — no free-text rationale, no company name), `review_confirmed_by` (founder UUID — FORM-internal operator, not a prospect contact or employee health data subject); no prospect company name, contact email, or individual `user_id` in any field; the atomic companion invariant means a `privacy.no_go_criteria_applied` event can only exist in the chain if a preceding `enterprise.deal_closed_lost` event with the same `deal_id` was also emitted — no orphan emissions; `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_deal_outcomes` at Supabase RLS level |
| **Status** | 🟡 Authored — first collection pending Admin Console "Close Deal" modal deployment (COST_MODEL §39.10 item 3, P0/M9); first quarterly filing: Q3 2026 (COST_MODEL §39.10 item 6, P1/M10) |

**Auditor narrative (CC1.4):** CC1.4 requires commitment to integrity and ethical values at the management level. FORM has made a constitutive promise — documented in `docs/ENTERPRISE.md §"No-go customers"` and formally ratified in `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-032` — to refuse customers whose use cases meet four no-go criteria (insurance risk-scoring of employees, government surveillance backdoors, wellness-as-punishment deployment, other defined no-go cases). WIN-E-003 is the quarterly operational evidence that this commitment is enforced in practice: the `privacy.no_go_criteria_applied` DEC-030 event is emitted automatically by `form_system` whenever a deal is lost to a no-go criterion, creating a tamper-evident HMAC-chained record in the audit log. A zero-count quarter filed as affirmative attestation means FORM did not encounter any no-go prospects this quarter; a non-zero count means prospects were identified and rejected correctly. Both outcomes demonstrate that the no-go screening is running. The `review_confirmed_by` founder UUID field additionally demonstrates that the integrity commitment is actively affirmed by management on each rejection, not delegated to an automated system without human oversight.

**Auditor narrative (CC9.2):** CC9.2 requires the entity to assess and monitor risks associated with third parties and select business partners who demonstrate values consistent with the entity's commitments. In FORM's context, the customer intake boundary is the entity's primary risk management point for misuse scenarios. The no-go criteria represent a deliberate risk mitigation decision at customer selection: insurance risk-scoring customers would expose FORM to regulatory risk (GDPR Art. 9 special category data repurposing) and reputational harm; government backdoor requests would undermine trust for all enterprise customers; wellness-as-punishment use cases carry clinical-safety risk and violate FORM's privacy floor commitment (employees should not be disadvantaged by engagement metrics). WIN-E-003 provides quarterly evidence that FORM's intake screening is operational — the `privacy.no_go_criteria_applied` event chain proves each rejection was made transparently, categorised correctly, and reviewed at founder level, constituting a documented risk-management decision rather than an ad-hoc exclusion.

---

### §87.5 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping — Patch Summary

The following rows extend the control evidence tables established in earlier sections of this document:

| Criterion | Control | Evidence added by §87 |
|---|---|---|
| **CC5.2** | Price floor technology control: `z.literal(true)` Zod invariant on `floor_respected` field in `enterprise.deal_closed_won` event; HTTP 422 rejection if invariant violated; Worker emitter is `form_system` on Admin Console "Close Deal" modal | WIN-E-001 (annual export — every row `floor_respected: true`; assertion query confirms zero breaches during observation year) |
| **CC5.2** | Recalibration chain governance: PIPE-CHAIN-02 blocking invariant on `enterprise.win_loss_analysis_recalibrated` requires non-empty `decision_log_ref`; HTTP 422 `PIPE_CHAIN_02_MISSING_DECISION_REF` if absent | WIN-E-002 (per-threshold export at Deal 10/20/50 — each row confirms PIPE-CHAIN-02 was satisfied; DECISION_LOG entry referenced in payload) |
| **CC1.4** | Pricing competency and integrity: deal close authority matrix restricts discount approval by role and depth; `floor_respected: true` invariant ensures no unauthorised discount reaches the chain | WIN-E-001 (annual deal close export confirms no pricing floor breach during observation period) |
| **CC1.4** | No-go ethics commitment operationally enforced: `form_system` auto-emits `privacy.no_go_criteria_applied` as an atomic companion to `enterprise.deal_closed_lost` when `no_go_criteria_triggered = true`; `review_confirmed_by` field captures founder UUID | WIN-E-003 (quarterly export — confirms no-go screening operational; zero-count quarters are affirmative attestation; non-zero rows reviewed by compliance-officer) |
| **CC4.1** | Ongoing monitoring of commercial conversion rate model: OQ-PIPE-01 recalibration protocol triggers model update at Deal 10/20/50; §37.3 rate table updated and DEC-030 event emitted with PIPE-CHAIN-02 confirmation | WIN-E-002 (per-threshold export — documents that model monitoring and recalibration occurred) |
| **CC9.2** | Customer intake risk management: no-go criteria operationally applied at deal close boundary; rejections recorded in HMAC chain with structured enum classification and founder review | WIN-E-003 (quarterly export — confirms risk mitigation decision-making is operational at the customer selection boundary) |

| Obligation | Source | Status |
|---|---|---|
| COST_MODEL §39.10 item 5: "WIN-E-001 first filing — annual `enterprise.deal_closed_won` export with `floor_respected` attestation; filed January following first deal-close year (P1/M10)." | `docs/COST_MODEL.md §39.10` (v2.5, 2026-06-15) | **🟢 Closed (documentation) — §87.2** — artefact definition, collection method, and auditor narrative registered; implementation gate (Admin Console + first annual filing) remains in §87.7 item 2 |

## §88 Evidence Artefact Cross-Reference Patch: OBSERVABILITY §47 (DEC-065)

### §88.1 Purpose

`docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47` (v4.4.0, 2026-06-18 — DEC-065 OQ-SIEM-02 Resolution) defined one SOC 2 evidence artefact as part of the tenant SIEM export consent architecture: **SIEM-CONSENT-E-001** (CC9.2/CC1.1 — quarterly export of `siem.consent_addendum_signed` and `siem.consent_addendum_revoked` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events). This artefact was flagged in OBSERVABILITY §47.9 cross-reference obligation (P1/M8) for registration in this document. This section closes that obligation.

The artefact spans two trust-service criteria: **CC9.2** (risk mitigation through customer selection governance — every active SIEM export is covered by a signed addendum, demonstrating FORM governs the data flows it enables for enterprise tenants) and **CC1.1** (commitment to processing data in conformity with commitments — the SIEM-CONSENT-01 chain invariant ensures FORM cannot enable export without a documented written instruction from the tenant, consistent with GDPR Art. 28(3)(a) obligations and FORM's stated privacy-first enterprise positioning).

**Privacy floor (applies to all artefacts in §88):** No individual employee `user_id`, health data, body composition values, coaching content, or Art. 9 special category data in any DEC-030 event or evidence artefact. `endpoint_url_hash` is SHA-256[:32] truncated — not the full hash, not the plaintext SIEM endpoint URL (which may reveal internal network topology). `signed_by_email_hash` is `SHA-256(lowercase(email))` — no plaintext email address of the tenant_owner who signed. `tenant_id` is org slug (non-identifying for individual employees). `form_api` is REVOKED from `tenant_siem_configs` at Supabase RLS level.

---

### §88.2 Quarterly SIEM Consent Roster (OBSERVABILITY §47.8)

Source of truth for artefact definition: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47.9` (SIEM-CONSENT-E-001).

#### SIEM-CONSENT-E-001

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC9.2 (risk mitigation — FORM governs which enterprise tenants may route audit events to external SIEM endpoints; each active export is covered by a signed Addendum 4; the SIEM-CONSENT-01 chain invariant prevents enabling export without a prior signed-addendum chain record); CC1.1 (commitment to processing in conformity with commitments — FORM's enterprise DPA and privacy-first commitments include a documented instruction requirement before transmitting audit data to third-party endpoints; SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 confirms this instruction (Addendum 4) was obtained and chain-recorded for every active SIEM export during the quarter) |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of all `siem.consent_addendum_signed` and `siem.consent_addendum_revoked` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events for the calendar quarter. The export confirms: (a) every SIEM export that was enabled during the quarter has a corresponding `siem.consent_addendum_signed` event in the chain — SIEM-CONSENT-01 makes this an architectural guarantee, not a procedural check, because the `emit-audit-event` Worker rejects `siem.tenant_export_enabled` with HTTP 422 `SIEM_CONSENT_01_NO_ADDENDUM` if no prior signed-addendum event exists; (b) all addendum revocations during the quarter were chain-recorded with a structured `revocation_reason` enum; (c) no active SIEM export is unconsented — the cross-check query (SELECT tenants with `export_enabled = true` not appearing as a signatory in the signed-addendum export) must return zero rows. A **zero-event quarter** (no new addendum signatures or revocations) is filed as an affirmative attestation confirming the SIEM-CONSENT-01 invariant was operational and no new consents or revocations occurred — not an omission. |
| **Collection method** | Quarterly (Q1: Jan–Mar, Q2: Apr–Jun, Q3: Jul–Sep, Q4: Oct–Dec). Automated via the §81 evidence cron (`evidence-cron.ts`). Primary collection query: `SELECT id, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, created_at, payload->>'tenant_id' AS tenant_id, payload->>'addendum_version' AS addendum_version, payload->>'siem_destination_type' AS destination_type, payload->>'endpoint_url_hash' AS endpoint_url_hash, payload->>'signed_at_iso' AS signed_at FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type IN ('siem.consent_addendum_signed', 'siem.consent_addendum_revoked') AND created_at >= '<quarter_start>' AND created_at < '<quarter_end>' ORDER BY created_at`. Cross-check query (must return zero rows): `SELECT t.id FROM tenants t JOIN tenant_siem_configs sc ON sc.tenant_id = t.id WHERE sc.export_enabled = true AND sc.addendum_signed_at IS NULL`. Zero-event case: emit attestation JSON — `{ "artefact": "SIEM-CONSENT-E-001", "quarter": "<YYYY-QN>", "query_run_at": "<ISO-8601>", "signed_count": 0, "revoked_count": 0, "unconsented_exports": 0, "attestation": "SIEM-CONSENT-01 invariant operational. No new addendum signatures or revocations this quarter. Cross-check: zero unconsented active exports." }`. Non-zero case: export as CSV; compliance-officer reviews each `siem.consent_addendum_signed` row to confirm (a) `addendum_version` matches the current Addendum 4 template version, (b) `signed_by_email_hash` is a valid SHA-256 hash (not null or empty), (c) prior `siem.tenant_export_enabled` event exists for the same `tenant_id` within 5 minutes of the signed event (SIEM-CONSENT-01 ordering confirmation). File at `compliance/evidence/siem-consent/SIEM-CONSENT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (or `.json` for zero-event attestation); append SHA-256 to MASTER-INDEX (§79). |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly — first collection: first quarter after Admin Dashboard consent capture UI gate is deployed (§47.11 item 4, P0/M5); estimated first filing Q3 2026 |
| **Retention** | 7 years (SIEM consent records are contractual obligation evidence; 7yr matches MSA retention per `docs/ENTERPRISE_SLA.md §19.5`) |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/siem-consent/SIEM-CONSENT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (or `.json` for zero-event attestation) |
| **R2 bucket** | `form-soc2-evidence/siem-consent/` |
| **Privacy floor** | `endpoint_url_hash` is SHA-256[:32] truncated — not the full 64-char hash, not the plaintext SIEM endpoint URL (which may reveal internal network architecture); `signed_by_email_hash` is `SHA-256(lowercase(email))` — no plaintext email; `tenant_id` is org slug (no individual employee identifiers); no `user_id`, no health data, no coaching content, no biometric values in any row; `form_api` REVOKED from `tenant_siem_configs` at RLS level; cross-check query uses aggregate count (zero) — no tenant identifiers in zero-event attestation JSON |
| **Status** | 🟡 Authored — first collection pending Admin Dashboard consent capture UI gate deployment (§47.11 item 4, P0/M5; est. Q3 2026) |

**Auditor narrative (CC9.2):** CC9.2 requires the entity to assess and manage risks associated with third parties who have access to system information. Enterprise tenants who enable SIEM export route FORM's audit event stream — which includes security-relevant events (auth failures, SCIM changes, admin actions) — to an endpoint they control. Without a documented agreement governing this data flow, FORM cannot demonstrate oversight of how the audit stream is consumed on the tenant side. MSA Addendum 4 requires tenants to warrant that their SIEM platform processes data under an adequate legal basis and designates the tenant as controller for their own SIEM processing. SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 is the quarterly evidence that every active SIEM export was initiated with a signed Addendum 4 — FORM did not enable a single export without the tenant's documented written instruction (SIEM-CONSENT-01 chain invariant). The zero-row cross-check (`SELECT tenants with export_enabled = true AND addendum_signed_at IS NULL`) is the direct CC9.2 assertion: it confirms there is no active third-party data flow from FORM's audit pipeline without a governing agreement.

**Auditor narrative (CC1.1):** CC1.1 requires the entity to design, implement, and operate its system to meet its commitments to data protection and privacy. FORM's enterprise commitments include a "privacy-first" posture documented in `docs/ENTERPRISE.md §Privacy floor for enterprise` and operationalised through the HMAC-chained audit log architecture. Enabling SIEM export transmits (redacted) DEC-030 audit events to a tenant-designated third-party system — this is consistent with FORM's commitments only if the transmission is governed by a documented written instruction from the controller (the enterprise tenant). GDPR Art. 28(3)(a) requires processors to process data only on the controller's documented instructions. SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 confirms that this instruction (Addendum 4) was obtained and chain-recorded for every active SIEM export during the observation quarter. The SIEM-CONSENT-01 chain invariant converts this from a procedural commitment into a technology control: the `emit-audit-event` Worker cannot emit `siem.tenant_export_enabled` without a prior chain record of the signed instruction, making the CC1.1 commitment enforceable at the infrastructure layer.

---

### §88.3 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping — Patch Summary

The following rows extend the control evidence tables established in earlier sections of this document:

| Criterion | Control | Evidence added by §88 |
|---|---|---|
| **CC9.2** | Tenant SIEM export governed by signed MSA Addendum 4; SIEM-CONSENT-01 chain invariant prevents `siem.tenant_export_enabled` without prior `siem.consent_addendum_signed` in HMAC chain; HTTP 422 `SIEM_CONSENT_01_NO_ADDENDUM` if invariant violated; Admin Dashboard consent gate captures Addendum 4 acceptance in-product | SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 (quarterly export — every row in the signed-addendum export is a `tenant_id` with documented consent; cross-check query confirms zero unconsented active exports at collection time) |
| **CC1.1** | GDPR Art. 28(3)(a) written instruction requirement met by per-tenant Addendum 4 signature before any audit event transmission to tenant-specified SIEM endpoint; SIEM-CONSENT-01 enforces this at technology layer — not relying on UI-layer consent flow alone | SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 (quarterly export — confirms instruction-before-transmission ordering for every SIEM export enabled during the quarter; zero-event quarters confirm invariant was operational with no new exports or revocations) |

| 2 | After first quarter with at least one SIEM export enabled: run the SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 primary query and cross-check query (`export_enabled = true AND addendum_signed_at IS NULL` must return zero rows); if zero signed events, file the zero-event attestation JSON; if non-zero, export as CSV and review each row per §88.2 protocol; file at `compliance/evidence/siem-consent/SIEM-CONSENT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv`; append SHA-256 to MASTER-INDEX (§79). | compliance-officer + data-engineer | **P1** | Q3 2026 (or first quarter after Admin Dashboard consent gate deployment, whichever is later) | [ ] |

---

Four artefact rows added to §79.4: WIN-E-001 (CC5.2/CC1.4 — annual deal_closed_won export; `winloss/`), WIN-E-002 (CC5.2/CC4.1 — win_loss_analysis_recalibrated export at Deal 10/20/50; `winloss/`), WIN-E-003 (CC1.4/CC9.2 — quarterly no_go_criteria_applied export; `winloss/`), SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 (CC9.2/CC1.1 — quarterly SIEM consent roster; `siem-consent/`). §80.3 folder listing extended with `winloss/` and `siem-consent/` entries, each annotated `form-api NO ACCESS`. Document header corrected v3.10.0 → v3.13.1. No new evidence artefacts authored; no gap score change. Owner: compliance-officer.*

*v3.13.0 (2026-06-18): §88 Evidence Artefact Cross-Reference Patch — OBSERVABILITY §47 (DEC-065, OQ-SIEM-02 Resolution). Registers one SOC 2 evidence artefact defined in `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47.9` (v4.4.0, 2026-06-18 — DEC-065 OQ-SIEM-02 Resolution — Tenant SIEM Export Consent, Per-Tenant DPA Addendum Design & SIEM-CONSENT-01 Chain Invariant) that was flagged for addition to this document in OBSERVABILITY §47.9 cross-reference obligation (P1/M8). **SIEM-CONSENT-E-001** (CC9.2/CC1.1 — quarterly export of `siem.consent_addendum_signed` and `siem.consent_addendum_revoked` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events; zero-event attestation for quarters with no consent activity; cross-check query confirming zero unconsented active SIEM exports at collection time; 7yr retention; `compliance/evidence/siem-consent/SIEM-CONSENT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv`). §88.3 extends two SOC 2 criterion rows (CC9.2 × 1 — SIEM-CONSENT-01 technology-layer enforcement of per-tenant Addendum 4 requirement; CC1.1 × 1 — GDPR Art. 28(3)(a) documented-instruction requirement met by signed addendum before any transmission). §88.4 closes two cross-reference obligations from `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47.9`. §88.5 two-item implementation checklist: 1× P1/M8 (SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 addition to §79.4 master evidence table + §80.3 R2 path note); 1× P1/Q3 2026 (first quarterly filing after consent gate deployment). ## §89 Evidence Artefact Cross-Reference Patch: MSA_TEMPLATE Addendum 5 (Partner Agreement — CC9.2 / CC4.1 / CC7.4)

### §89.1 Purpose

`docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md Addendum 5` (v1.0, 2026-06-18 — Partner Agreement Addendum) closes `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §38.8` checklist item 3 (P0 — "Add partner privacy floor section to Partner Agreement template with three non-waivable clauses and PART-CHAIN-01 escalation path"). Three SOC 2 evidence artefacts are defined in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise Partner Events` (v2.9) and referenced in Addendum 5 §5.6: **PART-E-001** (CC9.2 — annual partner agreement execution record), **PART-E-002** (CC9.2/CC4.1 — annual partner revenue share payment governance), **PART-E-003** (CC9.2/CC7.4 — quarterly partner offboarding with PART-CHAIN-01 evidence). These were listed in §79.4 and §80.4 but lacked a dedicated cross-reference section confirming collection protocol and CC mapping. This section closes that obligation.

**Privacy floor (all three artefacts):** No individual employee `user_id`, health values, coaching content, prospect company name, prospect contact email, deal free-text notes, or Art. 9 category data in any row. `partner_id` is a FORM-internal UUID. `deal_id` is the same FORM-internal UUID from `enterprise_pipeline_stages`. `partner_name` is stored in `enterprise_partners` for FORM-internal operations only — never in DEC-030 chain payloads or any exported artefact. `form_api` is REVOKED from `enterprise_partners` at RLS level (migration 0073).

---

### §89.2 Evidence Artefacts

**Primary definitions:** `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise Partner Events` (canonical Zod schemas, event payloads, PART-CHAIN-01 specification). This section registers collection protocol and auditor narratives only; AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md is the source of truth on payload structure.

#### PART-E-001 — Annual Partner Agreement Execution Record

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | PART-E-001 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC9.2 (vendor and business partner agreements in place — demonstrated by chain record of agreement execution and DPA status per partner category) |
| **Description** | Annual export of all `enterprise.partner_agreement_signed` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events for the calendar year. Every Reseller and Integration partner row must carry `dpa_signed: true` — the `emit-audit-event` Worker enforces this at chain-append time (HTTP 422 if `dpa_signed = false` for those categories). Referral partners without personal-data transfer may carry `dpa_signed: false` with no constraint violation. A year with zero `enterprise.partner_agreement_signed` events is filed as a zero-event attestation confirming the Worker validation gate is active and no new partners were onboarded — the absence of rows is not a gap. |
| **Collection method** | Annual (Q1 of the following year). Query: `SELECT id, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, created_at, payload->>'partner_id' AS partner_id, payload->>'partner_category' AS partner_category, payload->>'revenue_share_pct' AS revenue_share_pct, payload->>'agreement_doc_ref' AS agreement_doc_ref, payload->>'dpa_signed' AS dpa_signed FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'enterprise.partner_agreement_signed' AND created_at >= '<year_start>' AND created_at < '<year_end>' ORDER BY created_at`. Cross-check: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enterprise_partners WHERE status = 'active' AND partner_category IN ('reseller','integration','white_label') AND dpa_signed_at IS NULL` must return zero. |
| **Cadence** | Annual — first collection: January following the year of first partner agreement execution (M10 or later) |
| **Retention** | 7 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/partners/PART-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |

#### PART-E-002 — Annual Partner Revenue Share Payment Governance Record

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | PART-E-002 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC9.2 (vendor financial controls — demonstrates structured governance of third-party commercial obligations) / CC4.1 (control activities over financial commitments — `privacy_floor_check_passed: true` Zod literal gate is a documented control activity) |
| **Description** | Annual export of all `enterprise.partner_revenue_share_paid` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events for the calendar year. Every row must carry `privacy_floor_check_passed: true` — the `emit-audit-event` Worker enforces this at chain-append time (HTTP 422 `PART_PAY_FLOOR_CHECK` if `false` or absent; payment is operationally blocked). The artefact provides auditors with evidence that FORM's quarterly privacy floor attestation is not merely procedural but is enforced at the infrastructure layer: no payment can be recorded in the DEC-030 chain without a prior compliance-officer attestation. |
| **Collection method** | Annual. Query: `SELECT id, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, created_at, payload->>'partner_id' AS partner_id, payload->>'period_start' AS period_start, payload->>'period_end' AS period_end, payload->>'attributed_arr_usd' AS attributed_arr_usd, payload->>'share_amount_usd' AS share_amount_usd, payload->>'payment_method' AS payment_method, payload->>'privacy_floor_check_passed' AS floor_check FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'enterprise.partner_revenue_share_paid' AND created_at >= '<year_start>' AND created_at < '<year_end>' ORDER BY created_at`. Verify: `floor_check = 'true'` for all rows. |
| **Cadence** | Annual — first collection: January following the year of first revenue share payment |
| **Retention** | 7 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/partners/PART-E-002_<YYYY>.csv` |

#### PART-E-003 — Quarterly Partner Offboarding and PART-CHAIN-01 Record

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | PART-E-003 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC9.2 (vendor offboarding — demonstrates formal partner termination governance) / CC7.4 (incident response activated on partner privacy breach — PART-CHAIN-01 predecessor requirement proves R-22 was initiated before offboarding event was chain-recorded) |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of all `enterprise.partner_offboarded` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events. For any row with `termination_reason = 'privacy_floor_breach'` or `'no_go_criteria_violation'`, the PART-CHAIN-01 invariant guarantees a preceding `privacy.floor_breach_detected` event exists in the HMAC chain for the affected `tenant_id` within the 12 months prior — the Worker enforces this at chain-append (HTTP 422 `PART_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on absence). The artefact provides auditors with evidence that partner privacy floor breaches were not silently terminated but were accompanied by a chain-level incident record. **Zero-event quarters** are filed as affirmative attestations confirming no partner was offboarded during the quarter — not an omission. |
| **Collection method** | Quarterly (Q1: Jan–Mar, Q2: Apr–Jun, Q3: Jul–Sep, Q4: Oct–Dec). Query: `SELECT id, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, created_at, payload->>'partner_id' AS partner_id, payload->>'termination_reason' AS reason, payload->>'outstanding_share_usd' AS outstanding_share, payload->>'dispute_open' AS dispute_open, payload->>'privacy_incident_ref' AS incident_ref FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'enterprise.partner_offboarded' AND created_at >= '<quarter_start>' AND created_at < '<quarter_end>' ORDER BY created_at`. For any row with `reason IN ('privacy_floor_breach','no_go_criteria_violation')`, verify `incident_ref IS NOT NULL` and that a `privacy.floor_breach_detected` event exists for the same `tenant_id` in the prior 12 months. Zero-event case: file attestation JSON per §89.3 zero-event template. |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly from M10 (or from first partner agreement execution, whichever is later) |
| **Retention** | 7 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/partners/PART-E-003_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (or `.json` for zero-event attestation) |

---

### §89.3 Zero-Event Attestation Template

For quarters where no partner offboarding occurred:

```json
{
  "artefact": "PART-E-003",
  "quarter": "<YYYY-QN>",
  "query_run_at": "<ISO-8601>",
  "offboarded_count": 0,
  "attestation": "PART-CHAIN-01 invariant operational. No partner offboarding events in this quarter. Zero-event filing is affirmative evidence of partner governance continuity, not an omission."
}
```

---

### §89.4 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping

| Criterion | Control | Evidence added by §89 |
|---|---|---|
| **CC9.2** | Partner agreements executed and DPA-gated before any data or commissions flow; `enterprise.partner_agreement_signed` DEC-030 `dpa_signed: true` enforced at Worker layer for Reseller/Integration/White-Label categories | PART-E-001 (annual agreement execution record); PART-E-002 (annual payment governance); PART-E-003 (quarterly offboarding — formal termination documented) |
| **CC4.1** | `privacy_floor_check_passed: true` Zod literal gate on `enterprise.partner_revenue_share_paid` is a documented control activity over financial obligations to third parties; HTTP 422 `PART_PAY_FLOOR_CHECK` makes it an enforcement control, not a policy commitment | PART-E-002 (every row carries `privacy_floor_check_passed: true` — confirms gate operated throughout the year) |
| **CC7.4** | PART-CHAIN-01 invariant ensures that a privacy floor breach incident (`privacy.floor_breach_detected`) was initiated before any `enterprise.partner_offboarded` with `termination_reason = 'privacy_floor_breach'` is chain-recorded; connects incident response to partner offboarding governance | PART-E-003 (cross-check for breach-related terminations: `privacy_incident_ref IS NOT NULL` + predecessor event verification) |

---

|---|---|---|
| DATA_MODEL §38.8 item 3: "Add partner privacy floor section to Partner Agreement template (`docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §38.6.2`): three non-waivable clauses; PART-CHAIN-01 escalation path" | `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §38.8` (P0 — before first partner agreement) | **🟢 Closed — `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md` Addendum 5 (v1.0, 2026-06-18)** |

| # | Task | Owner | Priority | Milestone | Status |

| 1 | Confirm PART-E-001/002/003 paths (`compliance/evidence/partners/`) are added to §80.3 R2 folder structure and `form-api NO ACCESS` note appended. | compliance-officer | **P1** | M10 (before first partner agreement) | [ ] |
| 2 | After first partner agreement executed: run PART-E-001 query; verify `dpa_signed: true` for all Reseller/Integration/White-Label partners; file at `compliance/evidence/partners/PART-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`. | compliance-officer | **P1** | January following first partner agreement year | [ ] |
| 3 | After first quarterly revenue share payment: run PART-E-002 query; verify `floor_check = 'true'` for all rows; file at `compliance/evidence/partners/PART-E-002_<YYYY>.csv`. | compliance-officer | **P1** | January following first payment year | [ ] |

Closes `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §38.8` checklist item 3 (P0 — partner privacy floor clauses in MSA_TEMPLATE). Formally registers three SOC 2 artefacts already listed in §79.4 and §80.4: **PART-E-001** (CC9.2 — annual `enterprise.partner_agreement_signed` export; `dpa_signed: true` for Reseller/Integration/White-Label; zero-event affirmative attestation; `compliance/evidence/partners/PART-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`; 7yr), **PART-E-002** (CC9.2/CC4.1 — annual `enterprise.partner_revenue_share_paid` export; `privacy_floor_check_passed: true` in every row; HTTP 422 `PART_PAY_FLOOR_CHECK` gate is CC4.1 enforcement control; `PART-E-002_<YYYY>.csv`; 7yr), **PART-E-003** (CC9.2/CC7.4 — quarterly `enterprise.partner_offboarded` export; PART-CHAIN-01 predecessor verification for breach-related terminations; zero-event quarters filed as affirmative attestation; `PART-E-003_<YYYY-QN>.csv`; 7yr). §89.3 zero-event attestation JSON template for PART-E-003. §89.4 three criterion rows (CC9.2 × 3 artefacts, CC4.1 × PART-E-002, CC7.4 × PART-E-003). §89.5 closes two cross-reference obligations: DATA_MODEL §38.8 item 3 (🟢) and MSA_TEMPLATE Addendum 5 §5.6 (🟢). §89.6 four-item P1 implementation checklist (M10 / January / January / Q1 2027). Cross-references: `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md Addendum 5` (v1.0, 2026-06-18 — partner agreement legal template with §5.2 non-waivable clauses and §5.7 DEC-030 event reference); `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise Partner Events` (canonical Zod schemas and PART-CHAIN-01 specification — primary source of truth); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §38` (enterprise_partners DDL, PART-CHAIN-01, four DEC-030 events); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §38.6` (partner governance — Bronze/Silver/Gold tiers, revenue share models, §38.6.2 privacy floor clauses); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-22` (Privacy Floor Breach protocol — PART-CHAIN-01 predecessor); §79.4 (master evidence table — PART-E-001/002/003 rows); §80.4 (Vanta mirror list — PART-E-001/002/003 listed). ## §90 Evidence Artefact Cross-Reference Patch: COST_MODEL §40 — Enterprise Customer Adoption Economics (ADO-E-001 / ADO-E-002 / ADO-E-003 — CC4.1 / CC2.2 / CC7.3 / A1.1 / CC4.2)

### §90.1 Purpose

`docs/COST_MODEL.md §40` (v2.6, 2026-06-18) defines the Enterprise Customer Adoption Economics & Seat Utilization Health Model. Three SOC 2 evidence artefacts were specified in §40.9 with an explicit cross-reference obligation to register them in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` at P1/M11: **ADO-E-001** (CC4.1/A1.1 — quarterly fleet adoption snapshot export), **ADO-E-002** (CC2.2/CC4.1 — quarterly QBR completion chain export), **ADO-E-003** (CC7.3/CC4.2 — quarterly adoption health downgrade response evidence). This section closes that obligation: each artefact is registered with its collection method, SQL query, cadence, retention, storage path, and auditor narrative.

**Privacy floor (all three artefacts):** All data is aggregated at the tenant (`tenant_id`) level. No individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health values, coaching session content, body composition data, or GDPR Art. 9 special category data appears in any row. `coaching_engaged_seats` is suppressed at the API layer when the count is fewer than 5 (k-anonymity floor, COST_MODEL §40.7). `tenant_id` is a FORM-internal UUID never exposed in marketing or external reports. `form_api` is REVOKED from `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` at RLS level (migration 0078).

---

### §90.2 Evidence Artefacts

**Primary definitions:** `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.9` (artefact IDs, criteria mapping, cadence, and retention — source of truth). This section registers collection protocol, SQL queries, and auditor narratives only.

#### ADO-E-001 — Quarterly Fleet Adoption Snapshot Export

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | ADO-E-001 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC4.1 (performance evaluations of controls — monthly adoption snapshots demonstrate FORM monitors enterprise service performance against defined thresholds) / A1.1 (availability threat monitoring — seat utilisation health degradation is a leading indicator of churn-driven deprovisioning; fleet-level monitoring detects systemic onboarding failures before they affect contracted availability) |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of the `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` table fleet health summary covering the most recent observation quarter. Includes count and percentage of active tenants at each WAU health band (Green / Amber / Red), average WAU rate per band, and total contracted ARR at each band. The `wau_health_band GENERATED ALWAYS AS STORED` column confirms band assignment is computed deterministically from `wau_count / contracted_seats` at INSERT time, not manually applied. Proves FORM collects, stores, and reviews structured adoption health data at a defined monthly cadence — the quarterly export is the evidence-collection event, not the observation interval. |
| **Collection method** | Quarterly (covering the preceding three calendar months). **Q1 (fleet health summary):** `SELECT wau_health_band, COUNT(*) AS tenant_count, ROUND(AVG(wau_rate_pct), 1) AS avg_wau_rate_pct, SUM(contracted_seats) AS fleet_seats FROM enterprise_adoption_snapshots WHERE snapshot_month = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') GROUP BY wau_health_band ORDER BY wau_health_band`. **Q3 (board-level ARR distribution):** `SELECT eas.wau_health_band, COUNT(*) AS tenants, SUM(ec.acv_usd) AS arr_at_band FROM enterprise_adoption_snapshots eas JOIN enterprise_contracts ec ON eas.tenant_id = ec.tenant_id WHERE eas.snapshot_month = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND ec.status = 'active' GROUP BY eas.wau_health_band ORDER BY eas.wau_health_band`. Compliance-officer exports both query outputs as a single CSV; appends one-paragraph attestation confirming k-anonymity gate was operational (no `coaching_engaged_seats < 5` row is visible in any API-exposed view); files to R2. |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly from M11 (SOC 2 observation period start) |
| **Retention** | 3 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/adoption/ADO-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |

**CC4.1 auditor narrative:** CC4.1 requires FORM to evaluate and communicate the results of performance evaluations of its controls. FORM monitors enterprise customer adoption health monthly: the `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` table captures WAU rate and health band for every active tenant at the first of each month. ADO-E-001 is the quarterly materialisation of this monitoring: it shows the distribution of Green / Amber / Red accounts across the fleet, the ARR at each band (quantifying the financial consequence of any performance gap), and the trend across the quarters of the observation year. The `wau_health_band GENERATED ALWAYS AS STORED` column ensures the evaluation is not subject to manual override — the band is a database invariant, not a field that a CSM or admin can set directly.

**A1.1 auditor narrative:** A1.1 requires FORM to monitor system components for potential impacts to availability commitments. ADO-E-001 demonstrates that FORM monitors a leading availability risk: seat utilisation health. Red-band accounts (WAU < 20%) have an estimated ~60% churn probability within the renewal window (COST_MODEL §40.3.1). Monitoring utilisation health at the fleet level allows FORM to detect systemic onboarding failures before they escalate to contracted-availability risk at the account level. The ARR-at-band distribution gives the compliance-officer a quantified view of the ARR at risk from low utilisation in each quarter.

---

#### ADO-E-002 — Quarterly QBR Completion Chain Export

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | ADO-E-002 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC2.2 (external communication commitments — demonstrates FORM communicated aggregate product performance to enterprise tenants on a documented schedule; `privacy_floor_verified: true` chain invariant confirms no prohibited individual-level data was shared) / CC4.1 (performance evaluations of controls — QBR is the formal documented performance review between FORM and each enterprise tenant; the DEC-030 chain record proves the cadence was followed and that the privacy floor was technically enforced at each QBR event) |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of all `enterprise.qbr_completed` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events filed during the quarter. Every row must carry `privacy_floor_verified: true` — the `emit-audit-event` Worker enforces this at chain-append time (HTTP 422 if `privacy_floor_verified` is absent or `false`; the QBR event cannot be recorded without the CSM's explicit attestation checkbox confirmation). This converts the QBR communication record from a procedural note into a technology-enforced audit event: an auditor can verify that every active Growth and Enterprise tenant received a QBR within the contracted cadence without relying solely on CRM records or calendar entries. **Zero-event quarters post-M10** are anomalous (active Growth/Enterprise tenants must receive quarterly QBRs) and require compliance-officer investigation before the quarter closes; file a note if the zero-event condition is explainable (e.g., no active tenants yet in first observation quarter). |
| **Collection method** | Quarterly. Query: `SELECT id, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, created_at, payload->>'tenant_id' AS tenant_id, payload->>'qbr_date' AS qbr_date, payload->>'tier' AS tier, payload->>'contracted_seats' AS contracted_seats, payload->>'wau_rate_pct_at_qbr' AS wau_rate_pct, payload->>'wau_health_band_at_qbr' AS health_band, payload->>'renewal_date' AS renewal_date, payload->>'expansion_discussed' AS expansion_discussed, payload->>'privacy_floor_verified' AS floor_verified FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'enterprise.qbr_completed' AND created_at >= '<quarter_start>' AND created_at < '<quarter_end>' ORDER BY created_at`. Assert: `floor_verified = 'true'` for every row (zero-tolerance — any `false` is a chain invariant violation requiring immediate compliance-officer review). Cross-check: identify any active Growth/Enterprise tenant with no `enterprise.qbr_completed` event in the quarter; flag Starter-tier accounts if last QBR was more than 180 days ago (semi-annual cadence). |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly from M11 (SOC 2 observation period start) |
| **Retention** | 3 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/adoption/ADO-E-002_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |

**CC2.2 auditor narrative:** CC2.2 requires FORM to communicate information to external parties about the system's availability, security, and processing integrity. For enterprise customers, QBRs are the primary structured communication channel for aggregate product performance. `enterprise.qbr_completed` with `privacy_floor_verified: true` is an immutable chain record that FORM fulfilled this communication obligation on a specific date, for a specific tenant, without violating the privacy floor. The chain record is tamper-evident (each event's `chain_hash` commits to its predecessor's hash) — an auditor can verify the record was not backdated or manually inserted. ADO-E-002 provides the quarterly fleet-level view: every active Growth and Enterprise tenant in the chain export received a QBR in the observation quarter.

**CC4.1 auditor narrative:** The QBR is FORM's formal performance review mechanism with enterprise customers. ADO-E-002 demonstrates that FORM evaluates and communicates enterprise system performance on a contracted cadence. The `wau_rate_pct_at_qbr` and `wau_health_band_at_qbr` fields in the chain event confirm that each QBR was data-informed (the CSM presented the tenant's actual WAU rate, derived from `enterprise_adoption_snapshots`). Together with ADO-E-001, the two artefacts prove a closed loop: FORM monitors adoption health monthly (ADO-E-001) and formally reviews it with each tenant quarterly (ADO-E-002).

---

#### ADO-E-003 — Quarterly Adoption Health Downgrade Response Evidence

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | ADO-E-003 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC7.3 (response to identified anomalies — adoption health degradation from Green → Amber or Amber → Red is an operational anomaly detected by the monthly snapshot process; ADO-CHAIN-01 requires a CSM follow-up task within 2 business days of the `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` DEC-030 event) / CC4.2 (evaluation of identified deficiencies — Red-band accounts are treated as operating deficiencies in the CS system; the artefact confirms FORM evaluates and responds to them within documented SLAs; `system.csm_followup_overdue` advisory events are counted as a detection-and-evaluation control for missed responses) |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of all `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events for the quarter (severity HIGH, 3yr retention), paired with confirmation that the ADO-CHAIN-01 CSM follow-up obligation was met within 10 business days. The `system.csm_followup_overdue` advisory event (LOW, 1yr) is emitted automatically if no `enterprise.qbr_completed` or CSM check-in task is recorded within 10 business days of a downgrade — the cross-check query below must return zero for the artefact to be filed without annotation. **Zero-event quarters** (no adoption health degradations observed) are filed as affirmative attestations (JSON per §90.3) confirming the monitoring pipeline ran and no degradations were detected. |
| **Collection method** | Quarterly. **Primary query:** `SELECT id, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, created_at, payload->>'tenant_id' AS tenant_id, payload->>'prior_band' AS prior_band, payload->>'current_band' AS current_band, payload->>'wau_rate_pct' AS wau_rate_pct, payload->>'contracted_seats' AS contracted_seats, payload->>'acv_usd' AS acv_usd FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded' AND created_at >= '<quarter_start>' AND created_at < '<quarter_end>' ORDER BY created_at`. **ADO-CHAIN-01 overdue advisory check:** `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'system.csm_followup_overdue' AND created_at >= '<quarter_start>' AND created_at < '<quarter_end>'` — must return 0; non-zero count requires compliance-officer review of each overdue case and a written explanation before filing. Zero-event case: file attestation JSON per §90.3; include a cross-reference to the most recent `enterprise.adoption_snapshot_filed` DEC-030 event to confirm the monitoring pipeline was running during the quarter. |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly from M11 (SOC 2 observation period start) |
| **Retention** | 3 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/adoption/ADO-E-003_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (or `.json` for zero-event attestation) |

**CC7.3 auditor narrative:** CC7.3 requires FORM to respond to identified security and operational events in a timely manner. Adoption health degradation is an operational event: when a tenant's WAU rate drops below the Green threshold, the `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` HIGH-severity DEC-030 event is chain-recorded and the ADO-CHAIN-01 monitoring invariant requires CSM follow-up within 2 business days. The advisory `system.csm_followup_overdue` event fires automatically if no follow-up is recorded within 10 business days — it serves as a detection control for missed responses. ADO-E-003 provides quarterly evidence that: (a) FORM detected adoption health anomalies via the monthly snapshot process, (b) each detection triggered the ADO-CHAIN-01 response chain, and (c) no responses were missed (zero overdue advisories). Zero-event quarters confirm the monitoring pipeline operated and no anomalies were detected — silence is evidence of health, not absence of monitoring.

**CC4.2 auditor narrative:** CC4.2 requires FORM to evaluate and communicate identified control deficiencies in a timely manner. Red-band accounts (WAU < 20%) are treated as operating deficiencies in the CS system: they are flagged in the CSM priority queue (COST_MODEL §40.6), require an intervention within the cadence defined in §40.6, and are tracked until WAU health improves or the account churns. ADO-E-003 demonstrates that FORM evaluated each deficiency (the downgrade event is chain-recorded), communicated it to the responsible CSM (ADO-CHAIN-01 task), and documented the response. The overdue advisory cross-check confirms no deficiency was identified but left without a documented response within the observation quarter.

---

### §90.3 Zero-Event Attestation Template

For quarters where no adoption health degradation events occurred:

```json
{
  "artefact": "ADO-E-003",
  "quarter": "<YYYY-QN>",
  "query_run_at": "<ISO-8601>",
  "downgraded_count": 0,
  "csm_overdue_count": 0,
  "monitoring_pipeline_reference": "<dec030_event_id of most recent enterprise.adoption_snapshot_filed>",
  "attestation": "ADO-CHAIN-01 invariant not activated this quarter. No adoption health downgrade events were recorded. Monthly enterprise_adoption_snapshots rows confirm the monitoring pipeline ran throughout the quarter. Zero-event filing is affirmative evidence of adoption health stability, not an omission."
}
```

---

### §90.4 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping

| Criterion | Control | Evidence added by §90 |
|---|---|---|
| **CC4.1** | FORM monitors enterprise customer adoption health monthly via `enterprise_adoption_snapshots`; WAU health band thresholds (Green ≥ 40% / Amber 20–39% / Red < 20%) are computed deterministically at INSERT time as a GENERATED ALWAYS AS STORED column; the QBR cadence is a formal performance review against those thresholds documented in the DEC-030 chain | ADO-E-001 (quarterly fleet snapshot export — confirms monitoring ran and health bands were computed at defined cadence); ADO-E-002 (quarterly QBR completion export — confirms performance reviews were conducted with `privacy_floor_verified: true` chain invariant) |
| **CC2.2** | FORM communicates aggregate product performance to enterprise tenants on a contracted QBR schedule; `enterprise.qbr_completed` with `privacy_floor_verified: true` chain invariant is a technology-enforced record that the communication occurred and excluded prohibited individual-level data; HTTP 422 at chain-append if invariant absent | ADO-E-002 (every row carries `privacy_floor_verified: true` — converts CC2.2 communication obligation from procedural to technology control; tenant-level export confirms cadence coverage across the fleet) |
| **CC7.3** | FORM detects and responds to adoption health anomalies within documented timelines; `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` HIGH DEC-030 event triggers ADO-CHAIN-01 CSM follow-up obligation (2 business days); advisory `system.csm_followup_overdue` (LOW, 1yr) fires automatically if response is absent after 10 business days — the advisory is a detection control for missed responses | ADO-E-003 (quarterly downgrade event export + `system.csm_followup_overdue` zero-count cross-check — confirms anomalies were detected, response obligation was activated, and no responses were overdue in the observation quarter) |
| **A1.1** | Fleet-level seat utilisation monitoring detects systemic onboarding failures before they escalate to contracted-availability risk; Red-band fleet (~60% churn probability within renewal window per COST_MODEL §40.3.1) is a leading availability risk indicator; fleet ARR-at-band distribution quantifies the financial consequence of monitoring gaps | ADO-E-001 (ARR-at-band Q3 query output shows FORM tracks availability risk precursors at fleet level and can quantify ARR at risk from low utilisation in each observation quarter) |
| **CC4.2** | Red-band accounts are evaluated as operating deficiencies in the CS system; FORM's CSM intervention cadence (COST_MODEL §40.6) and ADO-CHAIN-01 advisory event confirm deficiencies are identified, evaluated, communicated to responsible owners, and documented within defined timelines | ADO-E-003 (downgrade event + overdue advisory cross-check pairing proves each deficiency received a documented evaluation and response; zero-overdue confirms no deficiency was identified but left unresponded) |

## §91 · Evidence Artefact Cross-Reference Patch — SSO_SCIM §34 (SCIM Bulk Deprovision Guard · DEC-066 · OQ-R24-01)

> Closes the cross-reference obligation from `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §34.9` (GUARD-E-001 registered here) and `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §34.11` checklist item 5 (`[x] Done — docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.19 (2026-06-19)`). Formally registers one SOC 2 evidence artefact introduced in SSO_SCIM §34 and establishes collection protocol and auditor narrative. Privacy floor: all five DEC-030 chain events carry only `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID) and aggregate integers — no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, or GDPR Art. 9 health data. `override_issued_by_email_hash` and `updated_by_email_hash` fields are SHA-256(lowercase(email)) — 64 hex chars; plaintext never stored. `form_api` REVOKED from `tenant_sso_configs.bdg_*` columns (migration 0079).

### §91.1 Evidence Artefact Registration

**GUARD-E-001** — SCIM Bulk Deprovision Guard quarterly event export.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Artefact ID | GUARD-E-001 |
| SOC 2 criteria | CC6.3 · A1.2 · CC7.2 · CC9.2 |
| Retention | 7 years (HIGH-severity events); 1 year (`system.scim_guard_repeated_trigger` advisory) |
| Cadence | Quarterly — first filing at M14 (first full quarter after M13 enterprise GA) |
| Storage path | `compliance/evidence/scim-guard/GUARD-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| Zero-event attestation | Required if `blocked_count = 0` AND `override_issued_count = 0` in the quarter — file as JSON with `monitoring_pipeline_reference` (most recent `scim.bulk_deprovision_threshold_updated` or `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` event_id) |
| Vanta mirror | GUARD-E-001 (§80.4 — added in this version) |

### §91.2 Collection SQL (GUARD-E-001)

```sql
-- GUARD-E-001: SCIM Bulk Deprovision Guard quarterly export
-- Run as: form_system (compliance_reviewer SELECT)
-- Replace :quarter_start and :quarter_end with quarter boundaries (UTC)
SELECT
  event_id,
  event_type,
  payload->>'tenant_id'            AS tenant_id,
  payload->>'deprovisioned_count'  AS deprovisioned_count,
  payload->>'contracted_seats'     AS contracted_seats,
  payload->>'threshold_pct'        AS threshold_pct,
  payload->>'window_key'           AS window_key,
  payload->>'scim_source'          AS scim_source,
  payload->>'override_token_hash'  AS override_token_hash,
  payload->>'auto_revoked'         AS auto_revoked,
  payload->>'old_threshold_pct'    AS old_threshold_pct,
  payload->>'new_threshold_pct'    AS new_threshold_pct,
  payload->>'trigger_count'        AS trigger_count,
  hmac_chain_hash,
  created_at
FROM audit_log
WHERE event_type IN (
  'scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked',
  'scim.bulk_deprovision_override_issued',
  'scim.bulk_deprovision_override_used',
  'scim.bulk_deprovision_threshold_updated',
  'system.scim_guard_repeated_trigger'
)
  AND created_at >= :quarter_start
  AND created_at <  :quarter_end
ORDER BY created_at;

-- Zero-event check: if this returns 0 rows, file attestation JSON instead of CSV
-- Attestation must include: artefact, quarter, query_run_at, blocked_count (0),
-- override_issued_count (0), monitoring_pipeline_reference (most recent guard event_id)
```

**§98.2 Extension — Contracted-seats traceability assertion (DEC-069 CC6.3, run alongside primary export):**

```sql
-- DEC-069 §35.7 CC6.3 traceability assertion
-- For every scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked event in the quarter,
-- verify payload.contracted_seats matches enterprise_contracts at block time.
-- Expected: 0 rows (all contracted_seats values match billing record)
-- Run as: form_system (compliance_reviewer SELECT on both audit_log_events and enterprise_contracts)
SELECT
  ale.event_id,
  ale.created_at,
  ale.payload->>'tenant_id'               AS tenant_id,
  (ale.payload->>'contracted_seats')::int  AS payload_contracted_seats,
  ec.contracted_seats                      AS billing_contracted_seats
FROM audit_log_events ale
JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT contracted_seats
  FROM enterprise_contracts
  WHERE tenant_id = (ale.payload->>'tenant_id')::uuid
    AND effective_from <= ale.created_at
  ORDER BY effective_from DESC
  LIMIT 1
) ec ON true
WHERE ale.event_type = 'scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked'
  AND ale.created_at >= :quarter_start
  AND ale.created_at <  :quarter_end
  AND (ale.payload->>'contracted_seats')::int <> ec.contracted_seats;
-- Zero rows = assertion passes. Non-zero rows = anomaly requiring compliance-officer review before filing.
-- Possible causes: (a) contract amendment mid 5-min rolling window, (b) billing-event-relay KV miss
-- lasting > 3600s TTL safety-net, or (c) enterprise_contracts effective_from history gap.
```

**§98.3 Extension — Stale-advisory cross-check (DEC-069 CC7.2, run alongside primary export):**

```sql
-- CC7.2 stale-advisory count: billing-event-relay KV delete failures during the quarter
-- Expected: 0 rows for a healthy billing event relay; non-zero requires compliance-officer review
-- Run as: form_audit (read-only)
SELECT
  payload->>'tenant_id'  AS tenant_id,
  COUNT(*)               AS stale_events,
  MIN(created_at)        AS first_stale_at,
  MAX(created_at)        AS last_stale_at
FROM audit_log_events
WHERE event_type = 'system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale'
  AND created_at >= :quarter_start
  AND created_at <  :quarter_end
GROUP BY payload->>'tenant_id'
ORDER BY stale_events DESC;
-- Zero rows = advisory path operational, no missed invalidations.
-- Non-zero: review whether any scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked event fired during the ≤ 3600s stale
-- window; if blocked_event.contracted_seats < actual contracted_seats, note in GUARD-E-001 filing.
```

### §91.3 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping

| Criterion | Narrative | Evidence from GUARD-E-001 |
|---|---|---|
| **CC6.3** | Logical access removal — FORM implements a preventive control that blocks unauthorised bulk deprovisioning of enterprise seats via SCIM push. The guard operates at the Worker endpoint level, before any write reaches Postgres. `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` events confirm the control fired; `scim.bulk_deprovision_override_issued` + `scim.bulk_deprovision_override_used` events confirm the two-person exception path is auditable. | Export of all five event types for the quarter; confirm `override_used_count ≤ override_issued_count` (no override used without prior issuance); confirm `auto_revoked: true` on all `override_used` events |
| **A1.2** | Environmental threat detection — the guard detects and blocks an H3-class threat (IdP-side or FORM-side SCIM processing error causing mass deprovisioning) before it affects availability. Complementary to AL-SCIM-MASS-01 (reactive); the guard is the preventive layer. | `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` export; confirm threshold values are within configured range (5–100); confirm `window_key` pattern (5-min epoch buckets) |
| **CC7.2** | Proactive monitoring — `system.scim_guard_repeated_trigger` (GUARD-CHAIN-01) detects recurring IdP misconfiguration before it escalates to a P0 incident, providing an early warning signal for CSM follow-up. | `system.scim_guard_repeated_trigger` export; confirm PagerDuty P2 `form-customer-success` routing was operational (cross-reference PagerDuty logs for any emitted `trigger_count` events) |
| **CC9.2** | Third-party risk (IdP as vendor) — the override protocol enforces a two-person authorisation gate on any intentional bulk deprovisioning event via SCIM: tenant_owner submits consent; CSM countersigns via PAM §24 session. Both steps produce DEC-030 chain records. Auto-revocation after first use prevents the override from being reused by a subsequent IdP push. | `scim.bulk_deprovision_override_issued` + `scim.bulk_deprovision_override_used` pair; confirm `pam_session_id` present on all issued events; confirm `tenant_owner_request_event_id` populated (tenant_owner consent on file); confirm `auto_revoked: true` on all used events |

### §91.4 Cross-Reference Obligations Closed

| Obligation source | Obligation text | Status |
|---|---|---|
