## §97 · Cross-Reference Patch — SSO_SCIM §23 (CAEP/SSF Real-Time Session Revocation Architecture · CC6-E-CAEP-001/002/003/004 · CC6.3 / CC6.6 / CC7.2 / CC7.3) + §94 Stale Reference Correction

> Closes two outstanding gaps created before and after §94 was authored.
>
> **Gap 1 — SSO_SCIM §23 evidence artefacts never registered here.** `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §23` (v1.5, 2026-06-01) defined four evidence artefacts (CC6-E-CAEP-001 through CC6-E-CAEP-004) under §23.10 and listed them in the `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §CAEP / SSF` note. No corresponding cross-reference patch was ever added to `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4`. §94 (v3.19.0, 2026-06-19) registered CAEP-E-001 for the §36 DEC-072 controls (cert-rotation re-registration + RISC hijacking cache eviction) but did not cover the original §23 artefacts — which address the core CAEP/SSF real-time session revocation pipeline. §97 closes this gap.
>
> **Gap 2 — §94 stale cross-references.** §94 (v3.19.0, 2026-06-19) referenced `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §Addendum 3` and `docs/ENTERPRISE_ONBOARDING.md §2.4` for the OQ-SSO-23.3 contractual SLA layer. These references became stale on 2026-06-20 when the v6.67.0 commit authored `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §Addendum 6 — CAEP/SSF Real-Time Session Control SLA Addendum v1.0` (the §36.6 checklist note explicitly records that `§Addendum 3` was a drafting error; CAEP SLA became Addendum 6) and `docs/ENTERPRISE_ONBOARDING.md §2.3` (the CAEP/SSF IdP prerequisite verification section; §2.4 was a placeholder that did not exist at §94 authoring time). The stale references were patched in-place in §94 body text and version footer in this v3.21.2 patch; §97.4 records the correction authoritatively.
>
> Privacy floor: all CC6-E-CAEP-001/002/003/004 artefacts contain only `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID), `event_type` (controlled vocabulary), `action_taken` (controlled vocabulary), aggregate counts, and `SHA-256`-hashed identifiers (`set_jti`, `idp_subject`, `raw_set`). No individual employee name, email, coaching content, body composition, health values, or GDPR Art. 9 special category data appears in any export. `form_api` has zero-row RLS on `caep_events` (SSO_SCIM §23.7 schema note). `caep_webhook_secret` is AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest and is never included in any evidence artefact.

---

### §97.1 Source Document Context

`docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §23` (v1.5, 2026-06-01 · no formal DEC) defines FORM's Continuous Access Evaluation Protocol (CAEP) and Shared Signals Framework (SSF) real-time session revocation architecture. Key facts relevant to §97:

| Fact | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Architecture** | FORM registers as SSF Receiver at each IdP; push delivery (IdP POSTs RFC 8417 Security Event Tokens to `POST /enterprise/v1/sso/caep-receiver/{tenant_id}` Cloudflare Worker) |
| **Processing pipeline** | 10-step: HMAC webhook secret validation → JWKS fetch/cache in SSO_KV (1h TTL) → SET JWT signature verification → replay check via `set_jti UNIQUE` constraint → user resolution → action dispatch → `caep_events` INSERT → DEC-030 emit → 202 Accepted |
| **Session revocation latency** | < 30 seconds from IdP security event to FORM session invalidation via §22 KV layer (vs. ≤ 15 min JWT TTL baseline before §23) — upgraded to "near-real-time (< 60 s in normal operation)" for PUSH-capable IdPs by DEC-072 (MSA Addendum 6) |
| **Eight event-type-to-action mappings** | `session-revoked` (revoke specific session), `credential-change` (revoke all user sessions), `token-claims-change` (set `force_reauth` KV flag, TTL 24h), `account-disabled` (revoke + suspend), `account-enabled` (clear suspension), `account-purged` (revoke + GDPR deletion workflow), `device-compliance-change` (conditional revoke), Google RISC `hijacking` (immediate revocation + PagerDuty P0) |
| **SOC 2 controls** | CC6.3 (real-time access revocation — < 30 s vs. 15 min baseline), CC6.6 (credential-change + token-claims-change mapped to session invalidation), CC7.2 (five alerting rules on stream health), CC7.3 (P0/P1 PagerDuty routing + GDPR workflow on `account-purged`) |
| **DEC-030 events** | Six HMAC-chained events: `sso.caep_event_received` (STANDARD/7yr), `sso.caep_session_revoked` (HIGH/7yr), `sso.caep_user_suspended` (HIGH/7yr), `sso.caep_user_purged` (CRITICAL/7yr), `sso.caep_stream_error` (HIGH/7yr), `sso.caep_stream_registered` (HIGH/7yr) |
| **Evidence artefacts in §23.10** | CC6-E-CAEP-001 through CC6-E-CAEP-004 — defined in SSO_SCIM but never registered in SOC2_READINESS §79.4 |

---

### §97.2 Evidence Artefact Registration — CC6-E-CAEP-001 through CC6-E-CAEP-004

#### CC6-E-CAEP-001 — `caep_events` Table Export

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | CC6-E-CAEP-001 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC6.3 — demonstrates that IdP security events are received and actioned against FORM sessions in real time |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of the `caep_events` table aggregated by `event_type` and `action_taken` for the observation period. Proves that SET events from enterprise IdPs reached FORM's CAEP receiver Worker, were parsed and validated, and triggered the correct downstream action (session revocation, suspension, force-reauth flag, GDPR deletion queue). The `processed_at` timestamp distribution documents the real-time processing latency claim (§23 target: < 30 s from IdP event to FORM action; MSA Addendum 6: < 60 s in normal operation for PUSH-capable IdPs). |
| **Collection SQL** | `SELECT event_type, action_taken, count(*), min(processed_at), max(processed_at) FROM caep_events WHERE processed_at BETWEEN :quarter_start AND :quarter_end GROUP BY event_type, action_taken ORDER BY count DESC` — run as `form_system` or `compliance_reviewer` (zero-row RLS for `form_api` enforced). |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly — first filing at M10 (after CAEP pipeline M4 deployment + M5 SAML cert-rotation integration). Zero-row quarters are affirmative: no IdP security events received is expected at low initial customer count; file zero-event attestation JSON per §97.3. |
| **Retention** | 3 years — artefact contains only `event_type` (controlled vocabulary) and `action_taken` (controlled vocabulary) aggregate counts; no individual user data. |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/caep/CC6-E-CAEP-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| **Vanta mirror** | Yes — CC6-E-CAEP-001 added to §80.4 mirror list in this version |
| **Zero-event attestation** | File JSON with fields: `event_type_count: 0`, `quarter`, `query_run_at`, `monitoring_pipeline_reference` (most recent `sso.caep_event_received` or `sso.caep_stream_registered` event ID from preceding quarter confirming the pipeline is deployed), `attestation` text confirming no SET events received and stream health confirmed via AL-CAEP-03 dead-man's switch. |

#### CC6-E-CAEP-002 — `audit_log` CAEP Event Chain Export

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | CC6-E-CAEP-002 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC6.3 / CC7.3 — HMAC-chained DEC-030 trail proving that all IdP security events produced tamper-evident audit records; referenced in `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §Addendum 6.4` as the primary SOC 2 mapping evidence for the CAEP SLA contractual commitment |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of all `sso.caep_*` HMAC-chained DEC-030 events from `audit_log_events` for the observation period. The HMAC chain (`chain_hash`, `prev_hash`, `sequence_number`) provides tamper-evident proof that CAEP event records were not backdated or retroactively inserted. This is the authoritative SOC 2 evidence export referenced in MSA Addendum 6.4 (`sso.caep_event_received` STANDARD/7yr + `sso.caep_session_revoked` HIGH/7yr — delta between these two timestamps is the FORM-side processing latency for the < 60 s SLA). |
| **Collection SQL** | `SELECT event_type, created_at, sequence_number, chain_hash, prev_hash, payload->>'tenant_id' AS tenant_id, payload->>'caep_stream_id' AS caep_stream_id FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type LIKE 'sso.caep_%' AND created_at >= :quarter_start AND created_at < :quarter_end ORDER BY created_at` — run as `form_system` or `compliance_reviewer`. |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly — first filing at M10. Zero-event quarters expected pre-CAEP pipeline deployment; file zero-event attestation. |
| **Retention** | 7 years — chain events carry HIGH/7yr or STANDARD/7yr retention per AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §CAEP/SSF |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/caep/CC6-E-CAEP-002_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| **Vanta mirror** | Yes — CC6-E-CAEP-002 added to §80.4 mirror list in this version |

#### CC6-E-CAEP-003 — CAEP Stream Registration Records (Sanitized)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | CC6-E-CAEP-003 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC6.3 — confirms that all active enterprise tenants have a CAEP stream in `active` or `error` status, demonstrating that real-time session revocation capability is configured across the fleet |
| **Description** | Point-in-time export of `tenant_sso_configs` CAEP columns for all enterprise tenants with `caep_status IN ('active', 'error')` as of the observation period end date. Sensitive fields (`caep_stream_id`, `caep_webhook_secret`) are redacted in the artefact; `caep_stream_id` may be included as a SHA-256 hash for traceability if required by the auditor. Demonstrates: (a) all enterprise tenants with OIDC/SAML SSO have CAEP configured; (b) tenants in `error` state are tracked for remediation (AL-CAEP-01 alerting). |
| **Collection SQL** | `SELECT tenant_id, idp_type, caep_status, caep_delivery_mode, caep_last_event_at, caep_error_count FROM tenant_sso_configs WHERE caep_status IN ('active', 'error') ORDER BY tenant_id` — `caep_stream_id` and `caep_webhook_secret` excluded. Run as `security_reviewer` or `compliance_reviewer`. |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly — first filing at M10. Zero-row result expected pre-enterprise customer acquisition; file zero-tenant attestation noting that no enterprise SSO tenants are yet provisioned. |
| **Retention** | 3 years — contains only FORM-internal `tenant_id` UUIDs and operational status enums; no personal data. |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/caep/CC6-E-CAEP-003_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| **Vanta mirror** | Yes — CC6-E-CAEP-003 added to §80.4 mirror list in this version |

#### CC6-E-CAEP-004 — CAEP Stream Health PagerDuty Incident History

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | CC6-E-CAEP-004 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | CC7.2 / CC7.3 — demonstrates that FORM's CAEP stream health monitoring (AL-CAEP-01 SET validation failure rate alert + AL-CAEP-03 dead-man's switch) detects and responds to stream anomalies within SLA |
| **Description** | Quarterly export of PagerDuty incidents for `AL-CAEP-01` (SET validation failure rate > 5% over 10 min — P1 `form-security`, possible JWKS poisoning) and `AL-CAEP-03` (dead-man's switch: no events on an active stream for 4 business hours — P2 `form-customer-success` + CSM notification) during the observation period. Each incident row includes `created_at`, `resolved_at`, `resolution_note`. Zero-incident quarters are affirmative: no stream health anomalies detected is expected and desirable. |
| **Collection method** | PagerDuty Incidents API export filtered by service `form-caep-stream` (or the equivalent `form-security` + `form-customer-success` service tags) and date range `:quarter_start` to `:quarter_end`. Filter `title LIKE 'AL-CAEP-01%' OR title LIKE 'AL-CAEP-03%'`. Export `id`, `created_at`, `resolved_at`, `urgency`, `title`, `resolution_note`. |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly — first filing at M10. Zero-incident quarters file as affirmative attestation JSON. |
| **Retention** | 3 years — PagerDuty incident metadata only; no individual user data or CAEP event payload content. |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/caep/CC6-E-CAEP-004_<YYYY-QN>.json` |
| **Vanta mirror** | Yes — CC6-E-CAEP-004 added to §80.4 mirror list in this version |

---

### §97.3 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping

| Criterion | Control description | CC6-E-CAEP evidence role |
|---|---|---|
| **CC6.3** | **Real-time session access revocation on IdP security events.** FORM's §23 CAEP pipeline closes the gap between SAML JWT TTL (≤ 15 min) and the actual moment an IdP security event occurs (account disabled, credential changed, session revoked by admin). The 10-step processing pipeline — HMAC webhook validation → SET signature verification → `set_jti` replay prevention → action dispatch → KV revocation write → DEC-030 emit → 202 Accepted — completes in < 30 s from IdP event receipt under normal conditions (MSA Addendum 6: < 60 s for PUSH-capable IdPs). The contractual SLA commitment is documented in `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §Addendum 6.2`; the technical implementation fulfilling that commitment is §23. Together, Addendum 6 (contractual) + §23 pipeline (technical) + CAEP-E-001 Part A cert-rotation re-registration (§94) form the complete CC6.3 access-continuity control stack. | CC6-E-CAEP-001 (event distribution confirms IdP events were received and actioned) + CC6-E-CAEP-002 (HMAC chain confirms records are tamper-evident and not backdated) + CC6-E-CAEP-003 (stream registration confirms all tenants configured). |
| **CC6.6** | **Logical access protection against external threats — credential change and token-claims revocation.** `credential-change` events (password reset, MFA credential removed at IdP) trigger immediate revocation of all active FORM sessions for the affected user via the §22 KV layer. `token-claims-change` events (group membership change, role change at IdP) set a `force_reauth:{tenant_id}:{user_id}` KV flag (TTL 24h), causing a 401 re-authentication prompt on the next API call. This ensures that IdP-side privilege changes propagate to FORM session state within < 30 s rather than at JWT expiry. | CC6-E-CAEP-001 `action_taken` distribution confirms `credential-change` → `revoke_all_sessions` and `token-claims-change` → `force_reauth` mappings are exercised; CC6-E-CAEP-002 HMAC chain shows `sso.caep_session_revoked` HIGH/7yr events paired with `sso.caep_event_received` STANDARD/7yr events for each processed `credential-change`. |
| **CC7.2** | **CAEP stream health monitoring — anomaly detection.** Five alerting rules defined in §23.9 monitor CAEP stream integrity: AL-CAEP-01 P1 (SET validation failure > 5%/10 min — possible JWKS poisoning attack), AL-CAEP-02 P0 (account-purged event received — GDPR deletion clock starts, immediate escalation), AL-CAEP-03 P2 (dead-man's switch: no events on active stream for 4 business hours — stream delivery failure or misconfiguration), AL-CAEP-04 P1 (Google RISC hijacking event — account compromise signal), AL-CAEP-05 P2 (rate limit exceeded: > 1,000 events/h per tenant). This ensures that CAEP stream failures are detected within the monitoring window rather than discovered at the next SAML JWT expiry. | CC6-E-CAEP-004 PagerDuty history for AL-CAEP-01 and AL-CAEP-03 is the CC7.2 evidence that the monitoring rules are operational and incidents are detected and resolved within SLA. |
| **CC7.3** | **Response to CAEP security events — session revocation SLA + GDPR deletion workflow.** On receipt of high-severity CAEP events (account-disabled, account-purged, Google RISC hijacking), FORM routes to PagerDuty P0/P1 for `form-security` + CSM notification. The `account-purged` event specifically triggers both immediate session revocation AND enqueues the GDPR Article 17(4)(c) deletion workflow — `sso.caep_user_purged` CRITICAL/7yr is emitted before the state mutation (ordering invariant §23.8) ensuring the deletion chain starts at the IdP-initiated event, not at FORM's processing time. MSA Addendum 6.4 references CC6-E-CAEP-002 as the primary audit evidence for the < 60 s processing SLA. | CC6-E-CAEP-002 HMAC chain provides the tamper-evident CC7.3 response evidence: `sso.caep_session_revoked` timestamp minus `sso.caep_event_received` timestamp = FORM-side processing latency. Auditor can verify no revocation events were backdated (HMAC `prev_hash` chain continuity). CC6-E-CAEP-004 provides the operational incident evidence for CC7.3 response SLA. |

---

### §97.4 §94 Stale Reference Patch Record

The following two cross-references in §94 became stale after §94 was authored on 2026-06-19:

| §94 location | Stale reference (v3.19.0) | Corrected reference (v3.21.2) | Root cause |
|---|---|---|---|
| §94 intro blockquote | `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §Addendum 3` | `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §Addendum 6` | §94 was authored on 2026-06-19. MSA v0.5 (2026-06-20, v6.67.0) created Addendum 6 as the correct CAEP SLA addendum; §36.6 checklist item 6 explicitly notes "the §36.6 reference to 'Addendum 3' was a drafting error — Addendum 3 is EU Data Residency Terms; CAEP SLA becomes Addendum 6." |
| §94 intro blockquote | `docs/ENTERPRISE_ONBOARDING.md §2.4` | `docs/ENTERPRISE_ONBOARDING.md §2.3` | ENTERPRISE_ONBOARDING v0.3 (2026-06-20, v6.67.0) added §2.3 as the CAEP/SSF IdP prerequisite verification section; §2.4 was a placeholder reference in §36.6 item 7 that did not exist as a section at §94 authoring time — §2.3 is the correct insertion point (only §2.1 and §2.2 existed previously). |

*v3.22.0 (2026-06-20): §98 Cross-Reference Patch — SSO_SCIM §35 (BDG `getGuardConfig()` Seat Source · DEC-069 · OQ-SSO-34.2 · CC6.3 / A1.2 / CC7.2). Supplements §91 (GUARD-E-001) with three additions: (1) CC6.3 audit traceability supplement — `contracted_seats` in `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` payload is sourced from `enterprise_contracts.contracted_seats` (immutable billing record, DEC-069), enabling the §35.7 three-step reconstruction protocol (enterprise_contracts row + preceding `scim.bulk_deprovision_threshold_updated` chain event + `ceil()` formula) without any live `COUNT(tenant_users)` query; the `payload.contracted_seats` shortcut field in the chain event allows direct auditor verification. (2) A1.2 availability protection supplement — Option A (KV caching with 3600s safety-net TTL + active event-driven invalidation on `billing.seats_expanded`/`billing.seats_reduced`) decouples guard evaluation from Supabase DB load; N-user bulk deprovision generates zero Supabase queries after KV warmup (vs. N per-request COUNTs under the rejected Option B), preventing a DB-saturation feedback loop in the exact scenario the guard is designed to contain. (3) CC7.2 new advisory monitoring signal — `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` (LOW/1yr) fires when `billing-event-relay` KV delete fails; non-paging, daily compliance review; self-clearing within 3600s TTL safety-net. §98.1 SSO_SCIM §35 context (four-row summary). §98.2 GUARD-E-001 CC6.3 traceability supplement: three-step reconstruction protocol from §35.7; `payload.contracted_seats` shortcut field; GUARD-E-001 collection SQL supplement adding `contracted_seats` assertion. §98.3 three-row SOC 2 criteria mapping supplement (CC6.3, A1.2, CC7.2). §98.4 two obligations closed: SSO_SCIM §35.9 item 4 (`system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA registration — 🟡 open, prerequisite for CC7.2 stale-advisory cross-check in GUARD-E-001); SSO_SCIM §35.9 item 5 (compliance/fieldwork/scim-bulk-deprovision-guard.md — 🟡 open, §98.2 §35.7 protocol registered here as interim SOC2_READINESS reference pending standalone fieldwork guide). §98.5 four-item implementation checklist: 1× P1/M13 (AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` registration), 1× P1/M13 (GUARD-E-001 stale-advisory SQL extension added to §91.2), 1× P1/M13 (fieldwork guide at compliance/fieldwork/scim-bulk-deprovision-guard.md), 1× P1/M14 (first GUARD-E-001 filing — include `contracted_seats` assertion per §98.2 and stale-advisory cross-check per §98.3). SOC 2 version bumped v3.21.2 → v3.22.0. Privacy floor: `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` LOW/1yr advisory carries only `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID) and a timestamp — no individual employee name, email, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `contracted_seats` in `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` payload is an integer (no identity dimension); `billing-event-relay` invalidation path does not touch any employee-level data. Cross-references: `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §35` (v2.7, 2026-06-19 — DEC-069 decision text; §35.4 `getGuardConfig()` implementation; §35.5 billing event KV invalidation; §35.6 edge cases; §35.7 SOC 2 audit traceability three-step protocol; §35.8 OQ-SSO-34.2 🟢 Resolved; §35.9 implementation checklist items 4–5); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §34.7` (`scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` payload `contracted_seats` field — §35.7 traceability shortcut); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §34.11` (original GUARD-E-001 cross-reference obligation — closed by §91); `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §System` (`system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` LOW/1yr — P1/M13 registration obligation per §98.5 item 1); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §91` (GUARD-E-001 primary registration — §98 supplements §91, does not replace it); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-069` (formal adoption decision — Option A: `enterprise_contracts.contracted_seats` with 3600s KV TTL + event-driven invalidation). Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect.*

## §98 · Cross-Reference Patch — SSO_SCIM §35 (BDG `getGuardConfig()` Seat Source: `enterprise_contracts` with Event-Refreshed 1-Hour KV Cache · DEC-069 · OQ-SSO-34.2 · CC6.3 / A1.2 / CC7.2)

> Closes the implicit cross-reference obligation arising from `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §35` (v2.7, 2026-06-19 — DEC-069 · OQ-SSO-34.2 Resolution) and the explicit obligations in §35.9 items 4–5 (P1/M13). Supplements §91 (GUARD-E-001 primary registration) with the CC6.3 audit traceability protocol from §35.7, the A1.2 availability protection rationale from DEC-069, and a new CC7.2 advisory monitoring signal (`system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale`). No new artefact IDs are introduced; GUARD-E-001 (§91) is extended. Privacy floor: `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` advisory carries only `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID) and an emit timestamp — no `user_id`, employee name, email, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 special category data. `contracted_seats` in `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` is an integer derived from the commercial billing record, not from any individual employee record. `billing-event-relay` KV invalidation path handles only `billing.seats_expanded` / `billing.seats_reduced` DEC-030 events and does not read or transmit any employee-level data.

---

### §98.1 SSO_SCIM §35 Context

`docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §35` (v2.7, 2026-06-19) resolves OQ-SSO-34.2 (P1, raised in §34.12) by adopting Option A — `enterprise_contracts.contracted_seats` as the seat source for `getGuardConfig()` — over Option B (real-time `COUNT(tenant_users WHERE is_active = true)`).

| Dimension | Decision (DEC-069) |
|---|---|
| **Seat source** | `enterprise_contracts.contracted_seats` — the billing record of what the tenant has paid for; cached at `scim:guard_cfg:{tenantId}` with 3600s safety-net TTL |
| **Cache invalidation** | Active: `billing-event-relay` Cloudflare Worker calls `SCIM_KV.delete('scim:guard_cfg:' + tenantId)` on receipt of `billing.seats_expanded` or `billing.seats_reduced` DEC-030 events, typically within seconds of a contract change. TTL safety-net: 3600s for missed invalidation events |
| **Option B rejected** | Real-time `COUNT(tenant_users WHERE is_active = true)` per SCIM DELETE/PATCH — N queries for N-user bulk deprovision batch; volatile COUNT has no immutable anchor at block time; DB-error failure mode affects every guard evaluation |
| **SOC 2 grounds (§35.3)** | (1) Anti-DoS: KV decouples guard from DB load at peak bulk-sync pressure. (2) Contract is authoritative: billing entitlement, not provisioning state. (3) Bounded staleness: event-driven invalidation within seconds; 3600s TTL as documented maximum bound. (4) CC6.3 audit traceability: guard limit at any block event is reconstructable from two immutable records per §35.7 protocol. (5) Pattern consistency with DEC-043/044/051/053/065/067 idiom |

---

### §98.2 GUARD-E-001 CC6.3 Traceability Supplement

GUARD-E-001 (registered in §91) collects `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` and related events quarterly. §35 (DEC-069) strengthens the CC6.3 auditor narrative by specifying that the `contracted_seats` field in the block event payload (§34.7) is sourced from `enterprise_contracts.contracted_seats` — an immutable billing record — enabling deterministic reconstruction of the guard limit at any historical block time without requiring a point-in-time active-user snapshot.

#### §35.7 Three-Step Reconstruction Protocol (CC6.3 Auditor Fieldwork)

To verify the guard limit recorded in a `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` event at time **T** for tenant **tid**:

| Step | Query / source | Expected result |
|---|---|---|
| **1 — Contracted seats at T** | `SELECT contracted_seats FROM enterprise_contracts WHERE tenant_id = :tid AND effective_from ≤ :T ORDER BY effective_from DESC LIMIT 1` | Returns the `contracted_seats` value that was in effect at block time T |
| **2 — Threshold at T** | In the DEC-030 HMAC chain: find the most recent `scim.bulk_deprovision_threshold_updated` event with `payload.tenant_id = :tid` and `created_at ≤ :T` → `payload.new_threshold_pct` | Returns the threshold percentage configured at block time T |
| **3 — Guard limit** | `CEIL(contracted_seats × threshold_pct / 100)` | Must equal `payload.deprovisioned_count + 1` (i.e., the block fires at one unit above the limit) |

**Shortcut:** The `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` event payload includes `payload.contracted_seats` (§34.7), which records the value used by `getGuardConfig()` at the time of the block. An auditor can verify this value directly against Step 1 without running the reconstruction — the reconstruction protocol confirms the shortcut field was populated correctly by `enterprise_contracts`, not from a volatile COUNT.

#### GUARD-E-001 Collection SQL Extension

The following assertion SQL should be appended to the §91.2 GUARD-E-001 collection procedure (run at M14 and each subsequent quarter):

```sql
-- DEC-069 §35.7 CC6.3 traceability assertion
-- For every scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked event in the quarter,
-- verify payload.contracted_seats matches enterprise_contracts at block time.
-- Expected: 0 rows (all contracted_seats values match billing record)
SELECT
  ale.event_id,
  ale.created_at,
  ale.payload->>'tenant_id'           AS tenant_id,
  (ale.payload->>'contracted_seats')::int  AS payload_contracted_seats,
  ec.contracted_seats                  AS billing_contracted_seats
FROM audit_log_events ale
JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT contracted_seats
  FROM enterprise_contracts
  WHERE tenant_id = (ale.payload->>'tenant_id')::uuid
    AND effective_from <= ale.created_at
  ORDER BY effective_from DESC
  LIMIT 1
) ec ON true
WHERE ale.event_type = 'scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked'
  AND ale.created_at >= :quarter_start
  AND ale.created_at <  :quarter_end
  AND (ale.payload->>'contracted_seats')::int <> ec.contracted_seats;
-- Zero rows = assertion passes. Non-zero rows = anomaly requiring compliance-officer review.
-- A non-zero result may indicate: (a) a contract amendment processed during the 5-min rolling window,
-- (b) a billing-event-relay missed invalidation that lasted > 3600s (TTL safety-net failure),
-- or (c) a data integrity issue in enterprise_contracts effective_from history.
```

**Privacy floor:** The assertion query reads `enterprise_contracts.contracted_seats` (aggregate integer) and `audit_log_events.payload.contracted_seats` (aggregate integer). No `user_id`, employee name, email, or GDPR Art. 9 data appears in either source or in the assertion output. `tenant_id` is a FORM-internal UUID. `form_api` has NO ACCESS to `enterprise_contracts` (per §91.1 table and migration 0079 RLS policy).

---

### §98.3 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping Supplement

The following rows supplement the criteria coverage established in §91.3 (GUARD-E-001 primary registration).

| Criterion | Supplement from DEC-069 / §35 | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **CC6.3** | **Guard limit reconstruction is deterministic and does not depend on volatile database state.** `getGuardConfig()` resolves `contracted_seats` from `enterprise_contracts` (billing record), not from a real-time `COUNT(tenant_users)`. The §35.7 three-step protocol enables a CC6.3 auditor to reconstruct the exact guard limit at any historical `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` event using only: (a) the `enterprise_contracts` row effective at block time T and (b) the preceding `scim.bulk_deprovision_threshold_updated` DEC-030 chain event. The `payload.contracted_seats` shortcut field provides direct verification. This reconstruction does not require querying live `tenant_users` state or the WAL transaction log. | GUARD-E-001 quarterly export (§91.2) + §98.2 assertion SQL confirming `payload.contracted_seats` matches `enterprise_contracts` at block time |
| **A1.2** | **KV caching decouples guard evaluation from DB load during peak bulk-sync pressure.** If `getGuardConfig()` queried `COUNT(tenant_users WHERE is_active = true)` per SCIM DELETE/PATCH request (Option B, rejected by DEC-069), an N-user bulk deprovision batch would generate N concurrent COUNT queries against Supabase — creating a DB connection pressure feedback loop at the exact moment the guard is designed to activate. Option A (KV hit, zero Supabase queries after warmup) eliminates this feedback loop: the guard fires HTTP 422 immediately from the KV value, before any DB query. On KV cold-cache miss (first request or post-TTL expiry), one Supabase query populates the KV entry; subsequent requests in the same 3600s TTL window are zero-DB-cost. This decoupling is the primary anti-DoS design rationale recorded in DEC-069 §35.3 ground 1. | DEC-069 in `docs/DECISION_LOG.md`; §35.3 grounds and §35.4 `getGuardConfig()` implementation (3600s `kv.put`, single JOIN on cold-cache miss); GUARD-E-001 `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` export confirms guard fires at Worker layer before Supabase mutation |
| **CC7.2** | **`system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` advisory event monitors missed billing-event KV invalidations.** If `billing-event-relay`'s `SCIM_KV.delete` call fails (KV transient error), `billing-event-relay` emits `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` (LOW, 1yr) via `emit-audit-event`. This event does not page on-call (LOW severity); compliance-officer reviews stale-advisory events during the next business day. The 3600s TTL safety-net self-corrects within 1 hour without operator action. The advisory event converts a silent error path (missed KV invalidation) into an observable monitoring signal in the DEC-030 audit chain. | `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` DEC-030 events counted during GUARD-E-001 quarterly collection (see §98.4 stale-advisory cross-check SQL); zero-count quarters confirm the billing-event-relay KV invalidation path operated without errors |

**Stale-advisory cross-check SQL (append to GUARD-E-001 collection):**

```sql
-- CC7.2 stale-advisory count: billing-event-relay KV delete failures during the quarter
-- Expected: 0 rows for a healthy billing event relay; non-zero requires compliance-officer review
SELECT
  payload->>'tenant_id'  AS tenant_id,
  COUNT(*)               AS stale_events,
  MIN(created_at)        AS first_stale_at,
  MAX(created_at)        AS last_stale_at
FROM audit_log_events
WHERE event_type = 'system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale'
  AND created_at >= :quarter_start
  AND created_at <  :quarter_end
GROUP BY payload->>'tenant_id'
ORDER BY stale_events DESC;
-- Zero rows = advisory path operational, no missed invalidations.
-- Non-zero: review whether a block event fired during the ≤ 3600s stale window;
-- if blocked_event.contracted_seats < actual contracted_seats, note in GUARD-E-001 filing.
```

---

(2) §80.3 R2 folder structure — `etf-cron/` subfolder added after `renewals/` with `form-api NO ACCESS` invariant (consistent with `renewals/`, `winloss/`, `siem-consent/`, `expansion/` precedents). SOC 2 doc v3.22.0 → v3.22.1. Privacy floor: assertion SQL output contains `event_id`, `created_at`, `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID), and two integers (`payload_contracted_seats`, `billing_contracted_seats`); no `user_id`, employee name, email, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 data in either source or output; stale-advisory SQL output contains `tenant_id` + aggregate integers + timestamps only. 7yr; quarterly from M10); CC6-E-CAEP-003 (tenant_sso_configs CAEP stream registration snapshot sanitized — CC6.3; excludes caep_stream_id + caep_webhook_secret; quarterly from M10; 3yr); CC6-E-CAEP-004 (AL-CAEP-01 + AL-CAEP-03 PagerDuty incident history — CC7.2/CC7.3; PagerDuty API export service `form-caep-stream`; quarterly from M10; 3yr). §97.3 four-row criteria mapping: CC6.3 (CAEP pipeline < 30 s gap closure vs. 15 min JWT TTL; Addendum 6 + §23 + CAEP-E-001 form the complete CC6.3 access-continuity stack; CC6-E-CAEP-001/002/003 evidence roles), CC6.6 (credential-change → revoke_all_sessions; token-claims-change → force_reauth KV flag TTL 24h; CC6-E-CAEP-001 action_taken distribution + CC6-E-CAEP-002 revocation event chain), CC7.2 (five alerting rules AL-CAEP-01 through AL-CAEP-05; CC6-E-CAEP-004 PagerDuty history for AL-CAEP-01/03), CC7.3 (account-purged GDPR deletion clock + §23.8 ordering invariant sso.caep_user_purged CRITICAL before state mutation; FORM-side processing latency derivable from CC6-E-CAEP-002 event timestamp delta). §97.4 four-row stale reference correction record (§94 intro blockquote, §94.4 CC6.3 row, §94 version footer — all corrected in-place with *(§97 patch)* annotation). §97.5 four obligations closed: SSO_SCIM §23.10 CC6-E-CAEP registration obligation (🟢), MSA Addendum 6.4 CC6-E-CAEP-002 reference obligation (🟢), §94 implicit stale-reference obligation (🟢), §90–§96 pattern series §23 gap (🟢). §97.6 five-item implementation checklist: 3× P0/M10 (§79.4 criteria rows registration, §80.3 caep/ subfolder confirmation, §80.4 Vanta mirror list addition), 2× P1/M10 (M5–M10 pre-flight zero-event filing, quarterly CAEP evidence collection schedule consolidation with CAEP-E-001). §80.4 Vanta mirror list: CC6-E-CAEP-001/002/003/004 added. SOC 2 doc v3.22.1 → v3.23.0. Owner: compliance-officer + enterprise-architect.*

## §99 · Cross-Reference Patch — DATA_MODEL §42 (`enterprise_contracts` Expansion Tracking Schema · migration 0083 · CC5.2 / CC1.4 / CC4.1 / A1.2)

> Closes the implicit cross-reference obligation arising from `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42` (v1.21, 2026-06-20 — migration 0083, `enterprise_contracts` expansion tracking columns). `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` (v3.23.0, 2026-06-20) registered EXP-E-001 and EXP-E-002 citing `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41` as the authoritative spec — but DATA_MODEL §42 was authored as the canonical Postgres DDL source (migration 0083) after v3.23.0 was committed. §99 closes this sequencing gap by registering DATA_MODEL §42 as the authoritative DDL source, supplementing the §79.4 evidence artefact entries with DDL-level proofs, and extending the SOC 2 criteria mapping with the CHECK constraint and partial-index invariants introduced by migration 0083. Pattern: §90–§98 series; same obligation type as §96 (DATA_MODEL §41 → §90 gap). No new artefact IDs are introduced; EXP-E-001 and EXP-E-002 (§79.4) are supplemented, not replaced. Privacy floor: all four columns added by migration 0083 (`initial_seats`, `current_seats`, `expansion_count`, `last_expansion_date`) are aggregate integers or a calendar date at the contract level — no `user_id`, employee name, email, health metric, or GDPR Art. 9 special category data is introduced by this migration or by any evidence artefact produced from it.

---

### §99.1 Three-Document Chain

The expansion tracking evidence chain runs through three documents, each authored at a different milestone:

| Step | Document | Role in chain | Version / date |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41` | Expansion economics spec: seat-expansion revenue model, NRR decomposition, discount authority matrix, DEC-030 Zod v2 schemas for four events, and original EXP-E-001/EXP-E-002 artefact definitions | v2.7, 2026-06-20 |
| 2 | `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4/§80.3/§80.4` (v3.23.0) | Evidence artefact registration: EXP-E-001 (CC5.2/CC1.4 — annual `billing.seats_expanded` floor attestation) and EXP-E-002 (CC4.1 — quarterly adoption health band cross-reference); `expansion/` R2 subfolder; Vanta mirror list entry | v3.23.0, 2026-06-20 |
| 3 | `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42` | Canonical Postgres DDL implementation: migration 0083 (`ALTER TABLE enterprise_contracts ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS initial_seats / current_seats / expansion_count / last_expansion_date`); CHECK constraints; two partial indexes; back-fill UPDATE; staging validation checklist; RLS behaviour; BDG cache invalidation sequence; four DEC-030 events (operational summary); `tenant_contract_portal` view patch excluding `initial_seats` | v1.21, 2026-06-20 |

**Sequencing gap:** SOC2_READINESS v3.23.0 (Step 2) pre-dated DATA_MODEL §42 (Step 3) by the same commit. v3.23.0 referenced `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41` as the DDL spec — which is correct for the economic model but insufficient for an auditor seeking the Postgres migration SQL. §99 closes this gap: DATA_MODEL §42 is now the authoritative DDL source for the four expansion tracking columns, and §42.3's staging validation checklist contains the DDL-level invariant proofs required for CC5.2 and CC1.4 auditor review.

**Pattern precedent:** §96 (v3.21.0, 2026-06-19) established the same three-document chain for `enterprise_adoption_snapshots`: COST_MODEL §40 (economics) → SOC2_READINESS §90 (evidence registration) → DATA_MODEL §41 (canonical DDL). §99 applies the identical closure pattern to the expansion tracking extension.

---

### §99.2 Canonical DDL Source Registration

`docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42` (migration 0083) is the authoritative Postgres DDL source for the four `enterprise_contracts` expansion tracking columns. The table below registers the design invariants that matter to SOC 2 auditors.

| Invariant | DDL location | SOC 2 relevance |
|---|---|---|
| `CHECK (current_seats >= initial_seats)` on `enterprise_contracts` | §42.3 migration 0083 `ALTER TABLE` block | **CC5.2** — price-floor enforcement corollary: the DDL forbids recording a seat count that has *decreased* relative to the original contract. An expansion that decremented `current_seats` would violate this constraint before reaching the HMAC chain layer. Provides an additional DDL-layer backstop behind the `z.literal(true)` chain constraint. |
| `CHECK (expansion_count >= 0)` on `enterprise_contracts` | §42.3 migration 0083 `ALTER TABLE` block | **CC1.4** — amendment counter integrity: `expansion_count` cannot be negative, preventing any bookkeeping anomaly that would undercount approved expansions in the NRR decomposition or misrepresent the audit trail depth to an auditor. |
| `initial_seats NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` + back-fill `SET initial_seats = contracted_seats WHERE initial_seats = 0` | §42.3 migration 0083 back-fill UPDATE | **CC1.4** — baseline establishment: every existing contract is assigned an `initial_seats` value equal to its `contracted_seats` at migration time. This makes the expansion ratio `(current_seats − initial_seats) / initial_seats` defined and non-null for all contracts in the SOC 2 observation period, even those signed before migration 0083 was applied. |
| `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ec_expansion_count ON enterprise_contracts (expansion_count DESC) WHERE expansion_count > 0` | §42.3 partial index | **A1.2** — evidence collection at scale: EXP-E-001 and EXP-E-002 JOIN `enterprise_contracts` on `expansion_count > 0`. Without this partial index, the collection queries scan the full `enterprise_contracts` table regardless of how many tenants have ever expanded. The partial index guarantees that evidence collection remains sub-second at enterprise scale (100+ tenants) without a schema change at observation time. |
| `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ec_last_expansion_date ON enterprise_contracts (last_expansion_date) WHERE last_expansion_date IS NOT NULL` | §42.3 partial index | **A1.2** — date-range evidence collection: EXP-E-001's annual sweep and EXP-E-002's quarterly cross-reference both filter on `last_expansion_date` ranges. The partial index (excluding NULL rows, i.e., contracts with no expansion history) ensures the evidence collection queries remain performant as the `enterprise_contracts` table grows. |
| `tenant_contract_portal` view excludes `initial_seats` (alongside `arr_cents`) | §42.5 RLS behaviour + view patch | **CC1.4 / privacy floor** — see §99.3 Supplement 1 below |
| `tenant_manager` (HR role) excluded from all `enterprise_contracts` RLS policies | §16.4 (unchanged by migration 0083); confirmed in §42.5 | **CC4.2** — deficiency evaluation: commercial contract terms, including seat counts and expansion history, are outside HR's operational scope. The exclusion is unchanged by migration 0083 — §99 confirms explicitly that the four new columns are covered by the existing exclusion without any policy modification required. |

**Staging validation as auditor evidence:** §42.3 includes a five-step staging validation checklist that exercises each DDL invariant programmatically (column types, back-fill correctness, index scan confirmation, CHECK violation test). Compliance-officer should retain the staging validation output (`\d+ enterprise_contracts` dump + `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` output + CHECK violation test log) as a migration artefact filed alongside EXP-E-001 at the first annual collection. File path: `compliance/evidence/expansion/migration-0083-validation_<YYYY-MM-DD>.txt`.

---

### §99.3 Privacy Floor Supplements

#### Supplement 1 — `initial_seats` Exclusion from `tenant_contract_portal`

`docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42.5` introduces a patch to the `tenant_contract_portal` view that excludes `initial_seats` from the columns visible to `tenant_admin` and `tenant_owner` roles via `form_api`. The patch adds `initial_seats` to the existing exclusion list alongside `arr_cents`:

```sql
-- From DATA_MODEL §42.5 — tenant_contract_portal re-creation:
-- Excluded: arr_cents (commercial sensitivity — existing), initial_seats (expansion benchmark — new)
-- Included: current_seats, expansion_count, last_expansion_date
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW tenant_contract_portal AS
  SELECT id, tenant_id, contract_ref, plan, seats_contracted,
         current_seats, expansion_count, last_expansion_date,
         billing_period, po_number, start_at, end_at, auto_renew, status
         -- arr_cents excluded  -- initial_seats excluded
  FROM enterprise_contracts WHERE status = 'active';
```

**Auditor implication:** `initial_seats` reveals the seat count at contract signing, which allows a tenant to infer FORM's expansion velocity benchmark (the `(current_seats − initial_seats)` delta is FORM's internal expansion KPI for that tenant). Exposing this to `tenant_admin` creates an information-asymmetry risk that is outside the scope of the Admin Dashboard self-service contract portal. The exclusion is consistent with the principle that FORM-internal commercial metrics are not disclosed through the tenant-facing API layer.

**Evidence artefact implication:** EXP-E-002's `enterprise_contracts` JOIN returns `expansion_count` and `last_expansion_date`, not `initial_seats` — confirming that the health-band cross-reference evidence does not expose the FORM-internal expansion benchmark to any output routed to the tenant. EXP-E-001 likewise uses only `billing.seats_expanded` DEC-030 chain events (which carry `seats_before` and `seats_after`, not `initial_seats`).

#### Supplement 2 — `tenant_manager` (HR) Exclusion Confirmed for New Columns

The four columns added by migration 0083 are covered by the existing §16.4 RLS exclusion for `tenant_manager` (the HR-adjacent role) without any policy modification. §42.5 explicitly confirms this. The privacy floor principle — "HR never sees individual user data" — extends here to commercial contract metadata (seat counts, expansion dates) that HR does not need for any legitimate workflow.

**Auditor implication for CC4.2:** The `tenant_manager` exclusion is enforced at the DDL layer (RLS policy) rather than at the application layer, making it audit-grade: a `tenant_manager` session cannot bypass it via a `form_api` call, and `form_api` itself has no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on `enterprise_contracts`. Any access audit (`SELECT * FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename = 'enterprise_contracts'`) will show zero rows matching `roles @> ARRAY['tenant_manager']` with SELECT privilege — a one-query DDL proof for CC4.2.

---

### §99.4 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping Supplement

The rows below supplement the §79.4 criteria mapping for EXP-E-001 and EXP-E-002 with the DDL-layer evidence from DATA_MODEL §42. §79.4 artefact definitions and collection SQL are unchanged; this section adds the DDL invariant layer that was not available when §79.4 was authored.

| Criterion | §79.4 coverage (EXP-E-001/002) | §99 DDL supplement from DATA_MODEL §42 |
|---|---|---|
| **CC5.2** — Selecting and developing control activities | EXP-E-001 collection SQL uses `floor_respected: true` attestation assertion over `billing.seats_expanded` chain events; annual attestation that zero events have `floor_respected IS DISTINCT FROM TRUE` | Migration 0083 `CHECK (current_seats >= initial_seats)` provides a DDL-layer backstop: even if the `z.literal(true)` chain gate were circumvented at the Worker layer (which is impossible without deploying a modified Worker), the DDL constraint would reject any `UPDATE` that decremented `current_seats` below `initial_seats`. The chain gate and the DDL constraint are independent control layers, each independently auditable. |
| **CC1.4** — Demonstrates commitment to competence | EXP-E-001 `amendment_id` field correlates each expansion event to a DocuSign counter-signature, establishing the competency of the approval chain | `initial_seats` back-fill UPDATE (§42.3): every existing contract has `initial_seats = contracted_seats` at migration time, ensuring the NRR decomposition denominator is defined and non-null across the full observation period. `CHECK (expansion_count >= 0)` prevents negative expansion counts — a bookkeeping anomaly that would indicate a process deficiency requiring auditor attention. Both invariants are exercised by the §42.3 staging validation queries, which serve as the first-deployment control-activity test for CC1.4. |
| **CC4.1** — Selects, develops, and performs ongoing evaluations | EXP-E-002 quarterly cross-reference confirms all expansions occurred in Green-band or Amber-band months (not Red-band); red-band anomaly count is a named finding requiring explanation | `idx_ec_expansion_count` partial index (§42.3): guarantees EXP-E-002 collection query performance at scale. Without this index, EXP-E-002 would degrade to a full-table scan as `enterprise_contracts` grows, creating an operational risk that quarterly evidence collection would be delayed or skipped. The index is a prerequisite for CC4.1 "ongoing" (i.e., consistently cadenced) evaluations. |
| **A1.2** — Environmental threats — availability | EXP-E-001/EXP-E-002 do not directly address availability | §42.6 BDG cache invalidation: the `billing-event-relay` Worker deletes `scim:guard_cfg:{tenantId}` within milliseconds of `billing.seats_expanded` emission. Without this invalidation, `getGuardConfig()` would return the pre-expansion `contracted_seats` for up to the 3600s safety-net TTL — creating a window in which newly purchased seats are blocked by the BDG (false-positive block). A false-positive block during a SCIM bulk-provisioning run for a recently expanded tenant is an A1.2 availability threat: the provisioning would fail silently for the new seats, causing the tenant to report a service degradation incident. Migration 0083 + §42.6 together eliminate this threat by coupling the `current_seats` update and the KV cache invalidation to the same `billing.seats_expanded` post-event hook. `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` LOW/1yr advisory (§42.6 failure mode, SSO_SCIM §35.5) is the monitoring signal that fires if the cache invalidation fails — routing to `#alerts-platform` Slack for next-business-day review. |

---

| Obligation | Source | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Implicit §90–§98 pattern obligation: every DATA_MODEL section introducing canonical DDL for a table (or table extension) whose SOC 2 evidence artefacts are registered in SOC2_READINESS receives a bidirectional cross-reference patch | `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42` (v1.21, 2026-06-20) introduced migration 0083 DDL for four `enterprise_contracts` columns; SOC2_READINESS v3.23.0 registered EXP-E-001/002 citing COST_MODEL §41 — not DATA_MODEL §42 — as the DDL source | 🟢 **Closed — §99 registers DATA_MODEL §42 as canonical DDL source; §99.4 supplements §79.4 criteria mapping with DDL-layer invariants; §99.3 formalises two privacy floor supplements** |
| DATA_MODEL §42 one-way reference closure: §42.8 states "Full auditor narratives, SQL collection queries, and cadence specifications are in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4/§80.3/§80.4` (registered v3.23.0, 2026-06-20)" — creating a one-way reference from §42 to §79.4 | `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42.8` (v1.21, 2026-06-20) | 🟢 **Closed — §99 creates the return pointer: SOC2_READINESS now cross-references DATA_MODEL §42 as the canonical DDL source, making the chain bidirectional** |
| §99.6 implementation checklist item 3 (`tenant_contract_portal` view patch for `initial_seats` exclusion) | `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42.5` — view patch specified but not yet applied as a discrete migration | 🟡 **Pending — tracked in §99.6 item 3 (P0/M10, platform-engineer)** |

### §99.6 Implementation Checklist

#### P0 — Before first expansion event is processed (M10)

| # | Task | Owner | Priority | Milestone | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Apply migration `0083_enterprise_contracts_expansion_fields.sql` and run the five-step staging validation checklist from §42.3: (a) `information_schema.columns` type/default check for all four new columns; (b) back-fill zero-count check (`unfilled = 0`); (c) `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` confirming index scan on `idx_ec_expansion_count` with ≥ 50 synthetic rows where `expansion_count > 0`; (d) `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` confirming index scan on `idx_ec_last_expansion_date` with a date-range filter; (e) CHECK violation test confirming `current_seats < initial_seats` raises constraint error. Retain full staging output at `compliance/evidence/expansion/migration-0083-validation_<YYYY-MM-DD>.txt` for first EXP-E-001 artefact package. Cross-reference: §42.9 item 1 (tracks the same operational act — filing here marks §42.9 item 1 done simultaneously). | platform-engineer | **P0** | M10 | [ ] |
| 2 | Implement `billing-event-relay` KV cache invalidation hook (§42.6 step 3c) and `emit-audit-event` Worker post-event hook for `enterprise.expansion_initiated` (UPDATE `contracted_seats`, `current_seats`, `expansion_count`, `last_expansion_date` in a single transaction after chain event). Integration test: emit a synthetic `billing.seats_expanded` event; call `getGuardConfig()` immediately after; confirm KV cache miss forces a re-query from Supabase and returns the updated `contracted_seats`. Cross-reference: §42.9 item 2 (same operational act). | platform-engineer | **P0** | M10 | [ ] |
| 3 | Apply `tenant_contract_portal` view patch (§42.5) to exclude `initial_seats` from the columns visible to `form_api` / `tenant_admin` / `tenant_owner` reads. Confirm via Supabase column-level permission test: `SET ROLE form_api; SELECT initial_seats FROM tenant_contract_portal LIMIT 1;` must return `ERROR: column "initial_seats" does not exist`. File test output in `compliance/evidence/expansion/` alongside migration-0083-validation. | platform-engineer | **P0** | M10 | [ ] |

#### P1 — Before first EXP-E-001 annual filing (M12 or first post-GA observation-year anniversary)

| # | Task | Owner | Priority | Milestone | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 4 | At first EXP-E-001 annual filing: (a) run §42.8 EXP-E-001 collection SQL; (b) run floor-violation assertion (`floor_violations = 0`); (c) add `expansion_count` cross-reference assertion — for each `billing.seats_expanded` event in the period, verify that `enterprise_contracts.expansion_count` for the same `tenant_id` equals the count of `billing.seats_expanded` events for that tenant: `SELECT ec.tenant_id, ec.expansion_count, chain.event_count, (ec.expansion_count = chain.event_count) AS counts_match FROM enterprise_contracts ec JOIN (SELECT (payload->>'tenant_id')::UUID AS tenant_id, COUNT(*) AS event_count FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'billing.seats_expanded' GROUP BY 1) chain USING (tenant_id) WHERE ec.expansion_count > 0;` — all rows must have `counts_match = true`; non-matching rows indicate a transaction integrity issue requiring compliance-officer review before filing; (d) file combined output at `compliance/evidence/expansion/EXP-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`; (e) append SHA-256 to MASTER-INDEX (§79). | compliance-officer | **P1** | M12 (first filing) | [ ] |

*v3.24.0 (2026-06-20): §99 Cross-Reference Patch — DATA_MODEL §42 (`enterprise_contracts` Expansion Tracking Schema · migration 0083 · CC5.2 / CC1.4 / CC4.1 / A1.2). Closes the implicit pattern obligation established by §90–§98: every DATA_MODEL section introducing canonical DDL for a table (or additive table extension) whose SOC 2 evidence artefacts are registered in SOC2_READINESS receives a bidirectional cross-reference patch. `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42` (v1.21, 2026-06-20) is the authoritative `enterprise_contracts` expansion tracking DDL source (migration 0083, four columns, two CHECK constraints, two partial indexes, five-step staging validation checklist, `tenant_contract_portal` view patch); SOC2_READINESS v3.23.0 registered EXP-E-001/EXP-E-002 and `expansion/` R2 subfolder citing `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41` as the DDL spec — pre-dating DATA_MODEL §42 by the same commit, creating a sequencing gap identical to the §96 / DATA_MODEL §41 pattern. §99.1 three-document chain: COST_MODEL §41 (v2.7 — expansion economics spec + original EXP-E-001/002 definitions) → SOC2_READINESS §79.4 (v3.23.0 — artefact registration citing COST_MODEL §41) → DATA_MODEL §42 (v1.21 — canonical Postgres DDL, four columns, CHECK constraints, partial indexes, BDG cache invalidation sequence, `tenant_contract_portal` view patch). §99.2 canonical DDL source registration: seven-row invariant table mapping migration 0083 DDL constructs to SOC 2 relevance — `CHECK (current_seats >= initial_seats)` (CC5.2 DDL backstop behind `z.literal(true)` chain gate), `CHECK (expansion_count >= 0)` (CC1.4 counter integrity), `initial_seats` back-fill UPDATE (CC1.4 baseline establishment for full observation period), `idx_ec_expansion_count` partial index (A1.2 evidence-collection performance), `idx_ec_last_expansion_date` partial index (A1.2 date-range query performance), `tenant_contract_portal` view patch (CC1.4/privacy floor — `initial_seats` excluded), `tenant_manager` exclusion confirmed (CC4.2 — HR outside commercial contract scope). §42.3 staging validation output registered as migration artefact to be filed alongside EXP-E-001 at first annual collection. §99.3 two privacy floor supplements: (1) `initial_seats` exclusion from `tenant_contract_portal` — exposes only `current_seats`, `expansion_count`, `last_expansion_date` to `tenant_admin`; `initial_seats` is FORM-internal expansion benchmark; EXP-E-001/002 artefact design confirmed to not expose `initial_seats` to any tenant-facing output; (2) `tenant_manager` exclusion confirmed — all four new columns covered by existing §16.4 RLS exclusion without any policy modification; `SELECT * FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename = 'enterprise_contracts'` returns zero rows granting SELECT to `tenant_manager` (CC4.2 one-query DDL proof). §99.4 four-row SOC 2 criteria mapping supplement (DDL reinforcement only — §79.4 artefact definitions and collection SQL unchanged): CC5.2 (`CHECK (current_seats >= initial_seats)` as independent DDL backstop to `z.literal(true)` chain gate — two independent control layers), CC1.4 (back-fill UPDATE + `CHECK (expansion_count >= 0)` ensure NRR denominator is defined and counter is non-negative across full observation period; §42.3 staging validation is first-deployment CC1.4 control-activity test), CC4.1 (`idx_ec_expansion_count` partial index as prerequisite for sustained quarterly EXP-E-002 cadence — evidence-collection performance is part of "ongoing" monitoring under CC4.1), A1.2 (§42.6 BDG cache invalidation eliminates false-positive SCIM block during post-expansion provisioning; `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` LOW/1yr advisory is the A1.2 monitoring signal for invalidation failure). §99.5 three obligations closed: §90–§98 pattern obligation (🟢), DATA_MODEL §42 one-way reference closure (🟢), §99.6 item 3 `tenant_contract_portal` view patch (🟡 pending P0/M10). §99.6 four-item implementation checklist: 3× P0/M10 (migration 0083 staging validation + artefact retention, `billing-event-relay` KV invalidation hook + integration test, `tenant_contract_portal` view patch + column-level permission test), 1× P1/M12 (first EXP-E-001 annual filing including `expansion_count` cross-reference assertion SQL). SOC 2 doc v3.23.0 → v3.24.0. Privacy floor: all four migration 0083 columns (`initial_seats`, `current_seats`, `expansion_count`, `last_expansion_date`) are aggregate integers or a calendar date at the contract level; no `user_id`, employee name, email, health metric value, coaching content, body composition, or GDPR Art. 9 special category data; `tenant_id` is a FORM-internal UUID; `initial_seats` excluded from `tenant_contract_portal` (not exposed to `form_api` tenant reads); `tenant_manager` excluded from all `enterprise_contracts` RLS policies (unchanged by migration 0083); staging validation artefact (`migration-0083-validation_<YYYY-MM-DD>.txt`) contains only DDL metadata and `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` output — no user data of any kind. Cross-references: `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42` (v1.21, 2026-06-20 — §42.1 design principles; §42.3 migration 0083 DDL + staging validation checklist; §42.4 column semantics; §42.5 RLS behaviour + `tenant_contract_portal` view patch; §42.6 BDG cache invalidation sequence; §42.7 four DEC-030 events operational summary; §42.8 EXP-E-001/EXP-E-002 collection SQL); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` (primary EXP-E-001/EXP-E-002 evidence narrative and collection SQL — §99 supplements, does not replace; §42.6 BDG cache invalidation is the A1.2 control described in §99.4); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §35.5` (`billing-event-relay` KV delete + `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` LOW/1yr advisory — A1.2 monitoring signal described in §99.4); `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §"Enterprise Seat Expansion & Tier Upgrade events"` (v2.22 — four DEC-030 events: `enterprise.expansion_initiated` STANDARD/7yr, `billing.seats_expanded` STANDARD/7yr, `enterprise.tier_upgraded` STANDARD/7yr, `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced` HIGH/7yr); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §96` (DATA_MODEL §41 cross-reference patch — pattern precedent; §96.1 three-document chain for `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` mirrors §99.1 structure); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §80.3` (`expansion/` R2 subfolder — §99.6 item 1 staging validation artefact filed here alongside EXP-E-001/002); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §16.4` (`enterprise_contracts` original schema + RLS policies — §42 additive extension; `tenant_manager` exclusion confirmed in §99.3 Supplement 2). ## §100 · Cross-Reference Patch — DATA_MODEL §43 (`enterprise_renewals` Schema · Migration 0084 · CC5.2 / CC1.4 / CC4.1)

> Closes the implicit pattern obligation established by §90–§99: every DATA_MODEL section introducing canonical DDL for a new enterprise table whose SOC 2 evidence artefacts are registered in SOC2_READINESS receives a bidirectional cross-reference patch.
> `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §43` (v1.22, 2026-06-20) is the canonical DDL registration for `enterprise_renewals` (migration 0084). `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42` (v2.8, 2026-06-20) authored the DDL inline as part of the renewal economics spec. §100 creates the return pointer, registers the DDL-layer invariants, and closes the evidence artefact cross-reference chain for REN-E-001/002/003.

### §100.1 Three-Document Chain

| Layer | Document | Version | Date | Role |
|-------|----------|---------|------|------|
| **Economic spec + DDL authoring** | `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42` | v2.8 | 2026-06-20 | Renewal pricing mechanics, price escalation model, multi-year commitment, `enterprise_renewals` DDL spec, DEC-030 Zod v2 schemas, RENEW-CHAIN-01/ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 chain invariants, REN-E-001/002/003 evidence artefact definitions |
| **Canonical DDL registration** | `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §43` | v1.22 | 2026-06-20 | Canonical Postgres DDL, RLS policies (REVOKE form_api), RENEW-CHAIN-01/ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 DDL relationship, SOC 2 evidence queries, staging validation checklist |
| **SOC 2 cross-reference closure** | `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §100` | v3.24.1 | 2026-06-20 | This section — bidirectional cross-reference registration, canonical DDL source update for REN-E-001/002/003, DDL-layer invariant auditor narratives, implementation checklist |
| **DEC-030 event registration** | `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise Contract Renewal` | v2.23 | 2026-06-20 | Three DEC-030 HMAC-chained events registered: `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` (STANDARD, 7yr), `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated` (HIGH, 7yr), `enterprise.contract_renewed` (STANDARD, 7yr); RENEW-CHAIN-01/ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 invariant blocks; Zod v2 schemas; REN-E-001/002/003 evidence artefact table; auditor narratives (CC5.2/CC1.4/CC4.1/CC2.2/A1.1). Closes COST_MODEL §42.10 item 1 (P0/M12 — 🟢 2026-06-20). |

**Sequencing note:** Unlike the §96/§99 pattern where DATA_MODEL additions post-dated SOC2_READINESS artefact registration, §43 and §100 are added simultaneously with COST_MODEL §42 (all 2026-06-20). The DEC-030 event registration in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.23 completes the four-document chain.

---

### §100.2 Canonical DDL Source Registration

DATA_MODEL §43 is the **canonical DDL source** for `enterprise_renewals` and its associated ENUMs (`renewal_type_enum`, `rate_basis_enum`), indexes, and RLS policies. COST_MODEL §42.6 is the **authoritative economic spec** — it defines the business rationale for each column and the chain invariants, but DATA_MODEL §43 is the single-source-of-truth for the Postgres DDL artefact.

| Construct | DATA_MODEL §43 location | SOC 2 relevance |
|-----------|------------------------|----------------|
| `CREATE TYPE renewal_type_enum` | §43.3.1 | Enables `renewal_type`-based audit queries for REN-E-003; auditor can enumerate valid values via `SELECT enum_range(NULL::renewal_type_enum)` |
| `CREATE TYPE rate_basis_enum` | §43.3.1 | Enables price-basis audit trail; `'escalation'` value is the ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 trigger condition |
| `CONSTRAINT chk_floor_always_respected CHECK (floor_respected = true)` | §43.3.1 | **Primary DDL-layer control for CC5.2** — pricing floor cannot be violated at INSERT regardless of Worker-layer state |
| `CONSTRAINT chk_escalation_fields` | §43.3.1 | **DDL-layer backstop for ESCALATION-CHAIN-01** — escalation audit fields (`escalation_pct`, `cpi_reference_month`) cannot be partially null |
| `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_er_tenant_renewal_date` | §43.3.1 | Performance guarantee for REN-E-002 quarterly notice compliance query; prevents evidence collection delay at fleet scale |
| `CREATE INDEX idx_er_renewal_type` | §43.3.1 | Performance guarantee for REN-E-003 annual conversion rate cross-tab |
| `REVOKE ALL ON enterprise_renewals FROM form_api` | §43.5 | CC6.1 access control — financial contract pricing data excluded from tenant-facing API |
| `notice_event_id UUID` (soft-ref column) | §43.4 | Per-row RENEW-CHAIN-01 compliance anchor; populated at INSERT time by Admin Console workflow; enables §43.6 chain compliance query |
| Two GENERATED ALWAYS AS STORED columns (`new_acv_usd`, `new_tcv_usd`) | §43.4 | REN-E-003 ARR waterfall queries use stored computed values — no in-query arithmetic risk; auditor can recompute from base columns to verify |

---

### §100.3 Privacy Floor Supplements

**Supplement 1 — No tenant role may access `enterprise_renewals`.**

Unlike `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` (§41, which grants `tenant_admin` and `tenant_owner` read access to their own aggregate health metrics), `enterprise_renewals` is FORM-internal financial data. Per-seat pricing, discount percentages, escalation rates, and TCV figures are sensitive commercial terms. No `tenant_admin`, `tenant_owner`, or `tenant_manager` role has any SELECT grant on `enterprise_renewals`. The full renewal history of a customer is invisible to the customer's own admin users — visible only to `form_admin` (internal FORM staff: compliance-officer, CSM, finance) and `compliance_reviewer` (SOC 2 auditor). **No exception to this rule exists.** Any request to add tenant-facing access to `enterprise_renewals` must be reviewed by enterprise-architect + compliance-officer before any DDL change.

**Supplement 2 — `tenant_manager` (HR/People-ops) exclusion confirmed.**

`tenant_manager` is the HR-adjacent admin role (People Operations, Heads of HR). It must never see individual employee wellness data (§16.4 privacy floor). By extension, it must also never see commercial renewal pricing data — renewal economics could be used to infer budget allocation, headcount, or organizational growth plans that HR personnel do not need for any legitimate workflow. The `form_api` REVOKE and the absence of any `tenant_manager` RLS policy on `enterprise_renewals` enforce this at the DDL layer without requiring any application-layer gate.

**Supplement 3 — `cpi_reference_month` is a date, not a URL.**

The BLS CPI-U reference data used in escalation calculations is a public dataset. The `cpi_reference_month` column stores only the first day of the reference month (e.g., `2026-01-01`), never a BLS URL or download link. This is consistent with the COST_MODEL §42.5.1 privacy note: storing a URL could create a data-adjacent audit trail that implies when FORM staff accessed the BLS website, which is unnecessary for the compliance record. The date alone identifies the BLS report unambiguously (BLS publishes one CPI-U release per month).

---

### §100.4 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping Supplement

The rows below supplement the §79.4 criteria mapping for REN-E-001/002/003 with DDL-layer evidence from DATA_MODEL §43. §79.4 artefact definitions, collection SQL, and cadence specifications are in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.9` (the authoritative source); this section adds the DDL invariant layer not yet captured in §79.4 at the time COST_MODEL §42 was authored.

| Criterion | COST_MODEL §42.9 coverage | §100 DDL supplement from DATA_MODEL §43 |
|-----------|--------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| **CC5.2** — Selecting and developing control activities (REN-E-001 primary criterion) | REN-E-001 annual chain export asserts `floor_respected: true` across all `enterprise.contract_renewed` events; Zod `z.literal(true)` is the primary Worker-layer control | Migration 0084 `CONSTRAINT chk_floor_always_respected CHECK (floor_respected = true)` is the DDL-layer backstop: even a malformed INSERT that bypasses the Worker schema validation would be rejected by Supabase at the DDL layer with a CHECK violation, returning HTTP 500 from the Admin Console workflow and surfacing the integrity issue visibly — not silently. Two independent control layers (Zod guard + DDL CHECK), each auditable via `\d enterprise_renewals`. |
| **CC1.4** — Demonstrates commitment to competence (REN-E-001 secondary criterion) | REN-E-001 `notice_event_id` FK correlation proves RENEW-CHAIN-01 compliance per renewal event; `confirmed_by` UUID in chain event establishes accountability | `UNIQUE INDEX idx_er_tenant_renewal_date (tenant_id, renewal_date)` enforces one renewal row per renewal date per tenant at the DDL layer — a duplicate renewal INSERT (which could indicate a process deficiency or race condition in the Admin Console workflow) fails with UNIQUE VIOLATION before any chain event is emitted, making it impossible to create duplicate renewal records without an observable DDL-layer error. `notice_event_id` soft-ref column is populated at INSERT time (§43.8 item 3 integration test); compliance query `WHERE notice_event_id IS NULL` provides auditor with a one-query chain integrity check. |
| **CC4.1** — Selects, develops, and performs ongoing evaluations (REN-E-002 and REN-E-003 primary criterion) | REN-E-002 quarterly notice compliance export; REN-E-003 annual renewal conversion rate analysis — both require consistent cadenced collection | `CREATE INDEX idx_er_renewal_date ON enterprise_renewals(renewal_date DESC)` guarantees REN-E-002 quarterly range scans perform at O(log n) as fleet grows. `CREATE INDEX idx_er_renewal_type ON enterprise_renewals(renewal_type)` guarantees REN-E-003 `renewal_type` cross-tab executes at index-scan speed rather than sequential scan, ensuring the annual analysis can be completed within the evidence-collection window even at 200+ tenant fleet scale. Without these indexes, "ongoing" (consistently cadenced) evaluation becomes operationally fragile at growth. |
| **CC2.2** — Communicates information (REN-E-002 secondary criterion) | REN-E-002 quarterly export proves FORM sent 90-day renewal notices per contractual commitment (MSA §6.1) | `CONSTRAINT chk_escalation_fields` ensures price escalation notices are internally consistent at INSERT: any notice with `escalation_applied = true` must carry `escalation_pct` and `cpi_reference_month` — the data that backs the contractual communication to the customer under MSA §8.6. An escalation notice sent without a documented CPI calculation is not auditable; the DDL constraint makes it impossible. |
