## §101 · Cross-Reference Patch — DATA_MODEL §44 (`enterprise_churn_events` Schema · Migration 0085 · C1.2 / CC6.1 / CC4.1 / CC5.2)

> Closes cross-reference obligation from `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §44.11` (v1.26, 2026-06-23) and `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.10 item 10` (v1.0, 2026-06-23): §101 registers DATA_MODEL §44 as the canonical DDL source for `enterprise_churn_events`, maps DDL-layer invariants to SOC 2 criteria C1.2 / CC6.1 / CC4.1 / CC5.2, and closes the bidirectional three-document chain for the enterprise post-churn lifecycle. Follows the §84–§100 pattern.

### §101.1 Three-Document Chain

| Layer | Document | Version | Date | Role |
|-------|----------|---------|------|------|
| **Economic spec + DDL authoring** | `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43` | v1.0 | 2026-06-23 | Post-churn economics, offboarding cost model, logo winback analytics, `enterprise_churn_events` DDL summary (§43.8), DEC-030 Zod v2 schemas (§43.7), OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01/WINBACK-CHAIN-01/DELETION-CHAIN-01 chain invariants, DEL-E-001/WIN-E-001/CHN-E-001 evidence artefact definitions (§43.9), implementation checklist §43.10 |
| **Canonical DDL registration** | `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §44` | v1.26 | 2026-06-23 | Canonical Postgres DDL (§44.3.1), three ENUMs, 22-column table, three CHECK constraints, three indexes, RLS policies (REVOKE form_api + no tenant_manager policy), six-item staging validation checklist (§44.3.2), OFFBOARD/WINBACK/DELETION-CHAIN-01 DDL relationships (§44.6), SOC 2 evidence cross-references (§44.9) |
| **SOC 2 cross-reference closure** | `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §101` | v3.26.0 | 2026-06-23 | This section — bidirectional cross-reference registration, canonical DDL source update for DEL-E-001/WIN-E-001/CHN-E-001, DDL-layer invariant auditor narratives, implementation checklist |
| **DEC-030 event registration** | `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise` | v2.39 · 2026-06-23 | 🟢 Done — COST_MODEL §43.10 item 1 | Five DEC-030 events registered: `enterprise.winback_initiated` (HIGH, 7yr), `enterprise.winback_converted` (CRITICAL, 7yr), `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` (CRITICAL, 7yr), `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` (HIGH, 7yr), `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` (CRITICAL, 7yr); OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01/WINBACK-CHAIN-01/DELETION-CHAIN-01 invariant blocks with HTTP 422 enforcement; Zod v2 schemas; DEL-E-001/WBK-E-001/CHN-E-001 artefact table. v2.40 amended WinbackConvertedPayload with `prior_tenant_id` (DEC-079 Option B). See §123. |

**Sequencing note:** Unlike §100 where DATA_MODEL §43 and SOC2_READINESS §100 were created simultaneously, §101 is authored retroactively after DATA_MODEL §44 was written (2026-06-23). This matches the §84–§99 pattern of retroactive cross-reference closure. The DEC-030 event registration in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md was completed 2026-06-23 (v2.39 — five post-churn events) and further amended 2026-06-23 (v2.40 — DEC-079 Option B `prior_tenant_id`). See §123 for full retroactive closure record.

---

### §101.2 Canonical DDL Source Registration

DATA_MODEL §44 is the **canonical DDL source** for `enterprise_churn_events` and its associated ENUMs (`churn_trigger_enum`, `churn_reason_enum`, `winback_status_enum`), indexes, and RLS policies. COST_MODEL §43 is the **authoritative economic spec** — it defines the business rationale for each column, the chain invariants, and the evidence artefact definitions. DATA_MODEL §44 is the single-source-of-truth for the Postgres DDL artefact.

| Construct | DATA_MODEL §44 location | SOC 2 relevance |
|-----------|------------------------|----------------|
| `CREATE TYPE churn_trigger_enum` | §44.3.1 | Enables `churn_trigger`-based audit queries for CHN-E-001; auditor can enumerate valid values via `SELECT enum_range(NULL::churn_trigger_enum)`. Four values: `contract_non_renewal`, `mutual_termination`, `for_cause_termination`, `bankruptcy` |
| `CREATE TYPE churn_reason_enum` | §44.3.1 | Enables winback probability segmentation in WIN-E-001 annual report (cross-tabbed against `wau_band_at_churn` per COST_MODEL §43.5.1). Six values: `low_utilization`, `champion_left`, `budget`, `competitive_loss`, `product_gap`, `unknown` |
| `CREATE TYPE winback_status_enum` | §44.3.1 | Seven terminal/non-terminal states; `idx_ece_winback_status` partial index excludes terminal states (`converted`, `abandoned`) to bound the active winback pipeline query surface to ~20% of rows at fleet scale |
| `CONSTRAINT ece_deletion_sla_respected CHECK (deletion_completed_at - deletion_requested_at <= INTERVAL '35 days')` | §44.3.1 | **Primary DDL-layer control for C1.2** — GDPR Art. 17 SLA backstop. Any INSERT or UPDATE that attempts to record a deletion beyond the 35-day window (30-day Art. 17 mandate + 5-day compliance-officer buffer) is rejected at the DDL layer with a CHECK violation. Independent of the `emit-audit-event` Worker and pg_cron job 43 (`deletion_sla_monitor`) — a two-control-layer architecture visible to the auditor via `\d enterprise_churn_events` |
| `CONSTRAINT ece_winback_acv_requires_converted` | §44.3.1 | **DDL-layer backstop for CC5.2** — ensures no conversion ACV is recorded without `winback_status = 'converted'`. WIN-E-001 annual winback programme revenue line cannot be inflated by recording ACV against non-converted outreach rows. Any attempt to INSERT a row with `winback_acv_usd IS NOT NULL AND winback_status != 'converted'` returns a CHECK violation before any DEC-030 event is emitted |
| `CONSTRAINT ece_offboarding_before_deletion` | §44.3.1 | **DDL-layer coherence for CC6.1** — enforces that `offboarding_initiated_at < deletion_requested_at` when both are non-NULL. Prevents recording an offboarding that occurred after a deletion was requested, which would indicate a process control failure in the deprovisioning sequence |
| `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_ece_tenant_churn_date (tenant_id, churn_date)` | §44.3.1 | Performance guarantee and completeness control for CHN-E-001 quarterly OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 compliance query. Prevents duplicate churn event records — a UNIQUE violation surfaces a process deficiency in the Admin Console "Mark Churn Confirmed" workflow before any DEC-030 event is emitted. Each UNIQUE violation is a visible error, not a silent duplicate |
| `CREATE INDEX idx_ece_churn_date ON enterprise_churn_events(churn_date DESC)` | §44.3.1 | Performance guarantee for WIN-E-001 annual winback programme report (reverse-chronological iteration); guarantees O(log n) range scans as fleet grows to Year 3+ scale |
| `CREATE INDEX idx_ece_winback_status` (partial, non-terminal states only) | §44.3.1 | Active winback pipeline query efficiency — the Admin Console "Winback Pipeline" UI and WIN-E-001 aggregate cross-tab require sub-100ms queries at Year 3 fleet scale (~500 churned tenants, ~100 in active pipeline). Partial index covers only ~20% of rows |
| `REVOKE ALL ON enterprise_churn_events FROM form_api` | §44.5 | CC6.1 access control — churn and offboarding data excluded from tenant-facing API. `form_api` is the Supabase role serving the consumer-facing product API; no tenant endpoint should read or write churn records. Confirmed via `has_table_privilege('form_api', 'enterprise_churn_events', 'SELECT') = false` |
| `fehs_at_churn NUMERIC(4,2) NULLABLE` | §44.7 | FEHS (Fleet Engagement Health Score) at time of churn — sourced from `tenant_engagement_snapshots` within the same SERIALIZABLE transaction as `enterprise.account_churned` DEC-030 emission. k ≥ 10 floor enforced at snapshot layer (COST_MODEL §33.7); NULL written for tenants below the floor. Scalar aggregate, never individual employee data |
| `offboarding_initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE` | §44.4 | OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 elapsed-time anchor — enables CHN-E-001 compliance query without joining to the full `audit_log_events` table. Set within the same SERIALIZABLE transaction as `enterprise.offboarding_initiated` DEC-030 emission (SSO teardown + SCIM deprovisioning trigger). Column-level evidence of deprovisioning timeline at fleet scale |

---

### §101.3 Privacy Floor Supplements

**Supplement 1 — `enterprise_churn_events` is exclusively FORM-internal operational data.**

Unlike `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` (§41, which grants `tenant_admin` and `tenant_owner` read access to their own aggregate health metrics), `enterprise_churn_events` contains commercially and legally sensitive post-exit data: churn reasons, ACV figures, deletion SLA timelines, and winback economics. No `tenant_admin`, `tenant_owner`, or `tenant_manager` role has any SELECT grant on `enterprise_churn_events`. The table is invisible to the former customer's admin users — visible only to `form_admin` (FORM internal: compliance-officer, CSM) and `compliance_reviewer` (SOC 2 auditor). **No exception to this rule exists.** Any request to add tenant-facing access must be reviewed by enterprise-architect + compliance-officer before any DDL change.

**Supplement 2 — `tenant_manager` (HR/People-ops) exclusion confirmed.**

`tenant_manager` must never see individual employee wellness data (§16.4 privacy floor). By extension, it must also never see post-churn offboarding timelines, churn reasons, or winback pipeline data — these are commercial terms and legal compliance records that HR personnel have no legitimate workflow need to access. The absence of any `tenant_manager` RLS policy on `enterprise_churn_events` is confirmed by the DDL auditor proof query defined in §44.5:

```sql
SELECT count(*) FROM pg_policies
 WHERE tablename = 'enterprise_churn_events'
   AND roles @> ARRAY['tenant_manager'];
-- Expected: 0
```

**Supplement 3 — `fehs_at_churn` is a tenant-aggregate score, not individual employee data.**

`fehs_at_churn` sources from `tenant_engagement_snapshots`, which enforces a k ≥ 10 anonymity floor at the snapshot-generation layer (COST_MODEL §33.7). A score in `fehs_at_churn` represents the engagement health of at minimum 10 users — it is never attributable to any individual employee. Tenants with fewer than 10 active users receive `NULL`; the NULL case is documented in CHN-E-001 quarterly reports. The column contains no `user_id`, no employee name, no email, no health metric, and no GDPR Art. 9 special category data.

**Supplement 4 — Offboarding timeline fields are FORM-process-operational, not employee-level provisioning records.**

`offboarding_initiated_at`, `deletion_requested_at`, and `deletion_completed_at` record when FORM's own processes ran — not when individual employees were removed from the IdP. The actual user-level provisioning change is executed by the IdP (Okta/Entra ID/Google Workspace) in response to SCIM deprovisioning; that IdP-level action is not recorded in this table. The HR privacy floor (§16.4 — HR must never see individual user data) is structurally enforced by the `tenant_manager` no-policy design.

---

### §101.4 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping Supplement

The rows below supplement the §79.4 criteria mapping for DEL-E-001/WIN-E-001/CHN-E-001 with DDL-layer evidence from DATA_MODEL §44. §79.4 artefact definitions, collection SQL, and cadence specifications remain in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.9` (the authoritative source); this section adds the DDL invariant layer not yet captured at §43.9 authoring time.

| Criterion | COST_MODEL §43.9 coverage | §101 DDL supplement from DATA_MODEL §44 |
|-----------|--------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| **C1.2** — Disposal of Confidential Information (DEL-E-001 primary criterion) | DEL-E-001 annual deletion certificate archive proves every GDPR Art. 17 deletion completed within 30 days; `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` CRITICAL/7yr DEC-030 event is the primary HMAC chain evidence; pg_cron job 43 monitors SLA at day 25 (P1) and day 29 (P0) | `ece_deletion_sla_respected CHECK (deletion_completed_at - deletion_requested_at <= INTERVAL '35 days')` is a DDL-layer structural control independent of both the Worker and pg_cron job 43. An auditor verifies C1.2 compliance with a single query: `SELECT * FROM enterprise_churn_events WHERE deletion_completed_at IS NOT NULL` — every returned row is structurally constrained to sub-35-day completion by the CHECK. This creates two independent evidence paths for C1.2: (1) DEC-030 `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` CRITICAL/7yr events (application-layer); (2) `ece_deletion_sla_respected` CHECK constraint (DDL-layer). A C1.2 finding requires both controls to fail simultaneously. |
| **CC6.1** — Logical Access Controls — Deprovisioning (CHN-E-001 primary criterion) | CHN-E-001 quarterly OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 compliance report proves SSO teardown and SCIM deprovisioning initiated within 24 hours of churn confirmation for every churned tenant; `enterprise.offboarding_initiated` DEC-030 event anchors the chain | `UNIQUE INDEX idx_ece_tenant_churn_date (tenant_id, churn_date)` ensures the CHN-E-001 quarterly export has exactly one row per churned tenant — no gaps (missed deprovisioning events) and no duplicates (double-counted records). `ece_offboarding_before_deletion CHECK` structurally prevents recording a deletion that preceded offboarding — a process sequencing failure that would otherwise be invisible to the CHN-E-001 annual query. `REVOKE ALL FROM form_api` ensures no tenant-facing endpoint can create or alter churn records; all writes require the Admin Console workflow with PAM elevation for sensitive operations. |
| **CC4.1** — Ongoing Evaluations (DEL-E-001 secondary + CHN-E-001 secondary criterion) | DEL-E-001 and CHN-E-001 both require consistent cadenced collection (annual and quarterly respectively) — collection must remain operationally reliable as the enterprise fleet grows | `idx_ece_tenant_churn_date` and `idx_ece_churn_date` guarantee DEL-E-001 and CHN-E-001 collection queries execute at O(log n) index-scan speed regardless of fleet size (confirmed by EXPLAIN ANALYZE at 500-row fleet in §44.8). Without these indexes, evidence collection degrades to sequential scans at Year 3+ fleet scale — evidence collection that exceeds the allocated window is not practically "ongoing." |
| **CC5.2** — Control Activities — Commitment and Accountability (WIN-E-001 primary criterion) | WIN-E-001 annual winback programme aggregate report proves FORM's outreach controls operate on the documented WINBACK-CHAIN-01 prerequisite chain (no conversion without prior outreach); `enterprise.winback_converted` CRITICAL/7yr DEC-030 event is the HMAC chain evidence | `ece_winback_acv_requires_converted CHECK (winback_acv_usd IS NULL OR winback_status = 'converted')` is the DDL-layer enforcement of WINBACK-CHAIN-01. Even if the `emit-audit-event` Worker's Zod guard fails, an INSERT with `winback_acv_usd IS NOT NULL AND winback_status != 'converted'` is rejected at the DDL layer with a CHECK violation — a visible HTTP 500 from the Admin Console workflow, not a silent data integrity failure. WIN-E-001 revenue figures sourced from this table can be audited directly from the CHECK semantics: every non-null `winback_acv_usd` row is structurally guaranteed to have `winback_status = 'converted'`. |
| **CC7.1** — System Operations — Monitoring (CHN-E-001 secondary criterion) | CHN-E-001 quarterly report covers pg_cron job 43 (`deletion_sla_monitor`) health evidence — the monitoring control that fires P1 at day 25 and P0 at day 29 ahead of the 35-day DDL backstop | `offboarding_initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ` enables the CHN-E-001 elapsed-time compliance query (§44.6 OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 query) at O(log n) speed via `idx_ece_tenant_churn_date` without joining to `audit_log_events` (which stores the full DEC-030 chain and grows unboundedly). The quarterly CHN-E-001 compliance check runs directly against `enterprise_churn_events` — a table bounded to one row per churned tenant — making the monitoring control operationally lightweight and reliably executable within the quarterly filing window at fleet scale. |

C1.2 finding requires both to fail), `ece_winback_acv_requires_converted CHECK` (CC5.2 DDL backstop — WIN-E-001 revenue line structural integrity; visible HTTP 500 on bypass attempt), `ece_offboarding_before_deletion CHECK` (CC6.1 process sequencing coherence — surfaces deprovisioning-after-deletion failures), `UNIQUE INDEX idx_ece_tenant_churn_date` (CC6.1 completeness — no gaps or duplicates in CHN-E-001 export), `INDEX idx_ece_churn_date DESC` (CC4.1/CC5.2 WIN-E-001 performance guarantee — O(log n) at fleet scale), `INDEX idx_ece_winback_status partial` (CC5.2 active pipeline query efficiency — 20% row coverage bound), `REVOKE ALL FROM form_api` (CC6.1 access control — churn data excluded from consumer API), `fehs_at_churn` (tenant-aggregate k≥10 sourced from tenant_engagement_snapshots §33.7; NULL for below-floor tenants), `offboarding_initiated_at` (CC7.1 OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 elapsed-time anchor; CHN-E-001 lightweight compliance query against bounded table). §101.3 four privacy floor supplements: (1) FORM-internal only — no tenant_admin/tenant_owner/tenant_manager SELECT, zero exceptions; ## §102 · Evidence Table Patch — DEL-E-001 / WBK-E-001 / CHN-E-001 Registration (COST_MODEL §43.9 · Post-Churn Lifecycle Evidence · C1.2 / CC4.1 / CC5.2 / CC6.1 / CC7.1)

`docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.9` (v1.0, 2026-06-23) defined three SOC 2 evidence artefacts as part of the enterprise post-churn economics model: **DEL-E-001** (C1.2/CC4.1 — annual deletion certificate archive), **WIN-E-001** (CC4.1/CC5.2 — annual winback programme report, aliased in source doc as WIN-E-001), and **CHN-E-001** (CC6.1/CC7.1 — quarterly OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 deprovisioning compliance report). These were flagged in COST_MODEL §43.10 checklist item 5 (P1/M11) as requiring registration in §79.4, with accompanying R2 subfolder additions (§80.3) and Vanta mirror list update (§80.4). §102 delivers that registration and resolves the **WIN-E-001 ID collision** identified during registration (§102.2).

### §102.1 Three-Artefact Source Chain

| Artefact | Source doc | Defined in | Canonical §79.4 ID | Storage path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Annual deletion certificate archive | `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.9` | v1.0, 2026-06-23 | **DEL-E-001** | `compliance/evidence/deletions/DEL-E-001_<YYYY>.zip` |
| Annual winback programme aggregate report | `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.9` (source alias: WIN-E-001) | v1.0, 2026-06-23 | **WBK-E-001** (§102.2) | `compliance/evidence/winback/WBK-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |
| Quarterly OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 compliance report | `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.9` | v1.0, 2026-06-23 | **CHN-E-001** | `compliance/evidence/offboarding/CHN-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |

DDL-layer evidence for all three artefacts: `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §44.9` (v1.26, 2026-06-23 — auditor narratives for C1.2/CC6.1/CC5.2 + EXPLAIN ANALYZE performance guarantees for evidence collection queries at 500-row fleet scale).

---

### §102.2 WIN-E-001 → WBK-E-001 ID Disambiguation

**Problem:** COST_MODEL §43.9 (2026-06-23) named the winback programme report "WIN-E-001". The §79.4 master evidence table already contains a row for "WIN-E-001" registered in §87.2 (2026-06-17): annual `enterprise.deal_closed_won` DEC-030 export with `floor_respected: true` attestation, sourced from COST_MODEL §39.9. Both artefacts use the WIN prefix but refer to entirely different evidence:

| ID | Source | Content | Storage path |
|---|---|---|---|
| **WIN-E-001** (existing §87.2) | COST_MODEL §39.9 — deal close governance | Annual export of `enterprise.deal_closed_won` DEC-030 events with `floor_respected: true` attestation | `compliance/evidence/winloss/WIN-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |
| **WIN-E-001** (COST_MODEL §43.9 alias) | COST_MODEL §43.9 — post-churn winback model | Annual `enterprise_churn_events` aggregate: outreach attempts × churn_reason × wau_band × winback_converted count | `compliance/evidence/winback/WIN-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |

Registering both under "WIN-E-001" in §79.4 would create a duplicate primary key in the master evidence registry — a data integrity failure that would cause fieldwork confusion when the auditor queries MASTER-INDEX-YYYY.csv by artefact ID.

**Resolution:** The churn winback artefact is registered in §79.4 as **WBK-E-001** (Winback Evidence 001). The alias relationship is documented in the §79.4 row and in COST_MODEL §43.10 item 5 checklist closure. COST_MODEL §43.9, DATA_MODEL §44.9, and SOC2_READINESS §101 continue to use "WIN-E-001" as the shorthand when referencing the winback artefact within their own section context. The §79.4 WBK-E-001 row carries a disambiguation note. No changes are required in those sections — the alias is a §79.4 registry-level resolution only.

**Auditor note:** When requesting this artefact in fieldwork, use the §79.4 canonical ID "WBK-E-001" and MASTER-INDEX path `compliance/evidence/winback/WBK-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`. Cross-references in COST_MODEL §43.9 and DATA_MODEL §44.9 that say "WIN-E-001" refer to this same artefact.

---

### §102.3 §79.4 Evidence Table Additions

Three rows added after REN-OBS-E-001 in the §79.4 master evidence collection table (v3.27.0 edit):

| ID | Domain | Criteria | Description | Cadence | Retention | Owner | Path |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **DEL-E-001** | C1/CC4 | C1.2 / CC4.1 | Annual deletion certificate archive confirming every GDPR Art. 17 deletion completed within 30 days and every standard post-churn deletion on schedule. DDL backstop: `ece_deletion_sla_respected CHECK` (DATA_MODEL §44.3.1). Source: COST_MODEL §43.9. | Annual | 7 yr | compliance-officer | `deletions/` |
| **WBK-E-001** | CC4/CC5 | CC4.1 / CC5.2 | Annual winback programme aggregate report (cross-tab `churn_reason` × `wau_band_at_churn`; outreach attempts; converted count; ACV premium). Fleet-level only. **§79.4 canonical ID for COST_MODEL §43.9 "WIN-E-001" — §102.2 disambiguation.** Source: COST_MODEL §43.9. | Annual | 3 yr | data-engineer + compliance-officer | `winback/` |
| **CHN-E-001** | CC6/CC7 | CC6.1 / CC7.1 | Quarterly OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 compliance report: elapsed time `account_churned` → `offboarding_initiated` per churned tenant; flag > 24 h breaches. Zero-breach quarters are positive evidence. Source: COST_MODEL §43.9. | Quarterly | 3 yr | compliance-officer | `offboarding/` |

**Privacy floor (all three artefacts):** No individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health values, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special category data in any exported row. DEL-E-001 identifies tenants by `tenant_id` UUID (FORM-internal). WBK-E-001 is a fleet aggregate — no tenant names. CHN-E-001 uses `tenant_id` UUID and elapsed-time integer only. `form_api` Supabase role has NO ACCESS to `compliance/evidence/deletions/`, `compliance/evidence/winback/`, `compliance/evidence/offboarding/` (R2 invariant per §80.3 addition below).

**C1.2 auditor narrative (DEL-E-001):** C1.2 requires FORM to dispose of confidential information per contractual data protection obligations. DEL-E-001 demonstrates that every GDPR Art. 17 deletion request completed within 30 days, supported by two independent evidence layers: (1) `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` CRITICAL/7yr DEC-030 HMAC-chained event (application-layer); (2) `ece_deletion_sla_respected CHECK (deletion_completed_at - deletion_requested_at <= INTERVAL '35 days')` DDL backstop (DATA_MODEL §44.3.1). A C1.2 audit finding requires both layers to fail simultaneously.

**CC5.2 auditor narrative (WBK-E-001):** CC5.2 requires FORM to enforce commitments and accountabilities. WINBACK-CHAIN-01 enforces that no winback conversion is recorded without a prior CSM-authorised outreach event. WBK-E-001 demonstrates that the commercial recovery programme operates under chain-enforced accountability — the `ece_winback_acv_requires_converted CHECK` ensures no winback ACV is recognised without a `winback_status = 'converted'` record, making revenue line integrity verifiable directly from DDL semantics.

**CC6.1 auditor narrative (CHN-E-001):** CC6.1 requires FORM to implement logical access controls including deprovisioning when principals no longer require access. CHN-E-001 proves that SSO teardown and SCIM deprovisioning were initiated within 24 hours of churn confirmation for every churned tenant. The `UNIQUE INDEX idx_ece_tenant_churn_date` ensures the quarterly export has exactly one row per churned tenant — no gaps (missed deprovisioning events) and no duplicates.

---

### §102.4 §80.3 R2 Folder Additions

Three folders added to the §80.3 `compliance/evidence/` folder structure (after the existing `renewals/` entry):

```
  deletions/     (GDPR Art. 17 + standard post-churn deletion certificates — DEL-E-001;
                  migration-0085-validation receipts; form-api NO ACCESS)
  winback/       (enterprise logo winback programme reports — WBK-E-001
                  [§79.4 canonical; COST_MODEL §43.9 alias WIN-E-001]; form-api NO ACCESS)
  offboarding/   (enterprise deprovisioning chain compliance — CHN-E-001;
                  OFB-E-005 [EU tenant data-export DEC-030 chain, DATA_MODEL §36.7];
                  OFB-E-006 [EU R2 jurisdiction config screenshot, DATA_MODEL §36.7];
                  form-api NO ACCESS)
```

**Note on `offboarding/`:** OFB-E-005 and OFB-E-006 (registered in §67.8, v3.9.1, 2026-06-15) both reference `compliance/evidence/offboarding/` as their storage path but the subfolder was not formally added to §80.3 at that time. §102 retroactively adds it. The `offboarding/` folder holds both the EU-egress DEC-030 chain exports (OFB-E-005/006) and the OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 deprovisioning compliance reports (CHN-E-001). These are distinct evidence families stored in the same folder: CHN-E-001 covers deprovisioning timing (CC6.1); OFB-E-005/006 cover EU data residency routing (C1.1/P4.0). Both are `form-api NO ACCESS`.

**Bucket policy invariant (all three new folders):** `r2:form-api` has ZERO access to `deletions/`, `winback/`, and `offboarding/`. Access matrix:
- `r2:compliance-officer` — read + write (evidence filing and retrieval)
- `r2:vanta-sync` — read-only (Vanta mirror upload path)
- `r2:devops-lead` — read-only (infrastructure verification)
- `r2:form-api` — **NO ACCESS** (tenant-facing API must never read or write evidence artefacts)

---

### §102.5 §80.4 Vanta Mirror List Addition

Five artefacts added to the §80.4 Vanta mirror list (v3.27.0 edit, after the existing TURH-CRON-E series):

```
DEL-E-001 (COST_MODEL §43.9 — annual deletion certificate archive;
  upload annually from first churn event)
WBK-E-001 (COST_MODEL §43.9 alias WIN-E-001 — annual winback programme report;
  upload annually from first winback outreach, est. M24)
CHN-E-001 (COST_MODEL §43.9 — quarterly OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 compliance report;
  upload quarterly from first churn event)
OFB-E-005 (DATA_MODEL §36.7 — quarterly EU tenant data_export_completed DEC-030 chain;
  upload quarterly from M11; zero-event quarters filed as affirmative attestation)
OFB-E-006 (DATA_MODEL §36.7 — annual EU R2 jurisdiction config screenshot + IAM export;
  upload annually from M7 EU R2 provisioning)
```

**Vanta upload cadence:** DEL-E-001 annual (January following observation year); WBK-E-001 annual (January following first winback outreach year); CHN-E-001 quarterly (on each Q1–Q4 filing); OFB-E-005 quarterly; OFB-E-006 annual.

### §102.7 Implementation Checklist

#### P1 — Before SOC 2 observation period (M11)

| # | Task | Owner | Priority | Milestone | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## §103 · Evidence Table Patch — ENGAGE-E-001/002/003 / SSO-OBS-E-005/006 / WS-E-001/002/003 / CONC-E-001 Registration (SOC2_READINESS §84 · C1.1 / C1.2 / CC2.2 / CC6.1 / CC7.2 / CC4.1 / CC7.4 / A1.1 / P3.2)

> Closes `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §84.8 item 10` (P1/M14 — "Add §84.6 criterion rows to §79.4 master evidence table; ensure ENGAGE-E-001/002/003, SSO-OBS-E-005/006, WS-E-001/002/003, CONC-E-001 appear in Vanta mirror list alongside existing artefacts." — 🟢 Closed). Simultaneously closes `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §84.8 item 9` (P1/M7 — documentation part: `engagement/`, `wearable/`, `ir-chain/` R2 subfolders added to §80.3) and `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §92.6 item 1` (P1/M11 — `sso/` subfolder formally added to §80.3 code block). Nine artefacts were defined in §84 (2026-06-16, v3.10.0) across four source documents but were never promoted to §79.4 rows. Privacy floor: no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health values, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special category data in any of the nine artefacts or their R2 paths; all involve aggregate metrics, alert configuration exports, or chain event metadata sourced from FORM-internal pg_cron and DEC-030 infrastructure; k-anonymity gate at emission for ENGAGE-E-001 (negative-privilege test, not user-data export) and WS-E-002 (pg_cron job 31 suppresses per-source breakdown when N < 5).

| ENGAGE-E-003 | SOC2_READINESS §84.2 (OBSERVABILITY §33.11) | CC7.2 / A1.1 | Annual alert history + quarterly DEC-030 from M13 | 3 yr | `engagement/` |
| SSO-OBS-E-005 | SOC2_READINESS §84.3 (OBSERVABILITY §26.7a) | A1.1 / CC7.2 | Quarterly from M5 | 3 yr | `sso/` |
| SSO-OBS-E-006 | SOC2_READINESS §84.3 (OBSERVABILITY §26.7b) | CC7.2 / CC6.1 | Quarterly from M5 | 3 yr | `sso/` |

### §103.2 §79.4 Evidence Table Additions

Nine rows added after WL-CERT-E-001 in the §79.4 master evidence table:

- **ENGAGE-E-001** (C1/C1 — C1.1/C1.2): `tenant_engagement_snapshots` DDL export + RLS policy list + negative-privilege test log (HR-never-sees-individual invariant); pg_cron 36-month cleanup schedule confirming C1.2 disposal. Annual, 3yr, `engagement/`. Privacy floor: RLS test is negative-privilege, not user-data export — zero `user_id` or employee data.
- **ENGAGE-E-002** (CC2/CC4 — CC2.2/CC4.1): anonymised QBR package + `tenant.qbr_report_generated` DEC-030 content-policy payload; tenant_id redacted. Quarterly, 3yr, `engagement/`. Privacy floor: no individual `user_id` or CHS score.
- **ENGAGE-E-003** (CC7/A — CC7.2/A1.1): AL-ENGAGE-01..06 PagerDuty alert history + `tenant.churn_risk_flagged` DEC-030 export; AL-ENGAGE-01/02 are A1.1 capacity leading indicators. Annual/quarterly, 3yr, `engagement/`. Privacy floor: tenant slug + aggregate metrics only.
- **SSO-OBS-E-005** (A1/CC7 — A1.1/CC7.2): AL-SCIM-MASS-01 PagerDuty log; zero-count is positive non-occurrence evidence. Quarterly, 3yr, `sso/`. Privacy floor: tenant slug + aggregate `deprovisioned_count` only.
- **SSO-OBS-E-006** (CC7/CC6 — CC7.2/CC6.1): AL-SCIM-01..04 PagerDuty log; AL-SCIM-04 zero-count = SCIM-CHAIN-01 ordering integrity. Quarterly, 3yr, `sso/`. Privacy floor: no `user_id` or employee name.
- **WS-E-001** (A1 — A1.1): quarterly `wearable.sync_completed` DEC-030 export per source; Garmin excluded pending OQ-WS-OBS-01. 5yr, `wearable/`. Privacy floor: aggregate `reading_count` per source per quarter only.
- **WS-E-002** (P3 — P3.2): quarterly `wearable.fleet_freshness_assessed` DEC-030 events with k-anonymity gate (per-source suppressed if N < 5). 5yr, `wearable/`. Privacy floor: `fresh_pct` + `slo_met` aggregate only.
- **WS-E-003** (CC7 — CC7.2): AL-WS-01..07 alert rule configuration screenshots; annual or on rule change. 3yr, `wearable/`. Privacy floor: alert configuration only.
- **CONC-E-001** (CC7/CC4 — CC7.4/CC4.1): §18.3.1 per-incident skip-and-verify SQL + §18.3.3 CONC-CHAIN-01 zero-row check; filed on zero-concurrent-incident quarters as zero-event attestation. Quarterly, 7yr, `ir-chain/`. Privacy floor: `incident_id` (FORM-internal INC format only) — no `user_id`, no employee name, no Art. 9 data.

### §103.3 §80.3 R2 Folder Additions

Four R2 subfolders added to §80.3 code block (after `webhooks/`, before `pre-obs/`):

- **`sso/`** — SSO/SCIM observability artefacts (SSO-OBS-E-005/006/007); `form-api NO ACCESS`. Formalises the path noted in §92.5 (v3.17.0, 2026-06-19) but not yet in the §80.3 code block. Closes §92.6 item 1 documentation obligation.
- **`engagement/`** — enterprise tenant engagement health artefacts (ENGAGE-E-001/002/003); `form-api NO ACCESS`. Closes §84.8 item 9 documentation obligation.
- **`wearable/`** — wearable integration observability artefacts (WS-E-001/002/003); `form-api NO ACCESS`. Closes §84.8 item 9 documentation obligation.
- **`ir-chain/`** — concurrent incident chain evidence (CONC-E-001); `form-api NO ACCESS`. Closes §84.8 item 9 documentation obligation.

Nine artefacts added after "Added by §102 (2026-06-23)" line in §80.4 Vanta mirror list:

## §104 · Cross-Reference Patch — INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-41 (GDPR Deletion SLA Monitor Stale · `deletion_sla_monitor` job 43 · DEL-MON-E-001 CC7.2 Extension · C1.2 / CC4.1 / CC7.2)

> Closes the CC7.2 criterion gap in the DEL-MON-E-001 evidence artefact registration (§79.4, v8.45.0, 2026-06-25). When DEL-MON-E-001 was registered in §79.4 it was mapped to C1.2/CC4.1 only. `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-41.9` (v1.0, 2026-06-25) establishes that R-41 activations also satisfy **CC7.2** (monitoring of system components — `pg-cron-health-monitor` §12.7 detected the pg_cron staleness event; R-41 defines the IC response protocol). This patch registers the CC7.2 mapping in SOC2_READINESS, extends the DEL-MON-E-001 §79.4 row to C1.2/CC4.1/CC7.2, and provides auditor narratives for all three criteria. Privacy floor: no `tenant_id`, no employee PII, no Art. 9 health data in any DEL-MON-E-001 column — aggregate counts and booleans only; `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_churn_events`. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.

### §104.1 Source

| Source | Section | Criterion added |
|---|---|---|
| `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-41` | GDPR Deletion SLA Monitor Stale runbook | **CC7.2** (monitoring of system components — `pg-cron-health-monitor` §12.7 detection of job 43 staleness + DEC-030 detection-to-restoration timeline) |

DEL-MON-E-001 was defined in `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-41.8` (Evidence Preservation) and registered in §79.4 with C1.2/CC4.1 only. `§R-41.9` (SOC 2 Criteria Mapping) maps the same runbook to CC7.2 via the `pg-cron-health-monitor` detective control: that layer emits `system.cron_job_stale` and routes PagerDuty P1 when job 43 exceeds its 7h freshness window, independent of the C1.2/CC4.1 confidential-disposal and compliance-monitoring evidence roles. The CC7.2 evidence role — tamper-evident DEC-030 detection-to-restoration timeline — was not carried into §79.4 at initial registration and is closed here.

---

### §104.2 DEL-MON-E-001 — Full Criterion Registration

Source of truth for artefact definition: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-41.8` + `§R-41.9`.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Artefact ID** | DEL-MON-E-001 |
| **SOC 2 Criteria** | C1.2 (confidential disposal — GDPR Art. 17 SLA monitoring sentinel continuity); CC4.1 (compliance monitoring — R-41 detect/respond protocol); CC7.2 (monitoring of system components — `pg-cron-health-monitor` §12.7 + DEC-030 detection-to-restoration timeline) |
| **Description** | Quarterly R-41 activation aggregate report: one CSV row per observation quarter recording `activation_count` (R-41 incidents declared), `total_stale_window_hours` across all activations, `danger_window_tenants_affected` (aggregate count of tenants in the 25–35-day Art. 17 danger window across all activations), and `art17_sla_breached` boolean. **Zero-activation quarters filed as affirmative attestation** that job 43 (`deletion_sla_monitor`, `0 */6 * * *`, 7h freshness window) ran continuously. DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering cross-check embedded in collection: every `system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` event must have a matching `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` for the same `incident_id`; unmatched pairs are open incidents. |
| **Collection method** | `SELECT DATE_TRUNC('quarter', created_at) AS quarter, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE event_type = 'system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared') AS activation_count, SUM((payload->>'stale_window_hours')::numeric) FILTER (WHERE event_type = 'system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored') AS total_stale_window_hours, SUM((payload->>'danger_window_tenants_affected')::int) FILTER (WHERE event_type = 'system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored') AS danger_window_tenants_affected, BOOL_OR((payload->>'art17_sla_breached')::bool) FILTER (WHERE event_type = 'system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored') AS art17_sla_breached FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type IN ('system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared', 'system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored') AND created_at BETWEEN $quarter_start AND $quarter_end GROUP BY 1`. File as `del-mon-e-001-<YYYY-QN>.csv`. |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly from first complete quarter with job 43 in production (est. M11) |
| **Retention** | 7 years |
| **Storage path** | `compliance/evidence/deletions/deletion-sla-stale/del-mon-e-001-<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| **R2 bucket** | `form-soc2-evidence/deletions/` (existing folder — DEL-E-001 also uses this path; `form-api` NO ACCESS per §80.3) |
| **Privacy floor** | `danger_window_tenants_affected` is an integer aggregate only — no `tenant_id`, no `user_id`, no employee name, no Art. 9 health data. `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_churn_events` (DATA_MODEL §44); report generated by `form_system` (pg_cron service role) only. |
| **Status** | 🟡 Authored — pending M11 job 43 production deploy (first quarterly filing est. Q4 2026) |

**Auditor narrative (C1.2):** C1.2 requires that FORM disposes of confidential information in accordance with stated obligations. FORM's GDPR Art. 17 commitment is a 30-day deletion window from termination notice (ENTERPRISE_SLA §5.2; DATA_MODEL §44 `ece_deletion_sla_respected` CHECK at day 35 as DDL backstop). `deletion_sla_monitor` (job 43) is the monitoring sentinel that fires pre-breach alerts at day 25 (P1, `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` HIGH/7yr) and day 29 (P0, `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` CRITICAL/7yr). DEL-MON-E-001 provides the auditor with a quarterly aggregate record: zero activations confirm the sentinel ran without interruption; any activation row includes `art17_sla_breached: false` as auditor-inspectable attestation that FORM's IC detected the monitoring gap and confirmed no GDPR Art. 17 SLA was breached during the stale window. When `art17_sla_breached: true`, INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-09 is activated and the GDPR Art. 33 evidence chain begins from `confirmed_stale_since` in the `system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` event — not from restoration — preserving the correct clock start for supervisory authority notification.

**Auditor narrative (CC4.1):** CC4.1 requires evaluation of whether the controls implemented to meet commitments and system requirements are operating effectively. FORM's GDPR deletion SLA monitoring depends on `deletion_sla_monitor` (job 43) executing on its `0 */6 * * *` schedule and the `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.7) detecting any 7h+ gap. DEL-MON-E-001 is the quarterly performance-evaluation record for this monitoring control: each quarter's row certifies how many times the control failed (`activation_count`), how long each failure lasted (`total_stale_window_hours`), whether any tenant was in the danger window during failure (`danger_window_tenants_affected`), and whether the 30-day Art. 17 SLA was breached (`art17_sla_breached`). The DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering cross-check confirms that every declared failure had a corresponding restoration record — proving the IC responded to every monitoring gap within the observation period.

**Auditor narrative (CC7.2):** CC7.2 requires monitoring of infrastructure and software for anomalies that could affect availability or security. `pg-cron-health-monitor` (OBSERVABILITY §12.7) is the monitoring-of-monitoring layer: it tracks the freshness of every §12.6 pg_cron compliance job, including job 43. When job 43 exceeds its 7h freshness window, `pg-cron-health-monitor` emits `system.cron_job_stale` and routes PagerDuty P1 `form-enterprise` → compliance-officer. The R-41 runbook provides the IC with diagnostic queries (R-41-C1 through R-41-C4) to classify root cause (H1 unauthorized deletion, H2 shared scheduler failure, H3 permission revocation, H4 platform outage) and restore the sentinel. The DEC-030 HMAC chain (`system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr → `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` STANDARD/3yr, DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant) provides auditors with a tamper-evident timeline from anomaly detection to component restoration, independently verifiable per `compliance/docs/hmac-chain-verification-algorithm.md` (HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001). DEL-MON-E-001 demonstrates that FORM's monitoring-of-monitoring layer (pg-cron-health-monitor) detected job 43 anomalies and that each was resolved under R-41 protocol within documented SLAs.

---

| **C1.2** | Confidential disposal — GDPR Art. 17 deletion SLA monitoring sentinel continuity; `art17_sla_breached: false` is auditor-inspectable attestation of no 30-day breach during stale window | DEL-MON-E-001 (registered §79.4 v8.45.0; auditor narrative formalised in §104.2) |
| **CC4.1** | Compliance monitoring — quarterly activation aggregate report as performance evaluation of GDPR deletion SLA monitoring control; DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering cross-check | DEL-MON-E-001 (registered §79.4 v8.45.0; auditor narrative formalised in §104.2) |
| **CC7.2** | Monitoring of system components — `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.7) detection of job 43 gaps; DEC-030 HMAC-chained detection-to-restoration timeline; HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 independent verifiability | **DEL-MON-E-001** (CC7.2 added by §104; §79.4 row amended from `C1/CC4 \| C1.2/CC4.1` to `C1/CC4/CC7 \| C1.2/CC4.1/CC7.2`) |

Closes the CC7.2 criterion gap in the DEL-MON-E-001 §79.4 registration: when DEL-MON-E-001 was registered in §79.4 (v8.45.0, 2026-06-25) it was mapped to C1.2/CC4.1 only, omitting the CC7.2 (monitoring of system components) criterion established by `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-41.9` (v1.0, 2026-06-25). §104.1 source: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-41 (job 43 `deletion_sla_monitor` stale recovery runbook, v1.0 2026-06-25). §104.2 full DEL-MON-E-001 artefact table with all three criteria and auditor narratives: C1.2 (GDPR Art. 17 SLA monitoring sentinel continuity — `art17_sla_breached: false` is auditor-inspectable attestation of no 30-day breach; `art17_sla_breached: true` → R-09 activation; 72h Art. 33 clock from `confirmed_stale_since` in `stale_declared` event); CC4.1 (quarterly performance evaluation of GDPR deletion SLA monitoring control — `activation_count`, `total_stale_window_hours`, `danger_window_tenants_affected`, `art17_sla_breached` aggregate; DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering cross-check in collection SQL confirms IC responded to every gap); CC7.2 (monitoring of system components — `pg-cron-health-monitor` §12.7 detection of job 43 staleness; DEC-030 HMAC-chained `stale_declared` → `restored` timeline; HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 independent verifiability). Collection SQL: `SELECT DATE_TRUNC('quarter', created_at) AS quarter, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE event_type = 'system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared') AS activation_count, SUM(…) … FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type IN ('system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared', 'system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored') AND created_at BETWEEN $quarter_start AND $quarter_end GROUP BY 1`. §104.3 criteria mapping: C1.2 and CC4.1 (pre-existing registrations, auditor narratives formalised); CC7.2 (new addition by §104 — DEL-MON-E-001 extends to this criterion). §104.4 §79.4 row amendment: `C1/CC4` → `C1/CC4/CC7`; `C1.2 / CC4.1` → `C1.2 / CC4.1 / CC7.2`. §104.5 cross-reference obligations closed: CC7.2 criterion gap in DEL-MON-E-001 (🟢); R-41.11 item 6 (🟢). §104.6 one-item P1 checklist: M11 first quarterly DEL-MON-E-001 filing. Cross-references: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-41.9` (SOC 2 criteria mapping for R-41 — authoritative source of CC7.2 criterion); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-41.8` (DEL-MON-E-001 canonical artefact definition); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-41.7` (`system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr and `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` STANDARD/3yr; DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.7` (`pg-cron-health-monitor` — CC7.2 detective control); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 43 `deletion_sla_monitor` registry entry, `0 */6 * * *`, v1.5 patch 2026-06-25); `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise Post-Churn & Deletion SLA events` (`system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr, `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` STANDARD/3yr, DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01, v2.41 2026-06-25); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §44` (`enterprise_churn_events` — `ece_deletion_sla_respected` CHECK DDL backstop at 35d; `form_api` REVOKED); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` (DEL-MON-E-001 row amended by §104: `C1/CC4` → `C1/CC4/CC7`, `C1.2/CC4.1` → `C1.2/CC4.1/CC7.2`); `compliance/docs/hmac-chain-verification-algorithm.md` (HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 — referenced in CC7.2 auditor narrative for independent chain verification). ## §105 · Cross-Reference Patch — OBSERVABILITY §31 + §32 (API Key Auth Observability · Victor AI Safety Observability · APIKEY-E-001/002/003/004/005 + VSAFETY-E-001/002/003/004 · CC6.1 / CC6.2 / CC6.4 / CC6.8 / CC7.2 / CC7.3 / CC7.4 / A1.2)

### §105.1 Source Table

| Source section | Source document | Version | Gap identified |
|---|---|---|---|
| §31 API Key Authentication & Usage Observability | `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md` | v2.1 (2026-06-10) | APIKEY-E-001 through APIKEY-E-005 defined in §31.9 with explicit SOC2_READINESS cross-reference obligation (§31.10 item 12, P1/M6) — no corresponding §79.4 rows or cross-reference patch authored; series appeared in §82 MASTER-INDEX without source attribution |
| §32 Victor AI Safety Monitoring & Clinical-Safety Observability | `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md` | v2.2 (2026-06-10) | VSAFETY-E-001 through VSAFETY-E-004 defined in §32.9 with explicit SOC2_READINESS cross-reference obligation (§32.10 item 11, P1/M6) — no §79.4 rows, no cross-reference patch, completely absent from §82 MASTER-INDEX |

**Gap summary:** Both OBSERVABILITY §31 (2026-06-10) and §32 (2026-06-10) explicitly stated a cross-reference obligation to this document (`docs/SOC2_READINESS.md` CC6.x/CC7.x/A1.2 control evidence rows) that was never fulfilled in any §84–§104 patch. APIKEY-E was partially tracked in the §82 MASTER-INDEX supplemental list but without §79.4 rows or auditor narratives. VSAFETY-E had no presence in SOC2_READINESS at all. This patch closes both gaps.

### §105.2 APIKEY-E-001 through APIKEY-E-005 · Evidence Artefact Registration (OBSERVABILITY §31.9)

Full source specification: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §31.9`.

| Artefact ID | SOC 2 Criterion | Control evidence | Auditor narrative | Collection method | Retention |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **APIKEY-E-001** | **CC6.2** (Credentials appropriate for the authentication process) | Scope enforcement in `api-key-auth.ts` prevents `reporting:read` keys from calling admin mutation routes; HTTP 403 `insufficient_scope` on mismatch — scope validated before route handler execution | **CC6.2 auditor narrative:** CC6.2 requires that logical access credentials are appropriate for the processes they authenticate. APIKEY-E-001 evidences that FORM's API key authentication layer enforces scope constraints at the Worker level — a `reporting:read` key cannot invoke admin-write mutations regardless of what the caller transmits. The evidence is a code review record (PR + CI gate pass log), which is the industry-standard evidence form for credential-scope controls that enforce at the code layer rather than purely at runtime configuration. The integration test supplement (`reporting:read` key rejected on `POST /v1/admin/seats` with HTTP 403 `insufficient_scope`) provides the runtime validation counterpart. | PR review log + CI gate pass for `api-key-auth.ts` scope enforcement; optional integration test output showing `reporting:read` key rejected on `POST /v1/admin/seats` with HTTP 403 `insufficient_scope`. File: `compliance/evidence/api-key-auth/APIKEY-E-001_<YYYY-MM-DD>_scope-enforcement-review.md` | 7 years |
| **APIKEY-E-002** | **CC6.4** (Access modification when credentials change or terminate) | `api_key.rotated` → `api_key.revoked` DEC-030 HMAC chain sequence; APIKEY-SLO-03 (rotation gap ≤ 26 h / 100% zero-tolerance); APIKEY-CHAIN-02 pg_cron job 13 detects breaches | **CC6.4 auditor narrative:** CC6.4 requires that access is modified or removed when credentials are changed or terminated. APIKEY-E-002 evidences FORM's HMAC-chained audit trail of every API key rotation: each rotation produces an `api_key.rotated` event anchoring the new key credential and an `api_key.revoked` event retiring the predecessor, both DEC-030 HMAC-chained in sequence. The 26-hour rotation gap SLO (APIKEY-SLO-03) governs the maximum window a predecessor key can remain active after a rotation event. APIKEY-CHAIN-02 (pg_cron `api_key_chain_monitor` job 13) detects any gap breach. Zero-rotation observation quarters are filed as affirmative attestation — an explicit record that no key reached the rotation threshold, rather than a gap in the evidence chain. | `SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE event_type IN ('api_key.rotated','api_key.revoked') AND event_ts BETWEEN $obs_start AND $obs_end ORDER BY event_ts` — export as CSV. File: `compliance/evidence/api-key-auth/APIKEY-E-002_<YYYY-QN>_rotation-chain.csv` | 7 years |
| **APIKEY-E-003** | **CC6.8** (Controls against unauthorised changes to security configurations) | `api_key.ip_enforcement_enabled` / `api_key.ip_enforcement_disabled` DEC-030 HIGH/7yr events; `enabled_by` actor UUID cross-referenceable against `form_admins` | **CC6.8 auditor narrative:** CC6.8 requires that controls prevent or detect unauthorised changes to security configurations. API key IP enforcement state (on/off per key) is a security configuration — disabling it removes a network-layer access gate that a tenant administrator intentionally activated. APIKEY-E-003 evidences that every such state change is recorded in a HMAC-chained DEC-030 event at HIGH severity (7yr retention), with the `enabled_by` actor UUID enabling the auditor to cross-reference against `form_admins` and confirm that all changes were made by authorised personnel. Zero-change months are positive evidence of configuration stability. | `SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE event_type LIKE 'api_key.ip_enforcement%' AND event_ts BETWEEN $obs_start AND $obs_end` — cross-reference `enabled_by` UUID against `form_admins`; zero-row = positive stability attestation. File: `compliance/evidence/api-key-auth/APIKEY-E-003_<YYYY-MM>_ip-enforcement-changes.csv` | 7 years |
| **APIKEY-E-004** | **CC7.2** (System monitoring for anomalies) | AL-APIKEY-01 (IP block spike), AL-APIKEY-02 (rotation gap), AL-APIKEY-03 (compromise declaration), AL-SCIM-IP-01 (SCIM provisioning disruption) — all PagerDuty "FORM Security" | **CC7.2 auditor narrative:** CC7.2 requires that system components are monitored for anomalies. The API key authentication layer is a critical system component: long-lived credentials with `admin:write` scope have broad access surface. APIKEY-E-004 evidences that FORM has defined and deployed four automated alert rules covering the principal anomaly scenarios: credential stuffing or misconfiguration (AL-APIKEY-01 — IP block spike > 10 / key / 10 min), rotation gap (AL-APIKEY-02 — no `api_key.revoked` for predecessor within 26 h), confirmed compromise (AL-APIKEY-03 — `api_key.revoked` with `reason = 'compromise'`), and SCIM provisioning disruption (AL-SCIM-IP-01 — `scim.ip_blocked` > 5 / tenant / 15 min). The PagerDuty incident export for the observation period provides an auditor-inspectable record of every detected anomaly and its acknowledgement and resolution timeline. Zero-incident quarters are positive evidence confirming alert rules were armed and the anomaly conditions did not occur during the observation period. | PagerDuty → Incidents → filter service "FORM Security" + alert_key prefix `apikey-` → export CSV (opened_at, acknowledged_at, resolved_at, alert_key, incident_id). File: `compliance/evidence/api-key-auth/APIKEY-E-004_<YYYY-QN>_pagerduty-api-key-health.csv` | 3 years |
| **APIKEY-E-005** | **CC6.1** (Logical access security controls) | API key IP allowlist (`ip_enforcement_enabled + ip_allowlist_config`) provides a network-layer gate on long-lived `admin:write` credentials; SCIM IP allowlist provides equivalent protection for the provisioning credential | **CC6.1 auditor narrative:** CC6.1 requires that logical access security controls restrict access to authorized principals. API key IP enforcement adds a second authentication dimension at the network layer — even a compromised `admin:write` key cannot be used from a non-authorised IP address. APIKEY-E-005 evidences that this control is actively enforced by exporting the `api_key.ip_blocked` and `scim.ip_blocked` DEC-030 event stream for the observation period. Zero-block months are valid compliance evidence for months where tenants had IP enforcement enabled but no out-of-allowlist requests occurred — these months are filed as positive attestation accompanied by a cross-reference to the `tenant_api_keys.ip_enforcement_enabled = true` count confirming the control was armed during the period. | `SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE event_type IN ('api_key.ip_blocked','scim.ip_blocked') AND event_ts BETWEEN $obs_start AND $obs_end` — cross-reference `tenant_api_keys` showing `ip_enforcement_enabled = true` for `admin:write` scope keys confirming control was active. File: `compliance/evidence/api-key-auth/APIKEY-E-005_<YYYY-MM>_ip-block-events.csv` | 3 years |

**Privacy floor (all APIKEY-E artefacts):** no raw API key (discarded in-Worker post-HMAC computation — never reaches `audit_log`); `client_ip_hash` SHA-256 only — no raw IP address in any event; no `key_preview` (customer-facing display identifier, excluded from internal observability per §31.8); `key_id` and `tenant_id` are internal UUIDs; `form_api` REVOKED from `tenant_api_keys` — collection queries executed by `form_system` (pg_cron service role) or compliance-officer under PAM elevation only.

### §105.3 VSAFETY-E-001 through VSAFETY-E-004 · Evidence Artefact Registration (OBSERVABILITY §32.9)

Full source specification: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §32.9`.

| Artefact ID | SOC 2 Criterion | Control evidence | Auditor narrative | Collection method | Retention |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **VSAFETY-E-001** | **CC7.2** (System monitoring for anomalies) | `FORM-VICTOR-001` PagerDuty "FORM Clinical Safety" service configuration screenshot + 90-day incident history export (opened_at, acknowledged_at, resolved_at, alert_key, incident_id) | **CC7.2 auditor narrative:** CC7.2 requires that system components are monitored for anomalies. Victor AI coaching is a critical system component with a zero-tolerance clinical-safety SLO (VICTOR-SLO-01: zero P0 incidents per 30-day window — unconditional zero-tolerance; VICTOR-SLO-02: FORM-VICTOR-001 fires within 5 min of P0 signal — 100% zero-tolerance). VSAFETY-E-001 evidences the primary monitoring control: `FORM-VICTOR-001` alert rule (PagerDuty "FORM Clinical Safety" service) fires on any single `VICTOR_SAFETY_TELEMETRY` row with `severity_class = 'P0'`, escalating immediately to clinical-safety + founder (0-min) with no auto-resolve. The configuration screenshot confirms the rule is deployed and correctly configured; the incident history export provides the auditor-inspectable observation-period record. Zero-incident periods are positive evidence confirming both safety control effectiveness (VICTOR-SLO-01) and alert-rule availability (VICTOR-SLO-02). Supplement: Cloudflare Analytics Engine confirmation query that `VICTOR_SAFETY_TELEMETRY` dataset is live. | PagerDuty → Incidents → filter service "FORM Clinical Safety" → export CSV (opened_at, acknowledged_at, resolved_at, alert_key, incident_id) + Cloudflare Analytics Engine query confirming dataset is live. File: `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/VSAFETY-E-001_<YYYY-QN>_victor-p0-pagerduty.csv` | 7 years |
| **VSAFETY-E-002** | **CC7.3** (Evaluate and communicate security events) | `VICTOR_SAFETY_TELEMETRY` Cloudflare Analytics Engine export: trigger category (VT-01 through VT-10), severity class (P0–P3), detection source, model version, prompt version, timestamp | **CC7.3 auditor narrative:** CC7.3 requires that security events are evaluated and communicated to appropriate stakeholders. Victor safety flags are clinical-safety security events: each flag is evaluated at the category level (VT-01 through VT-10 from R-23, covering medical claims, eating-disorder triggers, self-harm signals, and other clinical categories), assigned a severity class (P0–P3), and attributed to a detection source (model output, prompt analysis, rule-based filter, or human review). VSAFETY-E-002 evidences this evaluation layer — the `VICTOR_SAFETY_TELEMETRY` export provides an auditor-inspectable record of every safety classification made during the observation period. The auditor can verify: (a) the classification framework was applied consistently (VT codes and severity classes present on every row), (b) P0 events were escalated per FORM-VICTOR-001 (cross-reference with VSAFETY-E-001 incident history), and (c) no content is recorded (no `content` field in the dataset — privacy invariant enforced at the Worker emission layer). | `SELECT session_id, trigger_category, severity_class, detection_source, model_version, prompt_version, timestamp FROM victor_safety_telemetry WHERE timestamp BETWEEN $obs_start AND $obs_end ORDER BY timestamp` — export as JSONL via Cloudflare Analytics Engine REST API. File: `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/VSAFETY-E-002_<YYYY-QN>_telemetry-export.jsonl` | 7 years |
| **VSAFETY-E-003** | **CC7.4** (Respond to security events) | VSAFETY-CHAIN-01/02/03 monitor configuration (pg_cron job 15 DDL + `emit-audit-event` Worker VSAFETY-CHAIN-03 guard code) + SQL attestation that VSAFETY-CHAIN-03 fired zero times in the observation period | **CC7.4 auditor narrative:** CC7.4 requires that FORM responds to security events within defined SLAs and that response completeness is evidenced. For Victor AI safety incidents, the mandatory response sequence is: incident opened → contained → Victor disabled → safety_incident_resolved → Victor re-enabled. VSAFETY-CHAIN-03 is the write-time enforcement control: the `emit-audit-event` Worker rejects `ai.victor_reenabled` with HTTP 422 if no matching `ai.safety_incident_resolved` exists for the same `incident_id`, preventing Victor from being re-enabled without completing the full incident response cycle defined in R-23. VSAFETY-E-003 evidences this control via: (1) the VSAFETY-CHAIN-03 Worker code confirming the enforcement is implemented at the write layer, (2) the pg_cron job 15 configuration confirming VSAFETY-CHAIN-01/02 post-hoc chain monitors were active throughout the observation period, and (3) the SQL attestation confirming zero VSAFETY-CHAIN-03 constraint violations (i.e., zero `ai.victor_reenabled` events without a preceding `ai.safety_incident_resolved`) occurred in the observation period. | Code review export for VSAFETY-CHAIN-03 guard in `emit-audit-event` Worker + `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'ai.victor_reenabled' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM audit_log_events e2 WHERE e2.event_type = 'ai.safety_incident_resolved' AND e2.payload->>'incident_id' = audit_log_events.payload->>'incident_id')` returning zero. File: `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vsafety-e-003-chain-attestation.json` | 7 years |
| **VSAFETY-E-004** | **A1.2** (Availability monitoring and response) | Cloudflare KV `victor_coach_enabled` key access log (timestamp + changed_by_user_id per change) + DEC-030 `ai.victor_disabled` and `ai.victor_reenabled` HMAC chain export | **A1.2 auditor narrative:** A1.2 requires that FORM monitors system availability and responds to capacity and availability issues. Victor AI coaching is a contractually committed feature for enterprise tenants: its unavailability is a service delivery failure. Victor availability is managed via the `victor_coach_enabled` KV key — setting it `false` disables Victor platform-wide. VSAFETY-E-004 evidences that every disable/re-enable event had a documented owner and an associated incident reference: DEC-030 `ai.victor_disabled` (HIGH/7yr) and `ai.victor_reenabled` (HIGH/7yr) events are HMAC-chained with an `incident_id` payload field linking to the corresponding R-23 incident record. The KV Audit Log provides the complementary infrastructure-layer record confirming the `changed_by_user_id` identity. Zero-change quarters are positive attestation of continuous availability — filed as an explicit affirmative record rather than a gap in evidence. | Cloudflare KV Audit Log export for `victor_coach_enabled` key (timestamp + changed_by_user_id for each change, covering the observation window) + `SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE event_type IN ('ai.victor_disabled', 'ai.victor_reenabled') AND event_ts BETWEEN $obs_start AND $obs_end ORDER BY event_ts`. File: `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/VSAFETY-E-004_<YYYY-QN>_availability-log.json` | 7 years |

**Privacy floor (all VSAFETY-E artefacts):** no coaching content (`coaching_turns.content` excluded by Worker design at emission — no fragment appears in `VICTOR_SAFETY_TELEMETRY` or any DEC-030 event payload); `session_id` is UUID pseudonym — UUID-to-user-identity mapping is never compiled into evidence and is accessed only under legal hold by compliance-officer; `tenant_id` is FORM-internal UUID (not org slug, not employee name, not email); no GDPR Art. 9 health values in any field; VSAFETY-E-003 SQL attestation result is a scalar integer count. Victor safety classification signals characterise the *Victor output category* (medical claim, jailbreak attempt, etc.), not the user's health state.

scope validated before route handler execution; `reporting:read` rejected on admin mutations; industry-standard code-layer credential-scope evidence form); APIKEY-E-002 (CC6.4 — DEC-030 `api_key.rotated` → `api_key.revoked` HMAC chain; APIKEY-SLO-03 rotation gap ≤ 26 h; APIKEY-CHAIN-02 job 13 breach detection; zero-rotation quarters as affirmative attestation); APIKEY-E-003 (CC6.8 — `api_key.ip_enforcement_enabled/disabled` DEC-030 HIGH/7yr; ## §106 · Cross-Reference Patch — OBSERVABILITY §52 (SCA-OBS-E-001/002 · CC6.8 / CC7.1 / CC7.2 / CC9.2 / A1.1) + §80.3 R2 Folder Retroactive Additions: api-key-auth/, victor-safety/, sca/

**Gap closed:** `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §52.8` (v5.0.0, 2026-06-23) defined SCA-OBS-E-001 (CC6.8/CC7.1/CC9.2 — quarterly SCA SLO performance report) and SCA-OBS-E-002 (CC7.2/A1.1 — quarterly `sca_sla_monitor` job 42 pg_cron run history). OBSERVABILITY §52.10 item 4 recorded `[x] Done — 2026-06-23, SOC2_READINESS.md §54.9 SCA-OBS-E-001/002 registered`, confirming §54.9 registration. However, the §79.4 master consolidated evidence collection table (the canonical auditor-facing registry of all SOC 2 artefacts) was never updated — SCA-OBS-E-001 and SCA-OBS-E-002 were absent from §79.4 with no auditor narratives, no criterion mapping, no collection method, and no retention entry. This is the same gap pattern closed by §84–§103 for earlier evidence series and by §105 for APIKEY-E and VSAFETY-E.

**Retroactive §80.3 additions (also closed by this patch):** §105 (v3.30.0, 2026-06-25) added nine evidence artefacts to §79.4 (APIKEY-E-001/002/003/004/005 and VSAFETY-E-001/002/003/004) but failed to add the corresponding `api-key-auth/` and `victor-safety/` R2 folders to the §80.3 Cloudflare R2 folder map. This patch adds those two folders retroactively alongside the new `sca/` folder for SCA-OBS-E-001/002.

| `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §52.8` | v5.0.0 | 2026-06-23 | SCA-OBS-E-001, SCA-OBS-E-002 | CC6.8, CC7.1, CC7.2, CC9.2, A1.1 |
| `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §54.9` | v3.21.0 | 2026-06-23 | Prior partial registration of SCA-OBS-E-001/002 in §54 section table — §79.4 master table NOT updated | CC6.8, CC7.1, CC7.2, CC9.2, A1.1 |

### §106.2 SCA-OBS-E-001 and SCA-OBS-E-002 · Evidence Artefact Registration (OBSERVABILITY §52.8)

Full source specification: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §52.8`.

| Artefact ID | SOC 2 Criterion | Control evidence | Auditor narrative | Collection method | Retention |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **SCA-OBS-E-001** | **CC6.8** (Prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software), **CC7.1** (Detect and monitor for security events), **CC9.2** (Assess and manage risks from vendors and business partners) | Quarterly SCA SLO performance report: (1) SCA-SLO-01 breach count (`security.sca_sla_breach` event count for the quarter — zero-tolerance; any non-zero count requires root-cause note and AL-SCA-01 activation log); (2) SCA-SLO-02 High CVE 7-day SLA achievement rate (target ≥ 95%); (3) SCA-SLO-03 CI SCA scan pass rate per month (target ≥ 99.5%); (4) AL-SCA-01 PagerDuty `form-security` activation log for the quarter | **CC6.8 auditor narrative:** CC6.8 requires that FORM prevents or detects and acts upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software. Third-party open-source dependencies are the principal vector for malicious-software introduction in a cloud-native SaaS: a compromised or vulnerable dependency can introduce arbitrary code into the runtime. SCA-OBS-E-001 evidences that FORM operates a three-layer SCA detection pipeline (Dependabot → `npm audit` → Snyk; §52.1) with defined SLOs: Critical CVE zero-tolerance (SCA-SLO-01 — automated 24h remediation SLA enforced by pg_cron job 42; breach fires AL-SCA-01 and emits `security.sca_sla_breach` DEC-030 HIGH/7yr); High CVE 7-day SLA (SCA-SLO-02 — ≥ 95% quarterly achievement target); CI SCA gate (SCA-SLO-03 — ≥ 99.5% pass rate; blocks merge on Critical/High). Zero-breach quarters satisfy CC6.8 directly: AL-SCA-01 never activated means no Critical CVE survived beyond 24h during the period, confirming the prevention layer was effective. **CC7.1 auditor narrative:** CC7.1 requires that security events are detected and monitored. SCA-OBS-E-001 item (4) — the AL-SCA-01 PagerDuty activation log — evidences the monitoring layer: pg_cron job 42 (`sca_sla_monitor`) polls the `security_sca_findings` table every 15 minutes; any open Critical CVE aged > 24h fires AL-SCA-01 to PagerDuty `form-security`. The activation log provides an auditor-inspectable record of every SCA security event detected during the observation period, including detection timestamp and resolution timeline. **CC9.2 auditor narrative:** CC9.2 requires that FORM assesses and manages risks from vendors and business partners, including open-source dependency supply chain. SCA-OBS-E-001 items (1)–(3) evidence that FORM quantifies and monitors supply-chain risk on a continuous basis: SCA-SLO-01 zero-tolerance ensures critical supply-chain risk receives same-day response; SCA-SLO-02 and SCA-SLO-03 demonstrate sustained operational adherence to the risk management programme. Quarterly filing with an explicit zero-breach attestation for SCA-SLO-01 or a root-cause note for any breach provides auditors with a structured quarter-over-quarter risk management record. Privacy floor: CVE IDs and package names are public CVE metadata — safe for auditor delivery without redaction; no `user_id`, `tenant_id`, or GDPR Art. 9 health data appears in SCA findings or SLO reports. | `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_log WHERE event_type = 'security.sca_sla_breach' AND event_ts BETWEEN $q_start AND $q_end` for SCA-SLO-01; CI pipeline log query for SCA-SLO-02/03; PagerDuty → Incidents → filter service `form-security` + alert key prefix `sca-` → export CSV for AL-SCA-01 log. Compile into quarterly PDF + supporting CSV. File: `compliance/evidence/sca/SCA-OBS-E-001_<YYYY-QN>_sca-slo-performance.pdf` | 3 years |
| **SCA-OBS-E-002** | **CC7.2** (System components are monitored for anomalies), **A1.1** (Current processing capacity and usage are maintained and monitored) | Quarterly `sca_sla_monitor` pg_cron job 42 run history: export of `cron.job_run_details WHERE jobname = 'sca_sla_monitor'` for the quarter — total runs (expected ≈ 2,688 / quarter at `*/15 * * * *`), successful runs, stale-event count (freshness window breaches > 20 min as detected by `pg-cron-health-monitor` §12.7), AL-SCA-01 activations attributed to job 42 | **CC7.2 auditor narrative:** CC7.2 requires that system components are monitored for anomalies. pg_cron job 42 `sca_sla_monitor` is a critical monitoring component: it is the automated sentinel that detects open Critical CVEs exceeding the 24h SCA-SLO-01 remediation SLA and fires AL-SCA-01. If job 42 is stale (no successful run within 20 min), the SCA monitoring blind-spot materialises — an open Critical CVE could go undetected without alert. SCA-OBS-E-002 evidences that job 42 itself is monitored for operational anomalies by the `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.7), which fires `system.sca_monitor_stale_declared` (HIGH/7yr DEC-030) and triggers INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-42 if any 20-min window passes without a successful run. The quarterly run history export allows auditors to inspect total runs vs. successful runs, identify any stale windows (with their incident reference), and confirm the monitoring sentinel operated at the expected `*/15 * * * *` cadence throughout the observation period. Zero-stale-event quarters confirm the monitoring sentinel operated without interruption — no SCA detection blind-spot occurred during the period. **A1.1 auditor narrative:** A1.1 requires that FORM maintains and monitors current processing capacity and usage. The SCA monitoring pipeline is a capacity-sensitive component: pg_cron requires an active Postgres connection pool slot and pg_net requires network throughput to dispatch AL-SCA-01 webhooks. SCA-OBS-E-002 run history provides a capacity utilisation signal for the pg_cron + pg_net subsystem: total runs vs. successful runs trends inform whether connection-pool contention or pg_net saturation is affecting the 15-min cadence. A degrading success rate (even without threshold breach) is an early-warning capacity signal cross-referenced against `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12` (pg_cron health monitoring) and `docs/COST_MODEL.md §3.3` (Supabase compute tier). Privacy floor: `cron.job_run_details` carries only operational fields (jobname, runid, started, ended, status, return_message) — no `user_id`, `tenant_id`, CVE identifiers, or GDPR Art. 9 health data. `return_message` is a Postgres-internal status string; compliance-officer reviews for inadvertent CVE-ID leakage before filing, though the job's `return_message` is SQL-execution status only. | `SELECT jobname, runid, database, username, command, status, return_message, start_time, end_time FROM cron.job_run_details WHERE jobname = 'sca_sla_monitor' AND start_time BETWEEN $q_start AND $q_end ORDER BY start_time` — export as CSV; compute total / successful / failed counts; cross-reference stale-declaration events from `audit_log WHERE event_type = 'system.sca_monitor_stale_declared'`. File: `compliance/evidence/sca/SCA-OBS-E-002_<YYYY-QN>_job42-run-history.csv` | 3 years |

**Privacy floor (all SCA-OBS-E artefacts):** CVE IDs (e.g. `CVE-2024-XXXXX`) and package names are public vulnerability metadata from the NVD/OSV feed — no redaction required for auditor delivery. `security.sca_sla_breach` DEC-030 event payloads contain `cve_id`, `package_name`, `severity`, `detected_at`, `remediation_status` — no `user_id`, `tenant_id`, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 health data. pg_cron `cron.job_run_details` operational metadata only — no user-level data. Both artefacts are classified as STANDARD sensitivity; `form_api` REVOKED from `security_sca_findings` and `cron.job_run_details` — collection executed by `form_system` (pg_cron service role) or compliance-officer under PAM elevation only.

### §106.4 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Additions

Two artefacts added to the §80.4 Vanta auditor portal mirror list by this patch:

**SCA-OBS-E-001** (`SOC2_READINESS §106` — quarterly CC6.8/CC7.1/CC9.2 SCA SLO performance report; upload quarterly from M12; zero-breach quarters filed as positive SCA-SLO-01 zero-tolerance attestation — no AL-SCA-01 activation = no Critical CVE exceeded the 24h SLA during the quarter). **SCA-OBS-E-002** (`SOC2_READINESS §106` — quarterly CC7.2/A1.1 `sca_sla_monitor` job 42 pg_cron run history; upload quarterly from M12; zero-stale-event quarters confirm the 15-min SCA monitoring sentinel operated without interruption — no SCA detection blind-spot occurred during the period). Added by §106 (2026-06-25).

## §107 · Cross-Reference Patch — OBSERVABILITY §29 (PAM / Privileged Access Management Observability · CC6-E-PAM-001 through CC6-E-PAM-004 + CC7-E-PAM-001 · CC6.1 / CC6.2 / CC6.3 / CC6.6 / CC6.7 / CC7.2 / CC7.3)

**Gap closed:** `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §29.9` defines five evidence artefacts (CC6-E-PAM-001 through CC6-E-PAM-004 and CC7-E-PAM-001) covering the PAM elevation approval workflow, session automatic deprovisioning, KV TTL configuration, break-glass policy, and PagerDuty alert history. `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §29.10 item 10` (P1, Observation-period start) explicitly requires: "create entries in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md` CC6.1/CC6.2/CC6.3/CC6.7 control evidence rows." No §79.4 rows, no cross-reference patch, and no `pam/` R2 folder were ever authored for these artefacts. This patch closes that gap.

---

### §107.1 Source Table

| Source section | Document | Version | Gap description |
|---|---|---|---|
| §29.9 PAM Auditor Evidence Artefacts | `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md` | v5.0.1 (2026-06-25) | CC6-E-PAM-001 through CC6-E-PAM-004 and CC7-E-PAM-001 defined with explicit SOC2_READINESS cross-reference obligation (§29.10 item 10, P1/Observation-period start) — zero §79.4 rows, no cross-reference patch, `pam/` R2 folder absent from §80.3 |

---

### §107.2 PAM Evidence Artefact Table with Auditor Narratives

| Artefact ID | Criteria | Retention | Cadence | Collection method | Auditor narrative |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **CC6-E-PAM-001** | CC6.1 / CC6.2 | 7yr | Quarterly | `SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE event_type IN ('pam.elevation_approved','pam.elevation_denied') AND event_ts BETWEEN $obs_start AND $obs_end ORDER BY event_ts`; SHA-256 the export file; file to `compliance/evidence/pam/CC6-E-PAM-001_<YYYY-QN>_elevation-audit.csv` | **CC6.1 (no standing access):** every `pam.elevation_approved` event has an `approver_user_id` distinct from `requester_user_id` (PAM-CHAIN-01 no-self-approval invariant enforced at the `pam-elevation-service` Worker layer). HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 provides tamper-evident verification that the export is unmodified. **CC6.2 (authorization before credential issuance):** the `pam.elevation_approved` sequence precedes every PAM session — no session exists without a prior approval event in the DEC-030 chain. Zero-denial quarters filed as positive attestation: zero `pam.elevation_denied` entries = no credential stuffing or insider-threat attempts detected during the period. |
| **CC6-E-PAM-002** | CC6.1 / CC6.3 | 3yr | Quarterly | `SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE event_type = 'pam.session_expired' AND event_ts BETWEEN $obs_start AND $obs_end ORDER BY event_ts`; cross-reference approved vs. expired event counts (must be 1:1); file to `compliance/evidence/pam/CC6-E-PAM-002_<YYYY-QN>_session-expiry.csv` | **CC6.1 (no standing access):** every PAM session terminates automatically — no row in the export will have elapsed time exceeding the TTL declared in `constants.ts` (PAM-SLO-02 zero-tolerance invariant). **CC6.3 (deprovisioning):** automatic KV TTL expiry constitutes revocation; this export proves timely deprovisioning for every PAM session in the observation window. Auditor cross-check: `COUNT(approved_events) = COUNT(expired_events)` confirms one-to-one lifecycle closure with no abandoned sessions. |
| **CC6-E-PAM-003** | CC6.1 / CC6.7 | 3yr | Manual-event (on Worker TTL update) | Screenshot of `PAM_KV` binding in `wrangler.toml` + sanitized `workers/pam-elevation-service/src/constants.ts` excerpt showing `READ_WRITE_TTL_MS` and `DESTRUCTIVE_TTL_MS` constants (sanitized of internal IDs); file to `compliance/evidence/pam/CC6-E-PAM-003_<YYYY-MM-DD>_kv-ttl-config.png` | **CC6.1 (no standing access — architectural layer):** `PAM_KV` TTL constants are the architectural enforcement layer for zero standing access; any session beyond the declared duration is cryptographically inaccessible (KV key has expired). The `constants.ts` excerpt shows the values in source code, providing a code-level audit trail. **CC6.7 (information transmission protection):** PAM session credentials are time-bounded by cryptographic TTL — no credential can persist longer than the declared constant, limiting the window of exposure if a PAM KV key were exfiltrated. Artefact recollected on any Worker deployment that modifies TTL values; if no modification in a quarter, prior artefact remains current. |
| **CC6-E-PAM-004** | CC6.1 / CC6.6 | 7yr | Annual policy + quarterly identity-list review | Terraform plan output from `cloudflare/access/break-glass-policy.tf` (sanitized — email addresses replaced with role labels, max 5 identities); quarterly identity-list review recorded in GitHub Issue or Slack thread with compliance-officer sign-off; file to `compliance/evidence/pam/CC6-E-PAM-004_<YYYY-QN>_break-glass-policy.<ext>` | **CC6.1 (restricted access):** break-glass policy enforces that only named individuals (max 5, reviewed quarterly) can activate the highest-privilege tier. The Terraform output is a machine-verifiable configuration declaration filed as CC6.1 evidence of access scope control. **CC6.6 (security incident notification):** break-glass tier activation triggers AL-PAM-01 P0 immediately (OBSERVABILITY §29.4), which maps directly to CC6.6's requirement to notify relevant parties on security events. The quarterly identity-list review proves the access grant list is maintained under human governance, not purely automated. |
| **CC7-E-PAM-001** | CC7.2 / CC7.3 | 3yr | Quarterly | PagerDuty → Incidents → filter by service "FORM PAM" + observation date range → export CSV with columns `opened_at`, `acknowledged_at`, `resolved_at`, `alert_key`; file to `compliance/evidence/pam/CC7-E-PAM-001_<YYYY-QN>_pagerduty-pam-incidents.csv` | **CC7.2 (monitoring of system components):** AL-PAM-01/02/03 alert rules are continuously armed throughout the observation window; the PagerDuty log proves monitoring was active and alerts fired (or did not fire, in the zero-incident case). Zero-incident quarters filed as positive attestation: no AL-PAM-01 activation = no break-glass event; no AL-PAM-02 = no elevation-denial spike indicating credential attack; no AL-PAM-03 = no clock-skew anomaly. **CC7.3 (anomaly response):** any incident with `acknowledged_at - opened_at > 60 seconds` is a PAM-SLO-01 breach; the absence of such rows (or an incident response record where the breach was documented and investigated) satisfies CC7.3's response-SLA requirement. |

**Privacy floor:** CC6-E-PAM-001 and CC6-E-PAM-002 contain `admin_user_id` UUIDs and `pam_session_id` UUIDs — these are internal administrative pseudonyms used for security investigation, not employee `user_id` values and not GDPR Art. 9 health data. Access to `compliance/evidence/pam/` is restricted to `compliance-officer`, `r2:devops-lead` (read-only), and `r2:vanta-sync` (read-only for mirror); `form_api` has NO ACCESS (§107.4). CC6-E-PAM-003 is sanitized of internal IDs before filing. CC6-E-PAM-004 replaces email addresses with role labels in the Terraform output. CC7-E-PAM-001 contains PagerDuty operational metadata only — no `user_id`, no employee name, no health value, no coaching content.

---
