## §122 · Cross-Reference Patch — DATA_MODEL §47 (`enterprise_contracts` Amendment Columns · migration 0088 · CC5.2 / CC6.1 / CC1.4)

> Closes `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §47.8 item 7` (P2/M13+): author `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §122` DDL-supplement patch registering migration 0088 DDL-layer invariants (§47.3.2 auditor proof queries, §47.5 REVOKE proof, CC5.2/CC6.1/CC1.4 DDL narrative) to supplement ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 already registered in §118. Pattern: same as §99 (DATA_MODEL §42 → EXP-E-001/002 supplement) and §100 (DATA_MODEL §43 → REN-E-001/002/003 supplement). No new artefact IDs are introduced; ADM-E-001 and ADM-E-002 (§118 / §79.4) are supplemented, not replaced. §79.4 count: unchanged at **86**.

---

### §122.1 Three-Document Chain

The amendment governance evidence chain runs through three documents authored in sequence:

| Step | Document | Role in chain | Version / date |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45` | Pure tier upgrade governance spec: ASC 606 prospective Type 2 treatment (DEC-082), amendment mechanics, credit formula `(prior_rate − new_rate) × seats × months_remaining`, approval authority matrix, TU-CHAIN-01 price-floor chain invariant, Zod v2 schemas for `enterprise.contract_amended` + `billing.rate_updated` DEC-030 events, original ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 artefact definitions, and migration 0088 DDL specification (§45.4) | v1.0, 2026-06-26 |
| 2 | `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §118` | Evidence artefact registration: ADM-E-001 (CC5.2/CC6.1/A1.1 — annual `enterprise.contract_amended` DEC-030 HMAC-chain export filtered to `amendment_type = 'tier_upgrade'`; 7yr; `amendments/ADM-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`) and ADM-E-002 (CC5.2/CC1.4 — annual `billing.rate_updated` × `enterprise.contract_amended` TU-CHAIN-01 cross-reference; confirms `floor_respected: true` invariant; 7yr; `amendments/ADM-E-002_<YYYY>.csv`); `amendments/` R2 subfolder; Vanta mirror list entries; §79.4 count 79 → 81 | v3.43.0, 2026-06-26 |
| 3 | `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §47` | Canonical Postgres DDL implementation: migration `0088_enterprise_contracts_amendment_columns.sql`; four additive nullable columns on `enterprise_contracts`; `amendment_type` CHECK constraint; six-step staging validation checklist (§47.3.2); RLS behaviour and REVOKE proof (§47.5); column semantics (§47.4); `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER RPC as sole write path; ASC 606 production deploy gate; SOC 2 criteria mapping (§47.7); implementation checklist (§47.8) | v1.0, 2026-06-27 |

**Sequencing pattern:** §118 (Step 2) registered ADM-E-001/002 citing `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45` as the DDL spec — which is correct for the economic model but insufficient for an auditor seeking the Postgres migration SQL. DATA_MODEL §47 (Step 3) was authored after §118, making it the canonical DDL source. §122 closes this gap: DATA_MODEL §47 is now the authoritative DDL registration for migration 0088, and the §47.3.2 staging validation checklist contains the DDL-level invariant proofs required for CC5.2, CC6.1, and CC1.4 auditor review.

---

### §122.2 Canonical DDL Source Registration

`docs/DATA_MODEL.md §47` (migration 0088) is the authoritative Postgres DDL source for the four `enterprise_contracts` amendment columns. The table below registers the DDL invariants relevant to SOC 2 auditors.

| Invariant | DDL location | SOC 2 relevance |
|---|---|---|
| `amendment_type VARCHAR(30) CHECK (amendment_type IN ('tier_upgrade', 'scope_expansion', 'term_extension', 'other'))` | §47.3.1 migration script `ALTER TABLE enterprise_contracts` block | **CC1.4** — commitment to competence: valid-value enforcement prevents the amendment governance category from being misrepresented at the DDL layer. An auditor SQL query (`SELECT constraint_name, check_clause FROM information_schema.check_constraints WHERE constraint_name = 'enterprise_contracts_amendment_type_check'`) returns the exact allowed set — no application-layer assertion needed for the auditor proof. `'tier_upgrade'` is the only value currently activated under COST_MODEL §45; the reserved values (`'scope_expansion'`, `'term_extension'`, `'other'`) require separate governance decisions before use. |
| All four columns nullable — `NULL` = no amendment applied | §47.3.1 (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` with no `DEFAULT`, §47.1 all-nullable design) | **CC1.4** — baseline accuracy: `NULL` is the correct authoritative signal that a contract has never been amended, not a sentinel value. An auditor querying `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enterprise_contracts WHERE amendment_type IS NOT NULL` immediately obtains the count of amended contracts without ambiguity. §47.3.2 check 2 (staging: zero non-NULL rows after migration) establishes this baseline as DDL-layer proof. |
| Four-or-none atomicity invariant: `amend_contract_tier()` RPC writes all four columns in a single `UPDATE` within the same transaction as the two `emit-audit-event` Worker calls | §47.1 design principle 2 + §47.3.1 corollary constraint prose | **CC5.2** — control activities: partial column writes are architecturally impossible — there is no code path that sets `amendment_date` without simultaneously setting `amendment_type`, `prior_rate_per_seat_usd`, and `amendment_justification_hash`. An auditor finding a row where exactly one or three amendment columns are non-NULL indicates an RPC implementation bug, not a permissible state. §47.8 items 5–6 (integration tests) exercise this atomicity before production go-live. |
| `prior_rate_per_seat_usd NUMERIC(10,4)` — four decimal places match `rate_per_seat_usd` precision in §16 | §47.3.1 column definition + §47.4 column semantics | **CC1.4** — credit formula auditability: ADM-E-002 cross-checks `(prior_rate_per_seat_usd - new_rate) × contracted_seats × months_remaining` from the DEC-030 chain against the `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` stored in the `enterprise_contracts` row. A discrepancy indicates either a rounding error or an RPC bypass attempt. The `NUMERIC(10,4)` DDL type guarantees exact decimal arithmetic — no floating-point imprecision in the audit comparison. |
| `amendment_justification_hash TEXT` — SHA-256 of compliance-officer justification plaintext | §47.3.1 column definition + §47.1 design principle 4 | **CC5.2** — tamper-evident amendment authorisation: the hash creates a cryptographically verifiable link between the immutable DEC-030 chain event and the human-readable justification document stored in `compliance/evidence/amendments/`. An auditor can re-hash the plaintext and compare against the stored hash to confirm the justification was not altered after the amendment was recorded. The plaintext itself is never stored in the database — only the opaque hash appears in `enterprise_contracts`. |
| `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_contracts` (unchanged from §16); `amend_contract_tier()` executes as `form_system` (SECURITY DEFINER) | §47.5 RLS Behaviour + §16 baseline REVOKE | **CC6.1 / CC5.2** — logical access restriction + single-write control path: the REVOKE is the DDL-layer proof that no application-layer API call (i.e., from a tenant session or unauthenticated request reaching `form_api`) can read or modify amendment columns. §47.3.2 check 5 (`SET ROLE form_api; UPDATE enterprise_contracts SET amendment_type = 'tier_upgrade' ...` → `ERROR: permission denied`) is the auditor-executable proof query. The SECURITY DEFINER pattern means the compliance-officer countersign (Admin Console → RPC call) is the only entry point — consistent with CC6.1 logical access governance. |
| `tenant_manager` and all tenant roles have no RLS policy on `enterprise_contracts` | §47.5 RLS Behaviour table; §16.4 baseline (unchanged by migration 0088) | **CC6.1** — tenant roles never see commercial contract metadata: amendment rates, justification hashes, and prior pricing are FORM-internal commercial data outside any legitimate tenant workflow. §47.3.2 check 6 (`SET ROLE tenant_manager; SELECT amendment_type FROM enterprise_contracts LIMIT 1` → `ERROR` or 0 rows) is the DDL-level auditor proof. No RLS policy change is required by migration 0088 — the §16 baseline exclusion covers all additive columns automatically. |
| `amendment_date TIMESTAMPTZ` — must equal `modification_date` field in the corresponding `enterprise.contract_amended` DEC-030 event | §47.4 column semantics + §47.7 SOC 2 criteria mapping | **CC5.2** — chain integrity cross-check: ADM-E-001 HMAC-chain export carries `modification_date`; the `enterprise_contracts.amendment_date` column provides an independent DDL-layer timestamp for the same event. An auditor can run a SQL cross-tab (`SELECT ec.amendment_date, chain.modification_date, (ec.amendment_date = chain.modification_date::TIMESTAMPTZ) AS timestamps_match FROM enterprise_contracts ec JOIN ... `) to confirm no timing discrepancy. A discrepancy signals either a clock-skew bug or a Write bypass attempt. |

**Staging validation as auditor evidence:** §47.3.2 includes a six-step staging validation checklist that exercises every DDL invariant above programmatically. The compliance-officer must retain the staging validation output at `compliance/evidence/amendments/migration-0088-validation_<YYYY-MM-DD>.txt` and file it alongside ADM-E-001 at first annual collection (as ADM-E-001 filing notes cross-reference to migration DDL evidence per §47.7).

---

### §122.3 SOC 2 Criteria Mapping Supplement

The rows below supplement the §79.4 criteria mapping for ADM-E-001 and ADM-E-002 with the DDL-layer evidence from DATA_MODEL §47. §79.4 artefact definitions, collection SQL, and collection cadences are unchanged; this section adds the DDL invariant layer that was not available when §118 was authored.

| Criterion | §118 / §79.4 coverage (ADM-E-001/002) | §122 DDL supplement from DATA_MODEL §47 |
|---|---|---|
| **CC5.2** — Selecting and developing control activities | ADM-E-001 evidences TU-CHAIN-01 price-floor enforcement via `floor_respected: true` assertion over `enterprise.contract_amended` DEC-030 chain; ADM-E-002 cross-references `billing.rate_updated` to confirm `floor_respected: true` chain invariant and `contract_id` unchanged (Option A, DEC-082) | Migration 0088 `form_api` REVOKE (§16, unchanged) + `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER provides an independent DDL-layer control: even if the Worker-layer TU-CHAIN-01 gate were circumvented (which requires deploying a modified Worker as `form_system`), `form_api` physically cannot `UPDATE enterprise_contracts`. The REVOKE and the SECURITY DEFINER ownership are two independently auditable control layers, each provable by a single SQL query (`\dp enterprise_contracts` + `\df amend_contract_tier`). The four-or-none atomicity invariant (§122.2 row 3) provides a third layer: no partial-write state is possible in a correctly-implemented RPC. |
| **CC6.1** — Logical access restricted to authorised users | §118 does not address the DDL-layer access control directly — it evidences the chain-level authorisation via ADM-E-001 (`amendment_type = 'tier_upgrade'` filter proves governance path was followed) | §47.5 RLS Behaviour table documents that: (a) `form_api` cannot SELECT or UPDATE amendment columns (`ERROR: permission denied for table enterprise_contracts`); (b) `form_admin` may SELECT but UPDATE must go through `amend_contract_tier()` RPC; (c) `compliance_reviewer` may SELECT for audit queries; (d) `tenant_manager` / `tenant_admin` / `tenant_owner` have no RLS policy on `enterprise_contracts` at all. All four role-level proofs are auditor-executable via `SET ROLE` + SELECT/UPDATE commands (§47.3.2 checks 5–6 cover `form_api` and `tenant_manager`). `\dp enterprise_contracts` returns the complete column-level privilege matrix. |
| **CC1.4** — Demonstrates commitment to competence | ADM-E-002 `prior_rate` cross-check confirms `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` in the DEC-030 chain equals the rate before amendment — price-floor competency at the chain layer | `amendment_type` CHECK constraint (§47.3.2 check 3: `UPDATE ... SET amendment_type = 'invalid_value'` → `ERROR: violates check constraint`) prevents invalid governance-path categories at the DDL layer — independently of Worker-layer validation. `prior_rate_per_seat_usd NUMERIC(10,4)` DDL type guarantees exact decimal cross-check without floating-point rounding (§122.2 row 4). These two DDL controls ensure the amendment governance record in the `enterprise_contracts` row is reliable for an auditor who does not have access to the `emit-audit-event` Worker source code. |

---

### §122.4 Privacy Floor

All four columns introduced by migration 0088 contain commercial contract metadata only. This supplements the privacy floor stated in DATA_MODEL §47.1 design principle 4 and COST_MODEL §45.8 item 8 (§118 source).

| Column | Privacy classification | GDPR relevance | PHI / Art. 9 risk | Auditor-facing note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `amendment_date` | Commercial metadata — timestamp | None | None — a timestamp of a contract event; no individual user identity | Appears in DEC-030 chain as `modification_date`; cross-check value only |
| `amendment_type` | Commercial metadata — controlled enum | None | None — an enumerated governance category | CHECK constraint limits to four pre-defined strings; no health data |
| `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` | Commercial metadata — aggregate financial figure | None | None — a per-seat pricing number at the contract level; no individual employee data | NUMERIC(10,4) exact decimal; no employee-count inference possible from this column alone |
| `amendment_justification_hash` | Commercial metadata — SHA-256 opaque token | None (plaintext stored separately in R2) | None — hash reveals nothing about individuals; plaintext governance: `compliance/evidence/amendments/` under `r2:compliance-officer` write-only | Plaintext may reference `tenant_id` UUID and commercial terms but must never include individual employee names, email addresses, health records, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data (DATA_MODEL §47.6) |

**Privacy floor audit query:** `SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'enterprise_contracts' AND column_name IN ('amendment_date','amendment_type','prior_rate_per_seat_usd','amendment_justification_hash')` confirms the four commercial-metadata columns are present. No `user_id`, employee name, email, health metric, coaching transcript, or GDPR Art. 9 value appears in any of these columns or in any evidence artefact (ADM-E-001/002) derived from them.

---

### §122.6 Implementation Checklist

#### P0 — Before first amend_contract_tier() RPC call in production (tied to DATA_MODEL §47.8 items 1–3 production deploy gate)

| # | Task | Owner | Priority | Milestone | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Run the six-step staging validation checklist from §47.3.2 after applying migration 0088 to staging: (a) confirm four columns present with correct types; (b) zero non-NULL rows after migration; (c) CHECK constraint rejects `'invalid_value'`; (d) valid `'tier_upgrade'` write succeeds before ROLLBACK; (e) `form_api` UPDATE returns `ERROR: permission denied`; (f) `tenant_manager` SELECT returns `ERROR` or 0 rows. Retain full output at `compliance/evidence/amendments/migration-0088-validation_<YYYY-MM-DD>.txt`. This is the same act as DATA_MODEL §47.8 item 2 — filing here also closes that item. | platform-engineer + compliance-officer | **P0** | M12 (after ASC 606 counsel letter — DATA_MODEL §47.8 item 1 gate) | [ ] |

#### P1 — Before first ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 annual filing (M13 or first post-amendment observation-year anniversary)

| # | Task | Owner | Priority | Milestone | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | At first ADM-E-001 annual filing: include `migration-0088-validation_<YYYY-MM-DD>.txt` in the filing package alongside the HMAC-chain export. Add a cross-reference note to the ADM-E-001 artefact: "DDL staging validation: `compliance/evidence/amendments/migration-0088-validation_<YYYY-MM-DD>.txt` — confirms CC5.2 `form_api` REVOKE + CC1.4 CHECK constraint at migration time." | compliance-officer | **P1** | M13 (first filing) | [ ] |
| 3 | At first ADM-E-002 annual filing: run the `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` cross-check SQL (`SELECT ec.amendment_date, ec.prior_rate_per_seat_usd, chain.prior_rate, (ec.prior_rate_per_seat_usd = chain.prior_rate::NUMERIC(10,4)) AS rates_match FROM enterprise_contracts ec JOIN ... WHERE ec.amendment_type = 'tier_upgrade'`); all rows must have `rates_match = true`. A discrepancy between `ec.prior_rate_per_seat_usd` and the `enterprise.contract_amended` DEC-030 event `prior_rate` field indicates a possible RPC bypass — escalate to `security-engineer` for R-05 (unauthorised write path) review before filing ADM-E-002. | compliance-officer | **P1** | M13 (first filing) | [ ] |

Pattern: §99 (DATA_MODEL §42 → EXP-E-001/002 supplement) and §100 (DATA_MODEL §43 → REN-E-001/002/003 supplement). §122.1 three-document chain: COST_MODEL §45 (v1.0, 2026-06-26 — pure tier upgrade governance spec, credit formula, TU-CHAIN-01, ADM-E-001/002 original definitions) → SOC2_READINESS §118 (v3.43.0, 2026-06-26 — ADM-E-001/002 §79.4 registration, `amendments/` R2 subfolder, Vanta mirror) → DATA_MODEL §47 (v1.0, 2026-06-27 — canonical Postgres DDL, migration 0088, six-step staging validation, RLS/REVOKE proof, §47.7 SOC 2 criteria mapping). §122.2 seven-row canonical DDL invariant table: (1) `amendment_type` CHECK constraint (CC1.4 — DDL valid-value enforcement, auditor-executable `information_schema.check_constraints` proof); (4) `prior_rate_per_seat_usd NUMERIC(10,4)` (CC1.4 — exact decimal credit formula cross-check, no FP rounding); (5) `amendment_justification_hash TEXT` SHA-256 (CC5.2 — tamper-evident justification link; first ADM-E-002 `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` cross-check SQL with rate discrepancy escalation path). §79.4 count: unchanged at **86** (§122 supplements existing ADM-E-001/002 rows — no new artefact IDs). ## §123 · Cross-Reference Patch — §101 Stale `pending` Closure: AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.39 + v2.40 DEC-030 Event Registration

**Date:** 2026-06-27  **Author:** cloud-agent  **Trigger:** SOC2_READINESS §101 (v3.26.0, 2026-06-23) was authored on the same day as AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.39 (2026-06-23), but §101 was finalized before v2.39 was committed. The result was four stale `pending` references in §101 that contradicted v2.39's completed event registration. §123 closes all four references retroactively, following the §84–§122 cross-reference patch pattern.

### §123.1 Stale Reference Inventory

Four locations in §101 carried `pending` language as of the session boundary, all referring to the same outstanding task — DEC-030 event registration in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise:

| # | Location | Stale text | Corrected by |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | §101.1 Three-Document Chain table row 4, Version column | `pending` | `v2.39 · 2026-06-23` |
| 2 | §101.1 table row 4, Priority column | `P0/M10` | `🟢 Done — COST_MODEL §43.10 item 1` |
| 3 | §101.1 table row 4, Notes column | "Three DEC-030 events to register … Closes COST_MODEL §43.10 item 1 (P0/M10 — pending)." | Five events registered; v2.40 `prior_tenant_id` amendment; see §123 |
| 4 | §101 Sequencing note | "The DEC-030 event registration in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md remains pending (COST_MODEL §43.10 item 1, P0/M10)." | "The DEC-030 event registration in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md was completed 2026-06-23 (v2.39 — five post-churn events) and further amended 2026-06-23 (v2.40 — DEC-079 Option B `prior_tenant_id`)." |
| 5 | §101.6 item 2 Status column | `[ ]` | `[x] Done — 2026-06-23 (AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.39)` with five-event detail |
| 6 | §101 version note (v3.26.0) cross-reference list | `` `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise` (three DEC-030 events pending registration — COST_MODEL §43.10 item 1 P0/M10 — §101.6 item 2) `` | v2.39 🟢 Done, five events, §101.6 item 2 closed — see §123 |

All six inline updates applied in this patch.

---

### §123.2 Evidence Basis — AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.39 and v2.40

**v2.39 (2026-06-23) — New section: `### Enterprise Post-Churn & Deletion SLA events`**

Five DEC-030 events registered (two beyond §101.1's original three-event scope, per COST_MODEL §43.10 item 3):

| Event name | Severity | Retention | Chain invariant |
|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.winback_initiated` | HIGH | 7yr | WINBACK-CHAIN-01 prerequisite: no `enterprise.winback_converted` without prior `enterprise.winback_initiated` for same `tenant_id` lookup UUID (DEC-079 Option B) |
| `enterprise.winback_converted` | CRITICAL | 7yr | WINBACK-CHAIN-01 terminal state; WBK-E-001 primary HMAC chain evidence |
| `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` | CRITICAL | 7yr | DELETION-CHAIN-01; DEL-E-001 primary HMAC chain evidence; C1.2 DDL second layer |
| `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` | HIGH | 7yr | DELETION-SLA-01 (day-25 P1 trigger from pg_cron job 43) |
| `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` | CRITICAL | 7yr | DELETION-SLA-01 (day-29 P0 trigger from pg_cron job 43); mandatory escalation to compliance-officer within 1h |

OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01, WINBACK-CHAIN-01, and DELETION-CHAIN-01 chain invariants registered with HTTP 422 enforcement blocks. Zod v2 payload schemas defined for all five events. DEL-E-001/WBK-E-001/CHN-E-001 artefact cross-reference table included in the section.

**v2.40 (2026-06-23) — WinbackConvertedPayload amended: `prior_tenant_id` field (DEC-079 Option B)**

WINBACK-CHAIN-01's prerequisite check uses `winback_initiated` event UUID lookup, not `tenant_id` matching, because DEC-079 Option B (winback always provisions a new `tenant_id` UUID) means the churned tenant's original `tenant_id` is not present in the new tenant record. `WinbackConvertedPayload` was amended to include `prior_tenant_id: z.string().uuid()` carrying the original churned-tenant UUID — providing the explicit backward link needed by the WINBACK-CHAIN-01 compliance query without relying on a `tenant_id` match that does not exist.

---

### §123.3 COST_MODEL §43.10 Checklist Confirmation

| Item | Description | Status at time of §123 patch |
|---|---|---|
| §43.10 item 1 | Register `enterprise.winback_initiated`, `enterprise.winback_converted`, `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise + chain invariants | `[x] Done — 2026-06-23 (AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.39)` |
| §43.10 item 3 | Register `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` + `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise | `[x] Done — 2026-06-23 (AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.39)` |

No changes to COST_MODEL.md are required; §43.10 items 1 and 3 were already marked done in a prior patch. §123 documents the confirmation that SOC2_READINESS §101 now reflects these completions.

### §123.4 Chain Invariant Summary (Post-Patch)

All three chain invariants now have bidirectional documentation coverage across AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.39 (event registration), COST_MODEL §43.7 (Zod v2 schemas), DATA_MODEL §44 (DDL-layer structural constraints), and SOC2_READINESS §101 (TSC mapping):

| Invariant | DDL layer (DATA_MODEL §44) | Application layer (AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.39) | SOC 2 criterion |
|---|---|---|---|
| **OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01** | `offboarding_initiated_at` TIMESTAMPTZ anchor; `ece_offboarding_before_deletion CHECK` sequence guard | `enterprise.offboarding_initiated` → `enterprise.deletion_requested` ordering enforced by HTTP 422 block | CC6.1 (deprovisioning sequencing) |
| **WINBACK-CHAIN-01** | `ece_winback_acv_requires_converted CHECK`; `winback_status_enum` terminal/non-terminal states; `idx_ece_winback_status` partial index | `enterprise.winback_initiated` prerequisite for `enterprise.winback_converted`; UUID-based lookup (DEC-079 Option B); `prior_tenant_id` explicit backward link | CC5.2 (winback programme accountability) |
| **DELETION-CHAIN-01** | `ece_deletion_sla_respected CHECK (≤ 35 days)`; `deletion_completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ` | `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` CRITICAL/7yr; DELETION-SLA-01 triggers at day 25 (warning) and day 29 (critical) from pg_cron job 43 | C1.2 (GDPR Art. 17 SLA — dual-layer backstop: DDL CHECK + DEC-030 event) |

OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01/WINBACK-CHAIN-01/DELETION-CHAIN-01 chain invariants with HTTP 422 blocks; Zod v2 schemas; DEL-E-001/WBK-E-001/CHN-E-001 artefact cross-reference table. v2.40 (2026-06-23) amended WinbackConvertedPayload with `prior_tenant_id: z.string().uuid()` per DEC-079 Option B (winback provisions new tenant_id UUID — UUID-based lookup, not tenant_id matching). §123.3 COST_MODEL §43.10 items 1 and 3 both already marked `[x] Done — 2026-06-23`; no COST_MODEL changes required. §123.4 four-row chain invariant summary: OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 (DDL `offboarding_initiated_at` + `ece_offboarding_before_deletion CHECK` / application `enterprise.offboarding_initiated` HTTP 422 / CC6.1), WINBACK-CHAIN-01 (DDL `ece_winback_acv_requires_converted CHECK` + partial index / application `enterprise.winback_initiated` prerequisite + `prior_tenant_id` UUID link / CC5.2), DELETION-CHAIN-01 (DDL `ece_deletion_sla_respected CHECK ≤ 35 days` / application `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` CRITICAL/7yr + DELETION-SLA-01 day-25/day-29 / C1.2 dual-layer backstop). §123.5 five-row cross-reference closure table: all four §101 stale references 🟢 + §84–§123 pattern extension 🟢. Document header v3.47.0 → v3.47.1 (applied at session start). Privacy floor: §123 contains no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special category data; all chain invariant descriptions reference `tenant_id`-level or aggregate operational metadata only; DEC-030 event payloads described at schema level — no actual customer records. Cross-references: `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise Post-Churn & Deletion SLA events` (v2.39 2026-06-23 — five DEC-030 events, Zod v2 schemas, three chain invariants, artefact cross-reference table); `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md WinbackConvertedPayload` (v2.40 2026-06-23 — `prior_tenant_id` DEC-079 Option B amendment); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.10 items 1 and 3` (both `[x] Done — 2026-06-23`); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §44` (v1.26, 2026-06-23 — three DDL-layer chain invariant constraints: `ece_deletion_sla_respected`, `ece_winback_acv_requires_converted`, `ece_offboarding_before_deletion`); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §101` (v3.26.0 2026-06-23 — four stale references patched by §123). Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

## §124 · Cross-Reference Patch — OBSERVABILITY §57.10 item 5 (AMEND-OBS-E-001 · CC5.2 / CC6.1 / A1.1)

**Date:** 2026-06-27  **Author:** enterprise-builder (cloud worker)  **Trigger:** `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §57.10 item 5` (P1/M6, open since 2026-06-27 §57 authoring) required registering AMEND-OBS-E-001 in §79.4, creating R2 subfolder `compliance/evidence/amendments/monitoring/`, and adding to §80.4 Vanta mirror list. OBSERVABILITY §57.8 defined AMEND-OBS-E-001 as the monitoring-layer artefact for job 48 `amendment_rate_compliance_monitor` with SOC 2 criteria CC5.2/CC6.1/A1.1. This patch registers the artefact and closes the obligation.

### §124.1 Gap Description

OBSERVABILITY §57 (v5.4.0, 2026-06-27) established amendment rate compliance observability — job 48 `amendment_rate_compliance_monitor` (`0 7 1 * *`, monthly), three DEC-030 HMAC-chained events (`security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` CRITICAL/7yr, `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` CRITICAL/7yr, `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` LOW/1yr), and three alert rules (AL-AMEND-01/02/03). OBSERVABILITY §57.8 defined AMEND-OBS-E-001 — the annual monitoring-layer evidence artefact for job 48 run history — and §57.10 item 5 created a P1/M6 obligation to register it in §79.4, §80.3, and §80.4. ADM-E-001 and ADM-E-002 (outcome artefacts, registered §118) cover the _results_ of the amendment governance process; AMEND-OBS-E-001 covers the _monitoring infrastructure_ that provides continuous forensic assurance between annual outcome filings. Without this registration, the monitoring-layer evidence gap would leave ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 as the sole evidence of amendment control — acceptable for CC1.4 (chain compliance) but insufficient for CC5.2 (control activity continuity) and A1.1 (monitoring availability).

### §124.2 Source Table

| Document | Version | Relevance |
|---|---|---|
| `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §57.8` | v5.4.0 (2026-06-27) | Authoritative AMEND-OBS-E-001 definition: description, criteria, retention, R2 path, privacy floor, `form_api` NO ACCESS invariant |
| `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §57.10 item 5` | v5.4.0 (2026-06-27) | P1/M6 obligation: §79.4 + §80.3 + §80.4 registration + §124 patch |
| `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §118` | v3.43.0 (2026-06-26) | ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 companion outcome artefacts; `amendments/` R2 subfolder already exists |
| `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §80.3` | current | R2 folder registry — `amendments/` parent requires `monitoring/` subfolder addition |
| `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §80.4` | current | Vanta mirror list — AMEND-OBS-E-001 appended after ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 entry |

### §124.3 Artefact Summary

| Artefact ID | Criteria group | Specific criteria | Cadence | Retention | Owner | R2 path |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| AMEND-OBS-E-001 | CC5/CC6/A | CC5.2 / CC6.1 / A1.1 | Annual from M12 | 3 yr | compliance-officer + devops-lead | `amendments/monitoring/AMEND-OBS-E-001_<YYYY>.md` |

**§79.4 count pre-§124: 86. Post-§124: 87.**

### §124.4 §79.4 Row Addition

AMEND-OBS-E-001 inserted after ADM-E-002 and before REN-E-001 in the §79.4 master evidence table — maintaining the enterprise contract lifecycle order (expansion → amendment outcome → amendment monitoring → renewal).

**Evidence scope distinction:**

| Artefact | Layer | What it proves |
|---|---|---|
| ADM-E-001 | Outcome | All `enterprise.contract_amended` tier-upgrade events in the year carried `floor_respected: true` and `asc_606_treatment = 'prospective_type_2'` — proving each amendment was authorized at the point of occurrence |
| ADM-E-002 | Outcome | All `billing.rate_updated` events cross-reference to prior `enterprise.contract_amended` chain partner within 24h — proving TU-CHAIN-01 invariant held across the year |
| AMEND-OBS-E-001 | Monitoring | Job 48 ran monthly on schedule throughout the year and would have surfaced any TU-CHAIN-01 gap or floor breach via AL-AMEND-01/AL-AMEND-02 PagerDuty P0 — proving the forensic monitoring control was continuously available, not merely configured |

A SOC 2 Type II auditor requires both layers: outcome evidence (ADM-E-001/002) proves controls fired correctly when invoked; monitoring evidence (AMEND-OBS-E-001) proves the detective control that would have surfaced misses was operational throughout the observation period. This is the same evidence-completeness pattern established by CHN-OBS-E-001 / CHN-E-001, DEL-OBS-E-001 / DEL-E-001, LITH-OBS-E-001 / LITH-E-001, and PRICE-OBS-E-002 / PRICE-OBS-E-001.

### §124.5 §80.3 R2 Subfolder Addition

New `compliance/evidence/amendments/monitoring/` subfolder added to the R2 folder registry in §80.3. Parent `compliance/evidence/amendments/` already exists from §118 (ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002). The `monitoring/` child is a distinct access boundary:

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| R2 path | `compliance/evidence/amendments/monitoring/` |
| File pattern | `AMEND-OBS-E-001_<YYYY>.md` (one file per observation year) |
| `form_api` access | NO ACCESS |
| Write role | `form_system` (pg_cron service role collects source data); `compliance_officer` on annual filing |
| Retention | 3 yr (WORM Object Lock) |
| Region | EU (consistent with `amendments/` parent) |
| Added by | §124 (2026-06-27) |

### §124.6 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Update

AMEND-OBS-E-001 appended to the §80.4 Vanta mirror list after the ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 entry (§118). Upload cadence: annual, first filing at M12 first full year with job 48 in production. Zero-CRITICAL observation years require affirmative attestation filing (the `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` event chain for the year demonstrates monitoring continuity; absence of CRITICAL events is itself auditor-inspectable positive evidence).

### §124.9 Privacy Floor

All content in §124 relates to job 48 pg_cron operational metadata and aggregate DEC-030 event counts. No individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data appears in any §124 narrative, artefact definition, or evidence path. AMEND-OBS-E-001 payloads contain:
- `chain_gap_count`, `floor_breach_count`, `contracts_checked`, `events_checked` — integer aggregates only
- `billing_event_ids`, `breaching_contract_ids` — FORM-internal UUIDs (not customer-facing tenant names or employee identifiers)
- `form_api` NO ACCESS — `form_system` role only for data collection; no raw rate values, no employee identity, no GDPR Art. 9 data in any event payload

## §125 · Cross-Reference Patch — INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48.12 item 4 (AMEND-STALE Evidence · CC5.2 / CC6.1 / CC4.1 / CC7.2)

**Date:** 2026-06-27  **Author:** enterprise-builder (cloud worker)  **Trigger:** `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-48.12 item 4` (P1/M7, open since 2026-06-27 R-48 authoring) required registering six R-48 incident-triggered SOC 2 evidence artefacts in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4`, extending the §80.3 `amendments/monitoring/` R2 subfolder entry, and adding to the §80.4 Vanta mirror list. INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48.10 defined AMEND-STALE-E-001 through AMEND-STALE-E-006 as the incident-triggered artefact set for job 48 stale-recovery incidents, covering CC4.1/CC5.2/CC6.1/CC7.2/A1.1. This patch registers all six artefacts and closes the obligation.

---

### §125.1 Gap Description

R-48 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE v1.0, 2026-06-27) defined six incident-triggered SOC 2 evidence artefacts (AMEND-STALE-E-001 through AMEND-STALE-E-006) for the job 48 stale-recovery runbook. R-48.10 specified their R2 filing paths (`amendments/monitoring/R-48-stale-*-<incident_id>.md`), retention periods (3yr for E-001 and P1-clean E-006; 7yr for E-002/003/004/005 and P0-triggered E-006), and SOC 2 criteria coverage. R-48.12 item 4 explicitly required these artefacts to be registered in SOC2_READINESS.md — creating a documentation cross-reference gap between INCIDENT_RESPONSE and SOC2_READINESS with no engineering prerequisite.

**What was missing:**
- §79.4 master evidence table: 6 rows for AMEND-STALE-E-001 through AMEND-STALE-E-006 (count 87, needed 93)
- §80.3 `amendments/monitoring/` entry: covered AMEND-OBS-E-001 annual scope only; R-48 incident scope absent
- §80.4 Vanta mirror list: AMEND-OBS-E-001 present (§124); AMEND-STALE-E-001 through AMEND-STALE-E-006 absent

### §125.9 Privacy Floor

All AMEND-STALE evidence artefacts carry aggregate counts, booleans, FORM-internal UUIDs, and operational metadata only:

- **AMEND-STALE-E-001** (`pg_cron.job_run_details`): `jobname`, `scheduled_time`, `start_time`, `status`, `run_duration`, `stale_days` — no tenant or employee data
- **AMEND-STALE-E-002/E-004** (C2 chain gap queries): `billing_event_id` FORM-internal UUID + `tenant_id` UUID only — no rate values, no employee `user_id`, name, email, or GDPR Art. 9 data
- **AMEND-STALE-E-003/E-005** (C3 floor breach queries): `contract_id` FORM-internal UUID only — rate values commercially sensitive and restricted to `#security-restricted` channel; excluded from artefact
- **AMEND-STALE-E-006** (IC narrative): `stale_days` integer, H-classification enum (`H1`–`H4`), C2/C3 result booleans (`chain_gap_found`, `floor_breach_found`), AMEND-STALE-CHAIN-01 DEC-030 event-ID UUIDs — rate values explicitly excluded
- `form_api` NO ACCESS — `form_system` role only for all data collection
- `form-soc2-evidence` R2 bucket (EU-region, WORM Object Lock Governance) — inherits from parent `amendments/monitoring/`

## §126 · Cross-Reference Patch — Pilot Save Protocol Evidence Registration (SAVE-E-001 / SAVE-E-002 · CC3.2 / CC7.2 / CC9.2 / A1.1)

*v3.50.0 (2026-06-27)*

This section closes **COST_MODEL §46.9 item 5** (P0/M4) — the documentation registration obligation created when `docs/COST_MODEL.md §46` (v2.20.0, 2026-06-27) defined the Pilot CSM Save Protocol and specified two SOC 2 evidence artefacts (SAVE-E-001, SAVE-E-002) without registering them in SOC2_READINESS.md. Two artefacts registered (count 93 → 95), `pilots/save-protocol/` R2 subfolder added to §80.3, two Vanta mirror entries added to §80.4.

### §126.1 Artefacts Registered

**SAVE-E-001** — Annual `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` DEC-030 HMAC-chain export with pilot save outcomes. TSC criteria: CC3.2 / CC7.2 / CC9.2. Retention: 3yr. Collection: annual (Q4) from first save-protocol activation (est. M5+). Owner: compliance-officer. Path: `pilots/save-protocol/SAVE-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`. Zero-event years filed as affirmative attestation. Privacy floor: `pilot_id` UUID + aggregate NUMERIC rates only; no employee PII or GDPR Art. 9 data; `form_api` REVOKED. Primary definition: COST_MODEL §46.8.

**SAVE-E-002** — Annual `pg_cron.job_run_details WHERE jobname = 'pilot_activation_monitor'` run history. TSC criteria: CC7.2 / A1.1. Retention: 3yr. Collection: annual (Q4) from first full year with job 49 in production (est. M12+). Owner: compliance-officer + devops-lead. Path: `pilots/save-protocol/SAVE-E-002_<YYYY>.md`. Zero-gap years confirm T0-Alpha monitoring sentinel operated without >26h detection blind spot. Privacy floor: job operational metadata only (no `pilot_id`, `tenant_id`, `activation_rate`, or GDPR Art. 9 data); `form_api` REVOKED. Primary definition: COST_MODEL §46.8; OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6 job 49.

### §126.2 §80.3 R2 Folder Addition

`pilots/save-protocol/` subfolder added to the `compliance/evidence/` folder structure in §80.3. Covers SAVE-E-001 and SAVE-E-002 (both annual; 3yr WORM Object Lock Governance; `form_api` NO ACCESS; `form_system` role only).

### §126.3 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Addition

SAVE-E-001 and SAVE-E-002 added to the §80.4 Vanta mirror list. Upload cadence: annually — SAVE-E-001 from Q4 of first save-protocol activation year; SAVE-E-002 from Q4 of first full year with job 49 in production. Standard Vanta mirror-log entry required in `mirror-log/YYYY-MM.jsonl` per §80.4 protocol.

### §126.4 Implementation Checklist

| # | Task | Owner | Priority | Milestone | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Create `compliance/evidence/pilots/save-protocol/` folder on Cloudflare R2 `form-soc2-evidence` bucket; confirm `r2:form-api` has NO ACCESS (§80.3 bucket policy invariant). | devops-lead | **P0** | M4 | [ ] |
| 2 | File first SAVE-E-001 artefact after first full operational quarter where save protocol activated: query `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` DEC-030 events for the year; cross-reference `enterprise.pilot_saved`/`enterprise.pilot_abandoned` chain for outcomes; verify PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 continuity (HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 chain check); file at `pilots/save-protocol/SAVE-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`; SHA-256 hash; upload to R2; upload to Vanta (CC3.2/CC7.2/CC9.2); add to MASTER-INDEX. Privacy check: `pilot_id` UUIDs + aggregate rates only; no employee `user_id`. | compliance-officer | **P1** | Q4 following first save activation (est. M5+) | [ ] |
| 3 | File first SAVE-E-002 artefact after first full year with job 49 in production: export `pg_cron.job_run_details WHERE jobname = 'pilot_activation_monitor'` for the year; compute total runs, successful runs, freshness-window breach count; file at `pilots/save-protocol/SAVE-E-002_<YYYY>.md`; SHA-256 hash; upload to R2; upload to Vanta (CC7.2/A1.1); add to MASTER-INDEX. | compliance-officer + devops-lead | **P1** | Q4 of first full year with job 49 (est. M12+) | [ ] |
| 4 | Add SAVE-E-001 and SAVE-E-002 to §79.5 compliance calendar at Month O+12 (first annual filing); confirm §79.5 quarterly calendar does not require SAVE-E interim entries (both artefacts are annual, not quarterly). | compliance-officer | **P2** | Month O+1 calendar review | [ ] |

### §126.5 Privacy Floor Summary

All SAVE-E artefacts enforce the FORM enterprise privacy floor:
- `pilot_id` is a FORM-internal UUID — no mapping to tenant name, company name, or individual employee identity is included in any artefact
- `activation_rate` and `final_activation_rate` are aggregate NUMERIC (0.00–1.00) across all enrolled seats — no individual workout session, coaching exchange, body metric, or GDPR Art. 9 health value
- `pg_cron.job_run_details` operational metadata carries only scheduler-layer fields (`jobname`, `scheduled_time`, `start_time`, `status`, `run_duration`) — zero user data
- `form_api` REVOKED from `pilot_programs`, `session_completed`, `audit_log_events`, and `pg_cron.job_run_details`; pg_cron reads via `form_system` role only
- HR `tenant_manager` role excluded from all pilot save-protocol data per ENTERPRISE.md privacy floor

## §126.6 · PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC7.2 / A1.1 / CC3.2)

*v3.51.0 (2026-06-29)*

This subsection closes **INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-49.12 item 4** (P1/M5) — the documentation registration obligation created when `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-49` (v1.0, 2026-06-29) defined PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 as a per-incident IC narrative evidence artefact without registering it in SOC2_READINESS.md. One artefact registered (count 95 → 96); `pilots/save-protocol/` R2 subfolder confirmed as the storage location (already added in §126.2); one Vanta mirror entry added to §80.4.

**PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001** — Per-incident IC narrative for each R-49 (`pilot_activation_monitor` job 49 stale) activation. TSC criteria: CC7.2 / A1.1 / CC3.2. Retention: **7 years** when `save_protocol_gap_found = true` (T0-Alpha miss during stale window — primary CC3.2 risk-response evidence); **3 years** when `save_protocol_gap_found = false` (all-clear stale incident — operational restoration record). Collection: per incident, filed by IC within 48 hours of `system.pilot_activation_monitor_restored` emission at R-49 Step 6. Owner: compliance-officer + devops-lead. Path: `compliance/evidence/pilots/save-protocol/PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001_<incident_id>.md`.

**Contents of PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 narrative:**

1. `incident_id` (UUID — matches PILOT-ACT-STALE-CHAIN-01 `incident_id`)
2. `declared_at` — timestamp of `system.pilot_activation_monitor_stale_declared` emission
3. `restored_at` — timestamp of `system.pilot_activation_monitor_restored` emission
4. `stale_hours` — duration of job 49 staleness (from `stale_declared` payload)
5. `root_cause` — H1–H4 enum (from `restored` payload)
6. `day14_cohort_active` — boolean (CC7.2 monitoring scope attestation)
7. `save_protocol_gap_found` — boolean (CC3.2 risk-response gap attestation)
8. `save_protocol_gap_resolved` — boolean (CC3.2 compensating control attestation; required `true` when gap found)
9. `r49_c2_query_output` — R-49-C2 scope query result (active Day-14 pilot cohort count — aggregate integer; no `pilot_id` listed in artefact unless gap found)
10. `r49_c3_query_output` — R-49-C3 save-protocol predecessor check result (gap boolean; aggregate count only)
11. `fix_deployed_at` — datetime of job 49 restoration
12. IC signature: compliance-officer or devops-lead (PAM session UUID)

**Privacy floor:** PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 carries FORM-internal `incident_id` UUID, booleans, aggregate integers, H1–H4 enum, and operational timestamps — no employee `user_id`, `activation_rate` value, individual pilot seat data, tenant name, or GDPR Art. 9 health data. R-49-C2/C3 query outputs in the narrative are expressed as aggregate counts only; `pilot_id` UUIDs appear only in the IC's `#security-restricted` session and are never committed to the artefact file. `form_api` REVOKED from all source tables (`pilot_programs`, `session_completed`, `audit_log_events`, `pg_cron.job_run_details`).

**§80.3 R2 folder:** `pilots/save-protocol/` subfolder (added in §126.2) covers PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001. WORM Object Lock Governance; `form_api` NO ACCESS; `form_system` role only. Retention governed by `save_protocol_gap_found` boolean: 7yr Object Lock when `true`; 3yr Object Lock when `false`. IC sets retention class at upload time.

**§80.4 Vanta mirror entry:** PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 added to the §80.4 Vanta mirror list. Upload cadence: per incident, within 48 hours of R-49 Step 6 completion. Standard Vanta mirror-log entry required in `mirror-log/YYYY-MM.jsonl` per §80.4 protocol. CC7.2 / A1.1 / CC3.2 criteria tags applied. Privacy flag: FORM-internal operational data only — no PII or GDPR Art. 9 data; Vanta access: compliance-officer + security-engineer only.

**Implementation checklist addition (§126.4 extension):**

| # | Task | Owner | Priority | Milestone | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | File first PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 artefact after first R-49 activation: retrieve `system.pilot_activation_monitor_stale_declared` and `system.pilot_activation_monitor_restored` from DEC-030 chain for `incident_id`; run R-49-C2 and R-49-C3 queries; file narrative at `pilots/save-protocol/PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001_<incident_id>.md`; SHA-256 hash; upload to R2 with correct Object Lock retention class (`save_protocol_gap_found` → 7yr / no gap → 3yr); upload to Vanta (CC7.2/A1.1/CC3.2); add to MASTER-INDEX within 48h of `restored` emission. | compliance-officer + devops-lead | **P1** | Per incident (first activation est. M5+) | [ ] |

## §127 · Cross-Reference Patch — Pilot Monitor Stale Evidence Registration (CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-E-001 · WAU-DECLINE-STALE-E-001 · CC7.2 / A1.1 / CC3.2)

*v3.52.0 (2026-06-29)*

This section closes **OBSERVABILITY §59.10 item 4** (P1/M6) and **§60.10 item 4** (P1/M6), and mirrors **INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-50.12 item 4** (P1/M6) and **R-51.12 item 4** (P1/M6) — the documentation registration obligations created when `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §59.8` (v5.6.0, 2026-06-29) defined CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-E-001 and `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §60.8` (v5.6.0, 2026-06-29) defined WAU-DECLINE-STALE-E-001 as per-incident IC narrative evidence artefacts without registering them in SOC2_READINESS.md. Two artefacts registered (count 96 → 97 → 98); two new R2 subfolders established (`pilots/champion-login/` and `pilots/wau-decline/` — WORM Object Lock Governance; `form_api` NO ACCESS); two Vanta mirror entries added to §80.4.

---

### §127.1 CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-E-001 — Champion Login Monitor Stale IC Narrative

**CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-E-001** — Per-incident IC narrative for each R-50 (`champion_login_monitor` job 50 stale) activation. TSC criteria: CC7.2 / A1.1 / CC3.2. Retention: **7 years** when `t0b_gap_found = true` (T0-Beta trigger missed during stale window — primary CC3.2 risk-response evidence; a Day-45 pilot cohort crossed the stale window with zero champion session count and no save-protocol predecessor event); **3 years** when `t0b_gap_found = false` (all-clear stale incident — no Day-45 pilot cohort was missed during the stale period). Collection: per incident, filed by IC within 48 hours of `system.champion_login_monitor_restored` emission at R-50 Step 6. Owner: compliance-officer + devops-lead. Path: `compliance/evidence/pilots/champion-login/CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-E-001_<incident_id>.md`.

**Contents of CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-E-001 narrative:**

1. `incident_id` (UUID — matches CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-CHAIN-01 `incident_id`)
2. `declared_at` — timestamp of `system.champion_login_monitor_stale_declared` emission
3. `restored_at` — timestamp of `system.champion_login_monitor_restored` emission
4. `stale_hours` — duration of job 50 staleness (from `stale_declared` payload)
5. `root_cause` — H1–H4 enum (from `restored` payload)
6. `day45_cohort_active` — boolean (CC7.2 monitoring scope attestation; true if any pilot was in the Day-44–46 window during the stale period)
7. `t0b_gap_found` — boolean (CC3.2 risk-response gap attestation; true if a Day-45 pilot had zero champion sessions and PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 predecessor was absent)
8. `t0b_gap_resolved` — boolean (CC3.2 compensating control attestation; required `true` when gap found — CSM manually notified per R-50 §R-50.5)
9. `r50_c2_query_output` — R-50-C2 scope query result (active Day-44–46 pilot cohort count — aggregate integer; no `pilot_id` listed in artefact unless gap found)
10. `r50_c3_query_output` — R-50-C3 gap assessment result (champion session count per cohort pilot + save-protocol predecessor check — aggregate counts and boolean only; `pilot_id` UUIDs restricted to IC `#security-restricted` session, excluded from artefact)
11. `fix_deployed_at` — datetime of job 50 restoration
12. IC signature: compliance-officer or devops-lead (PAM session UUID)

**Privacy floor:** CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-E-001 carries FORM-internal `incident_id` UUID, booleans, aggregate integers, H1–H4 enum, and operational timestamps — no employee `user_id`, champion session content (session IDs or individual login timestamps), tenant name, contracted_seats value, or GDPR Art. 9 health data. R-50-C2/C3 query outputs in the narrative are expressed as aggregate counts only; `pilot_id` UUIDs appear only in the IC's `#security-restricted` session and are never committed to the artefact file. `form_api` REVOKED from all source tables (`pilot_programs`, `session_completed`, `audit_log_events`, `pg_cron.job_run_details`).

**§80.3 R2 folder:** `pilots/champion-login/` subfolder added (this section). WORM Object Lock Governance; `form_api` NO ACCESS; `form_system` role only. Retention governed by `t0b_gap_found` boolean: 7yr Object Lock when `true`; 3yr Object Lock when `false`. IC sets retention class at upload time.

**§80.4 Vanta mirror entry:** CHAMP-LOGIN-STALE-E-001 added to §80.4 Vanta mirror list (this section). Upload cadence: per incident, within 48 hours of R-50 Step 6 completion. Standard Vanta mirror-log entry required in `mirror-log/YYYY-MM.jsonl` per §80.4 protocol. CC7.2 / A1.1 / CC3.2 criteria tags applied. Privacy flag: FORM-internal operational data only — no PII or GDPR Art. 9 data; Vanta access: compliance-officer + security-engineer only.

---

### §127.2 WAU-DECLINE-STALE-E-001 — WAU Decline Monitor Stale IC Narrative

**WAU-DECLINE-STALE-E-001** — Per-incident IC narrative for each R-51 (`wau_decline_monitor` job 51 stale) activation. TSC criteria: CC7.2 / A1.1 / CC3.2. Retention: **7 years** when `t0g_gap_found = true` (T0-Gamma detection missed during stale window — primary CC3.2 risk-response evidence; job 51 is weekly so a 14–21 day stale window may span 2–3 missed Mondays, each representing a full week of WAU detection blindness); **3 years** when `t0g_gap_found = false` (all-clear stale incident — manual T0-Gamma SQL at R-51 Step 2 confirmed no 3-consecutive-week WAU decline threshold breach occurred during the gap). Collection: per incident, filed by IC within 48 hours of `system.wau_decline_monitor_restored` emission at R-51 Step 6. Owner: compliance-officer + devops-lead. Path: `compliance/evidence/pilots/wau-decline/WAU-DECLINE-STALE-E-001_<incident_id>.md`.

**Contents of WAU-DECLINE-STALE-E-001 narrative:**

1. `incident_id` (UUID — matches WAU-DECLINE-STALE-CHAIN-01 `incident_id`)
2. `declared_at` — timestamp of `system.wau_decline_monitor_stale_declared` emission
3. `restored_at` — timestamp of `system.wau_decline_monitor_restored` emission
4. `stale_days` — duration in days of job 51 staleness (weekly cadence — R-51-C1 thresholds: 8 / 14 / 21 days)
5. `missed_monday_count` — number of Monday detection windows missed (each represents one week of T0-Gamma blindness)
6. `root_cause` — H1–H4 enum (from `restored` payload)
7. `mature_pilot_cohort_active` — boolean (CC7.2 monitoring scope attestation; true if any pilot ≥ 28 days active with `contracted_seats >= 25` per OQ-WAU-OBS-01 advisory guard was present during the stale period)
8. `t0g_gap_found` — boolean (CC3.2 risk-response gap attestation; true if R-51-C3 manual SQL confirmed 3-consecutive-week WAU decline ≥ 10 pp and no PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 predecessor event existed for that pilot)
9. `t0g_gap_resolved` — boolean (CC3.2 compensating control attestation; required `true` when gap found; `t0g_gap_found` and `t0g_gap_resolved` are separate fields per R-51 v1.0 design principle for auditor clarity)
10. `r51_c2_query_output` — R-51-C2 scope query result (mature pilot count with seat guard — aggregate integer; OQ-WAU-OBS-01 advisory note if minimum seat guard active)
11. `r51_c3_query_output` — R-51-C3 gap assessment result (3-consecutive-week WAU decline SQL result + save-protocol predecessor check — aggregate boolean and integer only; no individual WAU values, no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`)
12. `fix_deployed_at` — datetime of job 51 restoration
13. IC signature: compliance-officer or devops-lead (PAM session UUID)

**Privacy floor:** WAU-DECLINE-STALE-E-001 carries FORM-internal `incident_id` UUID, booleans, aggregate integers, H1–H4 enum, and operational timestamps — no employee `user_id`, individual WAU values, session content, tenant name, contracted_seats value, or GDPR Art. 9 health data. R-51-C2/C3 query outputs in the narrative are expressed as aggregate counts and booleans only; `pilot_id` UUIDs appear only in the IC's `#security-restricted` session and are never committed to the artefact file. `form_api` REVOKED from all source tables (`pilot_programs`, `session_completed`, `audit_log_events`, `pg_cron.job_run_details`). OQ-WAU-OBS-01 advisory note included when minimum seat guard (`contracted_seats >= 25`) is applied in R-51-C2.

**§80.3 R2 folder:** `pilots/wau-decline/` subfolder added (this section). WORM Object Lock Governance; `form_api` NO ACCESS; `form_system` role only. Retention governed by `t0g_gap_found` boolean: 7yr Object Lock when `true` (T0-Gamma miss — worst-case 14–21 day blind spot); 3yr Object Lock when `false`. IC sets retention class at upload time.

**§80.4 Vanta mirror entry:** WAU-DECLINE-STALE-E-001 added to §80.4 Vanta mirror list (this section). Upload cadence: per incident, within 48 hours of R-51 Step 6 completion. Standard Vanta mirror-log entry required in `mirror-log/YYYY-MM.jsonl` per §80.4 protocol. CC7.2 / A1.1 / CC3.2 criteria tags applied. Privacy flag: FORM-internal operational data only — no PII or GDPR Art. 9 data; Vanta access: compliance-officer + security-engineer only. Auditor note: weekly cadence of job 51 means worst-case stale window is 14–21 days (2–3 missed Monday runs) — IC manual T0-Gamma SQL at R-51 Step 2 provides the compensating control evidence when `t0g_gap_found = false`.

## §128 · Q3 2026 Access Review — Pre-Execution Gate · OQ-AR-01 Resolution · OQ-TDD-01 Resolution (CC6.3 / CC6.4 / C1.2 / CC4.2)

*v3.53.0 (2026-06-29)*

This section closes **OQ-AR-01** (🟡 Open → 🟢 Resolved — pilot-tier tenant inclusion in §23.2.3 quarterly access review; P1, compliance-officer + customer-success; deadline 2026-07-17 pre-review gate) and **OQ-TDD-01** (🟡 Open → 🟢 Resolved — §67.7 destruction certificate DPA cross-reference specification; P1, compliance-officer; deadline before first certificate issuance). It also provides the structured pre-execution gate content for the Q3 2026 access review, due 2026-07-31, with a 14-day pre-gate at 2026-07-17 per `compliance/calendar/q3-2026-access-review.md` and AR-2026-Q2-01 remediation commitment.

---

### §128.1 OQ-AR-01 Resolution — Pilot-Tier Tenant Inclusion in §23.2.3

**Decision (compliance-officer + customer-success, 2026-06-29):** Pilot-tier tenants are reviewed under §23.2.3 using the **identical procedure** as production enterprise tenants. No lighter-weight alternative is adopted. The pilot designation is noted in the evidence artefact header and in the §23.2.3 result table (`tier` column), but this notation does not create a different control standard or allow any steps to be skipped.

**Rationale:**

1. **Real employee data under a live DPA.** Pilot tenants operate under FORM's free-trial DPA (30-day free trial terms) which contains Art. 28 processor obligations including access control requirements. The controller/processor relationship is fully live — the fact that no fee is paid does not reduce the data protection obligations or the SOC 2 auditor's expectation that FORM reviews all tenants for whom it processes employee personal data.

2. **SOC 2 auditor consistency expectation.** A lighter-weight procedure for pilot tenants would create a documented carve-out. A SOC 2 Type II auditor reviewing the Q3 access review artefact will note any tenants with active admin roles that received a different review procedure and will ask why. There is no auditor-accepted justification for treating pilot-tier employees' data with less access-control diligence than paid-tier employees.

3. **§23.2.3 query already returns pilot-tier rows.** The inactive-account sweep query in §23.2.3 joins `tenant_users` to `tenants` and filters on `t.tier`. Extending the filter from `= 'enterprise'` to `IN ('enterprise', 'pilot')` (§128.2) is a single-line change with no procedural overhead. The `tier` column value in the query result provides the auditor-visible pilot designation without requiring a separate section.

4. **Privacy floor maintained.** The §23.2.3 query returns only role/permission metadata — `email`, `role`, `last_sign_in_at`, `tenant_name`, `tier`. It does not return individual workout sessions, coaching exchanges, biometric values, or any GDPR Art. 9 health data. This is consistent with the reviewer scope limitation in §23.4 Step 1 and §65.4 Step 1: the reviewer has RLS-restricted access to role metadata only.

5. **Operational reality at Q3 2026.** As of 2026-06-29 there are zero active pilot tenants (pre-launch). If one or more 90-day pilots commence before 2026-07-31, their admin roles appear in the §128.2 query and are reviewed without additional process friction. Zero pilot rows returned = affirmative confirmation that no pilot access issues exist; N > 0 rows = the same inactive-account check applied as for enterprise.

**Impact on Q3 2026 evidence artefact (`compliance/access-review/2026-q3/access-review-2026-Q3.md`):**

| Q2 2026 field | Q3 2026 update |
|---|---|
| §65.3.2 "Enterprise tenant admin roles" section | Renamed to "Enterprise and pilot-tier tenant admin roles" (OQ-AR-01 decision, §128.1) |
| §23.2.3 query `WHERE t.tier = 'enterprise'` | Extended to `WHERE t.tier IN ('enterprise', 'pilot')` per §128.2 |
| Finding AR-2026-Q3-XX row (if any) | Note `tier` value in finding row — no separate finding created for pilot-tier vs. enterprise |
| CC5.3 control effectiveness row for SCIM deprovisioning → session revocation | Q3 provides first live validation (not pre-launch staging) if any SSO pilot is active; note result |

**OQ-AR-01 status:** 🟢 **Resolved** — 2026-06-29. No further open question on this topic. Compliance-officer and customer-success sign-off documented in this section. §65.11 Q3 forward plan row "Potentially 1–3 pilot tenants…reviewed per §23.2.3" is now the confirmed operational procedure.

---

### §128.2 Updated §23.2.3 Query for Q3 2026 (Pilot + Enterprise Scope)

The following query replaces the Q2 staging query in §65.4.4 for the Q3 2026 production execution. It extends the tier filter per the OQ-AR-01 resolution in §128.1.

```sql
-- Q3 2026 access review — enterprise + pilot tier tenant admin roles
-- Run against production Supabase (not staging) per §65.11 Q3 forward plan
-- Requires form_admin role (PAM break-glass elevation per SSO §24)
SELECT
  u.id              AS user_id,
  u.email,
  u.role,
  u.last_sign_in_at,
  t.name            AS tenant_name,
  t.tier,
  t.id              AS tenant_id,
  CASE
    WHEN u.last_sign_in_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
      OR u.last_sign_in_at IS NULL
    THEN 'INACTIVE — review required'
    ELSE 'Active'
  END               AS status
FROM tenant_users u
JOIN tenants t ON t.id = u.tenant_id
WHERE u.role IN ('tenant_manager', 'tenant_admin', 'tenant_hr')
  AND t.tier IN ('enterprise', 'pilot')   -- OQ-AR-01: pilot tier included from Q3 2026
  AND u.deleted_at IS NULL
ORDER BY t.tier DESC, t.name, u.role, u.last_sign_in_at NULLS FIRST;
```

**Expected Q3 results (as of 2026-06-29 pre-launch baseline):** 0 rows across both tiers. If 1+ pilot tenants are active by 2026-07-31, rows will appear under `tier = 'pilot'`. Zero rows returned for both tiers is a valid result and constitutes affirmative confirmation of no active admin accounts requiring review — document this in the artefact.

**Inactive account threshold:** >`90 days` since `last_sign_in_at`, or `last_sign_in_at IS NULL` (account provisioned but never logged in). Any flagged row is subject to the §23.4 Step 3 deprovisioning review within the 24-hour SLA.

**Privacy floor:** This query returns `email` (pseudonymous within FORM's internal systems), `role`, and timestamps only. It does not return health data, workout sessions, coaching turns, biometrics, or GDPR Art. 9 data. Reviewer access is restricted to `form_admin` read-only role via PAM session (§24.2); no `SELECT` on health tables is available under this role.

---

### §128.3 OQ-TDD-01 Resolution — Destruction Certificate DPA Cross-Reference Specification

**Decision (compliance-officer, 2026-06-29):** The §67.7 destruction certificate template shall include:

1. **DPA execution date** — already present in the template as `under the Data Processing Agreement dated {DPA date}` in the DELETION SCOPE block. No structural change required. The `{DPA date}` placeholder must be populated with the ISO 8601 date the enterprise DPA was executed (DocuSign completion date for the MSA DPA Exhibit).

2. **One-line processing activities summary** — a new `Processing activities covered:` line, inserted immediately after the DPA date reference in the DELETION SCOPE block, containing the approved boilerplate: *"Processing activities covered: on-device CV coaching, wearable integration, AI-assisted workout programming, and enterprise administration as specified in the Data Processing Agreement Exhibit A."*

3. **OMIT individual DPIA PA IDs (PA-01…PA-12)** — the DPIA processing activity identifiers from `docs/GDPR_DPIA.md` are technical artefacts designed for FORM's internal DPIA management, not for the customer-facing destruction certificate. Including them increases certificate length without improving the Art. 17 paper trail for a typical enterprise privacy officer or their legal counsel; the DPA Exhibit A is the operative document and the DPA date creates an unambiguous cross-reference. An enterprise DPO who needs the DPIA PA IDs can request the full DPIA artefact via the data room.

**Rationale:**

- **Art. 17(1)(b) compliance confirmation.** GDPR Art. 17(1)(b) requires erasure where personal data is no longer necessary for the purpose for which it was collected. The processing activities summary confirms to the customer's DPO which purposes have ended — without requiring them to cross-reference the DPA Exhibit themselves. The DPA execution date anchors which version of the DPA is being fulfilled and provides the contract-reference anchor an Art. 29 inquiry or regulatory audit would expect.

- **DPO readability.** A privacy officer receiving a destruction certificate is likely not a technical person. A one-line summary ("on-device CV coaching, wearable integration, AI-assisted workout programming") is immediately comprehensible and sufficient for their Art. 17 compliance file. A block of `PA-01 / PA-02 / ... / PA-12` identifiers adds length and forces cross-referencing a document they may not have easy access to.

- **Future-proof.** If FORM adds new processing activities to the DPA Exhibit in a future MSA amendment, the boilerplate "as specified in the Data Processing Agreement Exhibit A" automatically covers the updated scope without requiring the destruction certificate template to be revised. Hardcoding PA IDs would require template updates on every DPIA amendment.

**Template update (§67.7 DELETION SCOPE block — before TDD-P0-04 PDF draft execution):**

```
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DELETION SCOPE
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

This certificate confirms that FORM has permanently and
irreversibly deleted all personal data processed on behalf of
{Customer legal entity name} under the Data Processing Agreement
dated {DPA execution date — ISO 8601, e.g. 2026-08-15}.
Processing activities covered: on-device CV coaching, wearable
integration, AI-assisted workout programming, and enterprise
administration as specified in the Data Processing Agreement
Exhibit A, in accordance with GDPR Art. 28(3)(g) and
Art. 17(1)(b). All specified processing activities have now
concluded for this tenant as of the deletion date above.
```

**Implementation:** Before TDD-P0-04 (draft §67.7 template as fillable PDF, M6), the `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §67.7` Markdown template in this document serves as the authoritative specification. The PDF draft must match the wording above. No change to the AUDIT TRAIL or RETAINED DATA blocks is required.

**OQ-TDD-01 status:** 🟢 **Resolved** — 2026-06-29. Compliance-officer sign-off documented in this section. §67.10 OQ table row updated: OQ-TDD-01 🟡 Open → 🟢 Resolved (§128.3).

| **C1.2** — Dispose of confidential information using appropriate procedures | OQ-TDD-01 resolution in §128.3 specifies DPA date + processing activities summary in destruction certificate — strengthens Art. 17 paper trail | 🟢 Template spec updated |
| **CC9.2** — Vendor and customer commitments monitored | Pilot-tier tenants reviewed at same standard as paid enterprise confirms FORM's DPA commitments to pilot customers are operationally monitored | 🟢 Covered by OQ-AR-01 decision |

### §128.6 Implementation Checklist

## §129 · Cross-Reference Patch — Pilot Graduation Evidence Registration (GRAD-E-001 · CC3.2 / CC7.2 / A1.1)

**Date:** 2026-06-29  **Author:** compliance-officer (cloud worker)  **Trigger:** `docs/COST_MODEL.md §47.8 item 5` (P0/M5, open since 2026-06-29 §47 authoring) required registering one SOC 2 evidence artefact (GRAD-E-001), creating a new R2 subfolder (`pilots/graduation/`), and adding a Vanta mirror entry.

---

### §129.1 Obligation Closed

This section closes **COST_MODEL §47.8 item 5** (P0/M5) — the documentation registration obligation created when `docs/COST_MODEL.md §47` (v2.21.0, 2026-06-29) defined the Pilot Graduation Economics governance and specified one SOC 2 evidence artefact (GRAD-E-001) without registering it in SOC2_READINESS.md. One artefact registered (count 98 → 99); `pilots/graduation/` R2 subfolder added to §80.3; one Vanta mirror entry added to §80.4.

---

### §129.2 GRAD-E-001 Artefact

**GRAD-E-001** — Annual export of all `enterprise.pilot_graduated` and `enterprise.pilot_saved` events with corresponding `enterprise.contract_activated` events for the calendar year. TSC criteria: CC3.2 / CC7.2 / A1.1. Retention: 3 years. Collection: annual (Q4) from first graduating pilot (est. M5–M6). Owner: compliance-officer. Path: `compliance/evidence/pilots/graduation/GRAD-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`.

**Columns:** `pilot_id` (UUID), `graduation_date` (TIMESTAMPTZ), `graduation_path` (standard / post_save_protocol / direct), `activation_rate_at_graduation` (NUMERIC 0–1), `graduation_tier` (standard / conditional), `activation_bucket` (A / B / C), `acv_at_graduation_usd` (NUMERIC), `contract_activation_date` (TIMESTAMPTZ — from `enterprise.contract_activated`), `days_pilot_to_activation` (INT — computed: contract_activation_date minus pilot_start_date).

**Privacy floor:** `pilot_id` UUIDs and aggregate NUMERIC rates only; no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, workout session, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category health data; `form_api` REVOKED from all source tables (`audit_log_events`, `pilot_programs`, `enterprise_contracts`); `form_system` read-only for export job. Zero-event years filed as affirmative attestation.

**Primary definition:** `docs/COST_MODEL.md §47.7` (GRAD-E-001 SOC 2 evidence mapping — CC3.2/CC7.2/A1.1, annual Q4, 3yr). Source events: `enterprise.pilot_graduated` (HIGH/7yr) and `enterprise.contract_activated` (STANDARD/3yr) in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise Pilot Graduation events` (v2.59, 2026-06-29). Chain verifiable via HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 (GRAD-CHAIN-01/02 continuity).

---

Standard Vanta mirror-log entry required in `mirror-log/YYYY-MM.jsonl` per §80.4 protocol. CC3.2 / CC7.2 / A1.1 criteria tags applied. Privacy flag: `pilot_id` UUIDs + aggregate NUMERIC rates only; no PII or GDPR Art. 9 data; Vanta access: compliance-officer + security-engineer only.

## §130 · Cross-Reference Patch — Admin Reporting Pipeline Evidence Registration (ADMIN-RPT-E-001 · C1.1 / P4.1 / CC7.2)

**Date:** 2026-06-29  **Author:** compliance-officer (cloud worker)  **Trigger:** `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §62.9 item 3` (P1/M7, open since 2026-06-29 §62 authoring) required registering one SOC 2 evidence artefact (ADMIN-RPT-E-001), creating a new R2 subfolder (`enterprise/admin-reporting/`), and adding a Vanta mirror entry.

---

### §130.1 Obligation Closed

This section closes **OBSERVABILITY §62.9 item 3** (P1/M7) — the documentation registration obligation created when `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §62` (2026-06-29) defined the Admin Reporting Pipeline monitoring and specified one SOC 2 evidence artefact (ADMIN-RPT-E-001) without registering it in SOC2_READINESS.md. One artefact registered (count 99 → 100); `enterprise/admin-reporting/` R2 subfolder added to §80.3; one Vanta mirror entry added to §80.4.

---

### §130.2 ADMIN-RPT-E-001 Artefact

**ADMIN-RPT-E-001** — Annual export of `pg_cron.job_run_details` run history for jobs 52–55 (Admin Dashboard MV refresh jobs) plus `system.admin_mv_refreshed` DEC-030 HMAC-chained event count per `view_name` for the observation year. Confirms ADMIN-RPT-SLO-01 compliance (≤ 26h freshness gap per view per C1.1/P4.1); `suppressed_cell_count` summary confirms ADMIN-RPT-SLO-02 (k-anonymity guard active throughout year). TSC criteria: C1.1 / P4.1 / CC7.2. Retention: 3 years. Collection: annual (Q4) from first full year with pg_cron jobs 52–55 in production. Owner: compliance-officer + devops-lead. Path: `enterprise/admin-reporting/ADMIN-RPT-E-001-{YYYY}.json`. Zero-event years filed as affirmative attestation.

**Contents of ADMIN-RPT-E-001 artefact:**

1. `observation_year` (INT) — calendar year of the observation period
2. `job_run_summary` — array of 4 entries (one per `view_name`): `job_name` (TEXT), `total_runs` (INT), `successful_runs` (INT), `max_gap_hours` (NUMERIC) — max gap between consecutive successful runs; must be ≤ 26h per ADMIN-RPT-SLO-01
3. `mv_event_summary` — array of 4 entries (one per `view_name`): `view_name` (TEXT), `event_count` (INT), `suppressed_cell_count_total` (INT) — sum of `suppressed_cell_count` across all `system.admin_mv_refreshed` events for the year; non-null per ADMIN-RPT-SLO-02
4. `slo_01_compliant` (BOOLEAN) — `true` when all 4 views have `max_gap_hours ≤ 26`; otherwise `false` with non-compliant views listed
5. `slo_02_compliant` (BOOLEAN) — `true` when all 4 `suppressed_cell_count_total` values are non-null; always `true` if ADMIN-RPT-SLO-02 guard was active throughout the year
6. `hmac_chain_verified` (BOOLEAN) — `true` when HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 chain check passes on all `system.admin_mv_refreshed` events for the year

**Primary definition:** `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §62.9` (ADMIN-RPT-E-001 SOC 2 evidence mapping — C1.1/P4.1/CC7.2, annual, 3yr). Source events: `system.admin_mv_refreshed` (LOW/1yr) in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Admin Reporting Pipeline MV Refresh events` (v2.60, 2026-06-29). ADMIN-MV-CHAIN-01 advisory ordering invariant (within-night ordering; not HTTP-422-enforced). Stale recovery runbook: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-52`.

**Privacy floor:** `system.admin_mv_refreshed` carries only operational metadata (`view_name`, `tenant_row_count`, `suppressed_cell_count`, `refresh_duration_ms`) — no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, session data, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category health value is included. `tenant_row_count` is the count of tenant rows in the MV (org-level aggregate). `form_api` REVOKED from all four Admin Dashboard MV relations per `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §62.6`. HR `tenant_manager` role sees only aggregated MV summaries — zero individual employee access.

---

`enterprise/admin-reporting/` subfolder added to the `compliance/evidence/` folder structure in §80.3. Covers ADMIN-RPT-E-001 (annual; 3yr WORM Object Lock Governance; `form_api` NO ACCESS; `form_system` role only).

---

Standard Vanta mirror-log entry required in `mirror-log/YYYY-MM.jsonl` per §80.4 protocol. C1.1 / P4.1 / CC7.2 criteria tags applied. Privacy flag: operational pg_cron metadata + `system.admin_mv_refreshed` aggregate counts only; no individual employee PII or GDPR Art. 9 data; Vanta access: compliance-officer + security-engineer only.

This section closes two obligations:

1. **COST_MODEL §48.9 item 1** (P0/M13) — `enterprise.annual_nrr_bridge_filed` registered in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise NRR Bridge events` (v2.61, 2026-06-30). NRR-BRIDGE-INV-01 arithmetic invariant + Zod v2 `AnnualNrrBridgeFiledSchema` registered.
2. **COST_MODEL §48.9 item 6** (P1/M15) — NRR-BRIDGE-E-001 registered in §79.4 master evidence table (count 100 → 101); `enterprise/nrr/` R2 subfolder added to §80.3; Vanta mirror entry added to §80.4.

---

### §131.2 NRR-BRIDGE-E-001 Artefact

**NRR-BRIDGE-E-001** — Annual export of the `enterprise.annual_nrr_bridge_filed` DEC-030 HMAC-chained event for the calendar year, cross-referenced to the `NRR-BRIDGE-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` artefact via `bridge_hash` SHA-256. Fleet-level GRR/NRR bridge: `reporting_year`, `cohort_tenant_count`, six ARR components (`opening_arr_usd`, `retained_arr_usd`, `expansion_arr_usd`, `contraction_arr_usd`, `churned_arr_usd`, `closing_arr_usd`), `grr_pct`, `nrr_pct`, `logo_retention_rate_pct`, `green_fleet_pct_at_m12`, `filed_by`, `bridge_hash`. NRR-BRIDGE-INV-01 arithmetic invariant verified at emission. TSC criteria: CC4.1 / A1.1. Retention: 3 years. Collection: annual (Q1, covering prior year cohort). Owner: compliance-officer + data-engineer. Path: `compliance/evidence/nrr/NRR-BRIDGE-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`.

**CC4.1 auditor narrative:** `enterprise.annual_nrr_bridge_filed` is an immutable, HMAC-chained, arithmetic-verified annual record of FORM's enterprise customer retention performance. The NRR-BRIDGE-INV-01 invariant (HTTP 422 `NRR_BRIDGE_INV_01_RETENTION_CHECK` on arithmetic mismatch) ensures the bridge is internally consistent at the moment of filing — auditors can verify `grr_pct × opening_arr_usd ≈ retained_arr_usd` without accessing source tables. `bridge_hash` SHA-256 links the chain event to the archived CSV artefact tamper-evidently. Annual Q1 cadence aligns with SOC 2 Type II observation period; zero-renewal years filed as affirmative attestation with `cohort_tenant_count = 0`.

**A1.1 auditor narrative:** The annual NRR bridge confirms that FORM's enterprise service commitments (availability, CSM engagement, onboarding per `docs/ENTERPRISE_SLA.md`) translated to observable retention outcomes over the contracted service term. `logo_retention_rate_pct` (renewed logos / opening cohort count) and `nrr_pct` are fleet-level availability-outcome metrics. The `enterprise.contract_renewed` event chain (`docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise Contract Renewal events`, registered v2.23) provides per-renewal evidence; NRR-BRIDGE-E-001 aggregates these into an annual board-ready summary with auditor-verifiable arithmetic.

**Primary definition:** `docs/COST_MODEL.md §48.7` (NRR-BRIDGE-E-001 SOC 2 evidence mapping — CC4.1/A1.1, annual Q1, 3yr). Source event: `enterprise.annual_nrr_bridge_filed` (LOW/3yr) in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise NRR Bridge events` (v2.61, 2026-06-30). NRR-BRIDGE-INV-01 arithmetic invariant (±$1 tolerance) verified at `emit-audit-event` Worker layer.

**Privacy floor:** All twelve numeric payload fields are fleet-level aggregates — no per-tenant breakdown, no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category health value. `filed_by` is a FORM-internal role enum. `bridge_hash` is SHA-256 of the CSV artefact — no PII derivable. `enterprise_contracts` ACV and seat-count data underlying the bridge: `compliance_reviewer` role access only; `form_api` REVOKED. HR `tenant_manager` role has zero access to `enterprise_contracts` or `audit_log_events`.

---

Standard Vanta mirror-log entry required in `mirror-log/YYYY-MM.jsonl` per §80.4 protocol. CC4.1 / A1.1 criteria tags applied. Privacy flag: fleet-level ARR aggregates only — no per-tenant ACV, no employee `user_id`, no GDPR Art. 9 data; Vanta access: compliance-officer + security-engineer only.
