(2) `system.scim_mass_deprovision_monitor_restored` DEC-030 LOW event from IC after R-67 restoration (`incident_id`, `restored_at`, `root_cause` enum H1–H4, `stale_minutes_total`, `manual_mass_deprov_match_count` INT (≥ 0 at restoration), `r24_activated` BOOL); (3) SCIM-MASS-MON-STALE-CHAIN-01 predecessor assertion (HTTP 422 `SCIM_MASS_MON_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion enforced by `emit-audit-event` Worker); (4) IC runbook timestamp trace (R-67.3 detection T+0 → T+25 closure); (5) R-67-C2 scope query output (per-burst rows: `tenant_id` UUID, `window_start_10min`, `deprovisioned_in_window` INT, `seat_count` INT, `threshold`, `pct_of_seats`, `already_detected` BOOL, `r24_severity` enum — aggregate count of mass deprovisioning bursts that occurred during the stale window and were not detected by `fn_scim_mass_deprovision_check()`; `already_detected = true` rows were detected before job went stale; `already_detected = false` rows require P0-escalate and R-24 concurrent activation); (6) `pg_cron.job_run_details WHERE jobname = 'scim_mass_deprovision_check'` export covering the stale window; (7) CSM lead sign-off attestation if `initial_severity = p0_escalate` and R-24 was activated. **CC7.2 auditor narrative:** job 24 is FORM's near-real-time SCIM mass deprovisioning detection control (5-min cadence, CC7.2 anomalous-activity monitoring); `fn_scim_mass_deprovision_check()` queries `audit_log_events` for `scim.user_deprovisioned` events in 10-minute windows and fires AL-SCIM-MASS-01 via pg_net when ≥ 10% of a tenant's seats are deprovisioned in a window; any stale period means FORM had no automated CC7.2 detection of mass SCIM deprovisioning — an anomalous-activity monitoring gap; `manual_mass_deprov_match_count` in `stale_declared` is the IC-attested count of undetected mass deprovisioning bursts during the stale window; SCIM-MASS-MON-STALE-CHAIN-01 DEC-030 HMAC-chained event pair is tamper-evident proof of stale detection-and-remediation timing independently auditable per HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 (§93). **A1.1 auditor narrative:** A1.1 requires FORM to monitor for availability threats; SCIM-initiated mass deprovisioning is an enterprise availability threat — if ≥ 50% of a tenant's seats are deprovisioned in one window, all employees lose FORM access simultaneously; when job 24 goes stale, A1.1 availability-threat detection for this pattern is blind; `r24_activated = true` in `restored` confirms that R-24 (SCIM Mass Deprovisioning Emergency runbook) was activated concurrently when undetected bursts were found, bounding the A1.1 availability-threat impact window; severity P1 default (higher than companion stale runbooks R-65/R-66 P2 because A1.1 availability impact, not only CC7.2 monitoring gap) and P0-escalate if any `already_detected = false` bursts found. Privacy: `tenant_id` FORM-internal UUIDs + aggregate burst counts + seat-count integers + `incident_id` UUID + operational timestamps only — no individual `user_id`, deprovisioned user list, employee name, email, health value, GDPR Art. 9 data, or coaching content; `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`; `r2:form-api` REVOKED from `compliance/evidence/scim-mass-deprovision/` (§80.3 invariant). Source: AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.81 (2026-07-03, §SCIM Mass Deprovision Monitor Stale events); INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-67 (v3.33.0, 2026-07-03). Registered: §155 (SOC2_READINESS v3.81.0, 2026-07-03). 7yr retention. | Manual-event | Per-activation (one artefact per R-67 stale incident) | compliance-officer + security-engineer (CSM lead co-sign if `initial_severity = p0_escalate`) | `compliance/evidence/scim-mass-deprovision/scim-mass-mon-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` |
| **SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001** | CC7 | CC7.2 | Per-activation incident record for R-65 `siem_bridge_cr02_impossible_travel` (job 22, `*/5 * * * *`, 6-min freshness) stale events. Content: (1) `system.siem_cr02_stale_declared` DEC-030 HIGH event from IC PAM-elevated emission at R-65 T+0 (`incident_id` UUID, `confirmed_stale_since`, `stale_minutes`, `missed_runs_estimate`, `manual_cr02_match_count` INT (-1 = scope query not yet run at declaration), `initial_severity` enum); (2) `system.siem_cr02_restored` DEC-030 LOW event from IC after R-65 restoration (`incident_id`, `restored_at`, `root_cause` enum H1–H4, `stale_minutes_total`, `manual_cr02_match_count` INT (≥ 0 at restoration — count of impossible-travel login pairs confirmed missed during stale window), `security_engineer_sign_off` BOOL); (3) SIEM-CR02-STALE-CHAIN-01 predecessor assertion (HTTP 422 `SIEM_CR02_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion enforced by `emit-audit-event` Worker); (4) IC runbook timestamp trace (R-65.1 detection → R-65.5 recovery steps); (5) R-65-C2 scope query aggregate output (count of impossible-travel login pairs that occurred during the stale window and would have been detected by `fn_siem_bridge_cr02()` — sourced from the manual R-65-C2 self-JOIN against `audit_log_events`; 0 = no travel events confirmed missed; >0 = missed detections requiring security-engineer sign-off); (6) `pg_cron.job_run_details WHERE jobname = 'siem_bridge_cr02_impossible_travel'` export covering the stale window (confirms stale start time, missed-run timestamps, 5-min cadence miss count); (7) security-engineer sign-off attestation if `manual_cr02_match_count > 0` (P2-escalate protocol per R-65). **CC7.2 auditor narrative:** job 22 is FORM's near-real-time impossible-travel detection control (5-min cadence, CC7.2 anomalous-activity monitoring); `fn_siem_bridge_cr02()` self-JOINs `audit_log_events` for intercontinental login pairs within 2-hour windows and emits `siem.correlation_rule_matched` HIGH events via pg_net; any stale period means FORM had no automated CC7.2 detection of impossible travel — a SOC 2 anomalous-activity monitoring gap; `manual_cr02_match_count` in `stale_declared` is the IC-attested count of missed CR-02 impossible-travel detections during the stale window; `security_engineer_sign_off` in `stale_restored` confirms security-engineer reviewed the scope and accepted or escalated any missed matches; SIEM-CR02-STALE-CHAIN-01 DEC-030 HMAC-chained event pair is tamper-evident proof of stale detection-and-remediation timing independently auditable per HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 (§93). Privacy: actor SHA-256 pseudonym (`actor_user_sha256`) + aggregate login-pair counts + continent-pair strings + `incident_id` UUID + operational timestamps only — no plaintext `actor_user_id`, employee name, email, IP address, GDPR Art. 9 health data, or coaching content; `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`; `siem_country_continent` static reference table (249 countries, 7 continents) does not contain PII; `r2:form-api` REVOKED from `compliance/evidence/siem-bridge-stale/` (§80.3 invariant). Source: AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.79 (2026-07-03, §SIEM Bridge CR-02 Impossible Travel Monitor Stale events); INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-65 (v3.31.0, 2026-07-03). Registered: §153 (SOC2_READINESS v3.79.0, 2026-07-03). 7yr retention. | Manual-event | Per-activation (one artefact per R-65 stale incident) | compliance-officer + security-engineer (sign-off required if `manual_cr02_match_count > 0`) | `compliance/evidence/siem-bridge-stale/siem-cr02-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` |
| **PKJWT-E-001** | CC6 | CC6.6 | Quarterly export of all `sso.pkjwt_key_generated` and `sso.pkjwt_key_rotated` DEC-030 HMAC-chain events for the observation quarter, confirming: (1) every PKJWT-enabled tenant (`oidc_client_auth_method = 'private_key_jwt'`) has a documented key-generation or rotation record within the preceding 12 months (annual rotation policy per SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION §42.9); (2) private key material is absent from all chain event payloads (Zod schema constraint — `SsoPkjwtKeyGeneratedPayload` and `SsoPkjwtKeyRotatedPayload` contain `tenant_id` UUID, `kid` UUID, and timestamps only, no `private_key` field); (3) `rotation_reason` for any supplemental collection triggered by `rotation_reason: 'incident'` or `rotation_reason: 'customer_request'`. CC6.6 auditor narrative: FORM's `private_key_jwt` client authentication (DEC-097, RFC 7523) eliminates the shared-secret exposure risk inherent in `client_secret_post` by using per-tenant RSA-2048/EC P-256 asymmetric key pairs; private key is generated on FORM infrastructure, stored AES-256-GCM encrypted in `tenant_sso_configs.pkjwt_private_key_encrypted`, and never transmitted to the IdP. PKJWT-E-001 quarterly chain export demonstrates that: (a) every PKJWT tenant has a documented key-generation record in the immutable DEC-030 HMAC chain; (b) all keys in the covered tenants were rotated within the 12-month policy window; (c) private key material is structurally excluded from the evidence artefact by Zod v2 schema enforcement. Supplemental per-incident collection: triggered on any `sso.pkjwt_key_rotated` event with `rotation_reason: 'incident'` or `rotation_reason: 'customer_request'` — filed as `PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>_sup-<YYYYMMDD>.csv` within 48h. Zero-PKJWT-tenant-quarters filed as affirmative nil attestation. Privacy: `tenant_id` FORM-internal UUID + `kid` UUID + timestamps + `rotation_reason` enum only — no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; `r2:form-api` REVOKED from `compliance/evidence/sso/` (§80.3 invariant). Source: AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.73 (2026-07-02, §SSO-PKJ-Lifecycle); DEC-097 (2026-07-02); SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION §42.9 (2026-07-02). Registered: §145 (SOC2_READINESS v3.71.0, 2026-07-02). 7yr retention. | Manual-periodic | Quarterly; supplemental per `rotation_reason: 'incident'/'customer_request'` event; zero-PKJWT-tenant quarters as nil attestation | compliance-officer + security-engineer | `compliance/evidence/sso/PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |

## §143 · Cross-Reference Patch — INCIDENT_RESPONSE §R-54 · KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 Registration in §58.16 (CC6.8 · DEC-030 Chain Integrity)

> **Date:** 2026-07-02. **Trigger:** `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54.9` cross-reference obligation — "`docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §58` — KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 cross-reference | §R-54.7 item 5 (P1/M8) | 🟡 Pending." **Owner:** compliance-officer.

### §143.1 Purpose

`docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54` (v3.19.0, 2026-07-02 — OQ-KEY-01 Resolution) introduced `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` and `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` as DEC-030 event types, and the KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant. §R-54.7 item 5 created an obligation: register KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §58` (HMAC chain integrity controls section). This §143 fulfils that obligation via an inline §58.16 subsection addition and closes the §R-54.9 cross-reference row and §R-54.7 item 5 checklist entry in `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md` (v3.19.1, 2026-07-02).

### §143.2 Change Summary

| Document | Change | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §58` | Added §58.16 — Chain Ordering Invariant — KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 Cross-Reference: full invariant definition, five-event rotation chain diagram, SOC 2 CC6.8 mapping table, auditor SQL query, privacy floor note, registration note (AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md + Worker deployment remain P0/M7 pending operational tasks) | 🟢 **Done — this pass** |
| `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54.7 item 5` | `[ ]` → `[x] Done — SOC2_READINESS.md v3.69.0, §58.16 + §143, 2026-07-02` | 🟢 **Done — inline patch INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md v3.19.1** |
| `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54.9` cross-reference row | `🟡 Pending` → `🟢 Done — SOC2_READINESS.md v3.69.0, §58.16 + §143, 2026-07-02` | 🟢 **Done — inline patch INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md v3.19.1** |

### §143.3 What KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 Adds to §58 Chain Integrity

The three §58.10 DEC-030 rotation events (`admin.hmac_key_rotation_initiated`, `admin.hmac_key_rotated`, `admin.hmac_key_rotation_verified`) previously had no IC anchor in the chain. KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 adds an IC boundary pair: every future `HMAC_AUDIT_CHAIN_KEY` rotation produces five ordered events, with `incident_id` as the cross-reference key across all six IR-KEY-E-001 through IR-KEY-E-006 evidence artefacts and the PagerDuty incident timeline. SOC 2 CC6.8 auditors can now query the HMAC chain directly to confirm the rotation lifecycle was bookended by a formally opened and formally resolved incident control — without relying on external PagerDuty records as primary evidence.

| `emit-audit-event` Worker — deploy both events; KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 enforcement; integration tests A + B | §R-54.7 item 2 (P0/M7) | 🟢 **Done — 2026-07-02 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md v3.21.0, §R-55: `key-ic-chain-01.ts` enforcement spec + `index.ts` patch + Integration Tests A+B + PRE-55-E-001; operational deploy: §R-55.7 items 1–9, platform-engineer, M7)** |
| IR-KEY-E-001..E-006 evidence templates — `incident_id` JSON field + auditor cross-reference query | §R-54.7 item 4 (P1/M7) | 🟢 **Done — 2026-07-02 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md v3.20.0, R-21.9)** |

Cross-references: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54` (KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 authoritative definition: §R-54.4; event payload specs: §R-54.3; Zod v2 schemas: §R-54.5; step sequence integration: §R-54.6); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54.7 item 5` ([x] Done — 2026-07-02); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54.9` (🟢 Done — 2026-07-02); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §58.10` (three HMAC rotation DEC-030 events — IC chain now spans all five); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §58.12` (CC6.8 mapping — extended by §58.16 IC chain guarantee); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §30` (AL-KEY-01..04 trigger alert IDs referenced in KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 stale-window monitoring). Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

## §144 · Cross-Reference Patch — GEO-E-001 Registration (CC1.3 / CC3.1 · AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.72 · DEC-096)

> **Date:** 2026-07-02. **Trigger:** `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.72` (2026-07-02) introduced evidence artefact GEO-E-001 (CC1.3/CC3.1, annual, 7yr) as part of the `geo.contract_currency_set` HIGH/7yr DEC-030 event (DEC-096 UA enterprise billing in USD). `docs/COST_MODEL.md §56.9` had stated "No new SOC 2 evidence artefact is required for the billing currency decision itself", so `docs/COST_MODEL.md §56.12` contains no obligation row for SOC2_READINESS registration. This §144 closes the resulting gap. **Owner:** compliance-officer.

### §144.1 Purpose

`docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.72` (2026-07-02) — the `geo.contract_currency_set` HIGH/7yr DEC-030 event for DEC-096 (UA enterprise billing governance, USD only, EU entry order) — named a new SOC 2 evidence artefact: **GEO-E-001** (CC1.3/CC3.1, annual, 7yr, R2 path `compliance/evidence/geo/GEO-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`).

At the time COST_MODEL §56 (v2.29.0, 2026-07-02) was authored, §56.9 explicitly stated: *"No new SOC 2 evidence artefact is required for the billing currency decision itself (it is contractual, not a technical control)."* Consequently §56.12 (the DEC-096 cross-reference completion table) contains no row obligating SOC2_READINESS.md to register GEO-E-001. AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.72 then created the artefact anyway — closing the gap between the policy statement and the audit-log event model — but left SOC2_READINESS unnotified.

The result: GEO-E-001 is fully specified in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md and carries two named SOC 2 criteria (CC1.3, CC3.1), but does not appear anywhere in this document. Every other named artefact in the system has been registered via a cross-reference patch (§§136–143 all follow this pattern). This §144 closes that registration gap: §79.4 master evidence table row (count 111 → 112), §80.3 R2 subfolder description for `compliance/evidence/geo/`, §80.4 Vanta mirror entry, and implementation checklist.

### §144.2 GEO-E-001 Artefact Description

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Evidence ID** | GEO-E-001 |
| **TSC Domain** | CC1, CC3 |
| **Criteria** | CC1.3, CC3.1 |
| **Class** | Manual-event (annual) |
| **Cadence** | Annual (Q4 of each SOC 2 observation year) |
| **Retention** | 7 yr WORM Object Lock Governance |
| **R2 path** | `compliance/evidence/geo/GEO-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |
| **Owner** | compliance-officer |
| **Source** | `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md` (v2.72, 2026-07-02); DEC-096 (2026-07-02) |

**Artefact content:** Annual five-column CSV export confirming: (1) `billing_currency = 'USD'` on all `geo.contract_currency_set` DEC-030 HMAC-chain records for the observation year; (2) GEO-CHAIN-01 predecessor coverage — for every `enterprise.tenant_created` event in the period, a matching `geo.contract_currency_set` event with the same `tenant_id` precedes it in the DEC-030 HMAC chain. Columns: `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID), `contract_currency` ('USD'), `geo_chain_predecessor_present` (BOOL), `event_id` (UUID), `changed_at` (ISO 8601). Zero-enterprise-tenant-created years filed as nil attestation at `GEO-E-001_<YYYY>-nil.json`.

**CC1.3 auditor narrative:** `geo.contract_currency_set` is an immutable DEC-030 HMAC-chained record that management communicated the USD billing-policy commitment at the individual contract level before each enterprise tenant was onboarded. The annual CSV demonstrates 100% GEO-CHAIN-01 predecessor coverage — no tenant in the observation period was created without a documented, chain-verifiable currency commitment. Management policy (DEC-096) and individual-contract evidence (DEC-030 chain) are therefore aligned at the artefact level.

**CC3.1 auditor narrative:** FX risk is documented at the individual contract level (one `geo.contract_currency_set` event per tenant), not only at the policy level. The annual export confirms that the FX risk assessment decision (USD billing — DEC-096, 2026-07-02) was applied consistently to every new enterprise contract in the observation period. Auditors can verify risk assessment was performed before, not after, each tenant onboarding by confirming `geo_chain_predecessor_present = TRUE` for all rows.

**GEO-CHAIN-01 invariant:** `geo.contract_currency_set` MUST precede `enterprise.tenant_created` for the same `tenant_id` in the DEC-030 HMAC chain. A `enterprise.tenant_created` event without a preceding `geo.contract_currency_set` for the same `tenant_id` is a GEO-CHAIN-01 violation; the `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `GEO_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`. Source: `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.72` (2026-07-02).

**Privacy floor:** GEO-E-001 contains `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID), `contract_currency` ('USD' literal), `geo_chain_predecessor_present` (BOOL), `event_id` (UUID), `changed_at` (ISO 8601 timestamp). No individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching transcript, body-composition data, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. `r2:form-api` REVOKED from `compliance/evidence/geo/` per §80.3 bucket policy invariant. Vanta access: compliance-officer only.

| Subfolder | Purpose | Access | Retention |
|---|---|---|---|
| `compliance/evidence/geo/` | GEO-E-001 annual billing-currency governance export — `geo.contract_currency_set` DEC-030 HMAC-chain confirmations for the observation year; GEO-CHAIN-01 predecessor coverage assertion for all `enterprise.tenant_created` events in the period | `form_system` (compliance-officer write via PAM session); `compliance_reviewer` + `r2:compliance-officer` read; `r2:form-api` **NO ACCESS** | WORM Object Lock Governance, 7yr minimum |

Filename convention: annual file at `GEO-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` (e.g. `GEO-E-001_2026.csv`); nil attestation for zero-enterprise-tenant-created years at `GEO-E-001_<YYYY>-nil.json`.

## §145 · Cross-Reference Patch — PKJWT-E-001 Registration (CC6.6 · AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.73 · DEC-097)

> **Date:** 2026-07-02. **Trigger:** `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §42.12` cross-reference obligation — "`docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` — PKJWT-E-001 evidence artefact registration (CC6.6, quarterly, 7yr) | §42.11 item 12 | 🟡 Pending — M6." (v2.16, 2026-07-02). **Owner:** compliance-officer.

### §145.1 Purpose

`docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §SSO-PKJ-Lifecycle` (v2.73, 2026-07-02) — "three DEC-030 HMAC-chained SSO PKJWT Key Lifecycle events" — introduced SOC 2 evidence artefact PKJWT-E-001 (CC6.6, quarterly + per-incident, 7yr) and deferred its registration here to `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §42.12` item 5 / M6. This §145 closes that registration gap: §79.4 master evidence table row (count 112 → 113), §80.3 `sso/` subfolder update, §80.4 Vanta mirror entry, and implementation checklist. Closes `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §42.12` row 5.

### §145.2 Artefact Description

PKJWT-E-001 demonstrates that FORM's OIDC `private_key_jwt` client authentication (DEC-097, RFC 7523) is operated with documented, rotation-policy-compliant asymmetric keys, and that private key material is structurally excluded from the audit chain by Zod v2 schema enforcement.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Evidence ID** | PKJWT-E-001 |
| **TSC Criterion** | CC6.6 |
| **Retention** | 7 years (DEC-030 HIGH events `sso.pkjwt_key_generated` / `sso.pkjwt_key_rotated`) |
| **R2 path** | `compliance/evidence/sso/PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (quarterly primary); `PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>_sup-<YYYYMMDD>.csv` (per-incident supplement) |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly + per-incident supplement on `rotation_reason: 'incident'` or `rotation_reason: 'customer_request'`; nil attestation for zero-PKJWT-tenant quarters |

**Artefact content:** Quarterly CSV export of all `sso.pkjwt_key_generated` and `sso.pkjwt_key_rotated` DEC-030 HMAC-chain events for active PKJWT tenants (`oidc_client_auth_method = 'private_key_jwt'` at any point in the quarter). Columns: `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID), `kid` (UUID), `event_type` (`pkjwt_key_generated` | `pkjwt_key_rotated`), `algorithm` (RS256 | ES256), `key_generated_at` / `rotated_at` (ISO 8601), `rotation_reason` (scheduled | incident | customer_request — null for generation events), `key_expires_at` (ISO 8601). Two assertions per filing: (1) **rotation-policy assertion** — every row with `event_type = 'pkjwt_key_generated'` has a subsequent `pkjwt_key_rotated` or a `key_expires_at` more than 11 months in the future from the collection date (confirming within-12-month rotation compliance); (2) **payload-privacy assertion** — no `private_key` or `pkjwt_private_key_encrypted` field exists in any row (Zod schema constraint). Zero-PKJWT-tenant quarters filed as nil attestation at `PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>-nil.json`.

**CC6.6 auditor narrative:** CC6.6 requires that FORM implements logical access security controls over sensitive information during transmission. FORM's OIDC `private_key_jwt` client authentication (DEC-097, RFC 7523) addresses transmission risk by eliminating shared secrets from the IdP-to-FORM token exchange: instead of a `client_secret` transmitted in every POST body, FORM uses per-tenant RSA-2048 (RS256, default) or EC P-256 (ES256) asymmetric key pairs. The private key is generated and stored exclusively on FORM infrastructure (AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest in `tenant_sso_configs.pkjwt_private_key_encrypted`), and is never transmitted to the IdP or to the customer. Only the public key is published via the JWKS discovery endpoint (`GET /auth/oidc/{tenant_id}/.well-known/jwks.json`, KV-backed, `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600`). PKJWT-E-001 provides three CC6.6 assurances: (a) *key existence* — every PKJWT-enabled tenant has a DEC-030 HMAC-chain key-generation record, proving the asymmetric key pair was established before SSO was activated; (b) *rotation compliance* — all keys were rotated within the annual policy window (SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION §42.9), mitigating long-lived key risk; (c) *payload privacy* — private key material is structurally absent from the evidence artefact itself, confirmed by Zod v2 schema enforcement (`SsoPkjwtKeyGeneratedPayload` and `SsoPkjwtKeyRotatedPayload` Zod schemas contain no `private_key` field; the `emit-audit-event` Worker validates payload shape before chain write). The pg_cron job 58 (`pkjwt_key_expiry_sweep`, daily 09:00 UTC per OBSERVABILITY §12.6) provides 30-day advance warning (`sso.pkjwt_key_expiry_warning`, STANDARD/3yr) before any key reaches expiry — this advisory event is the operational belt-and-suspenders; the primary CC6.6 evidence is carried by the HIGH/7yr key-generation and rotation chain events.

**Privacy floor:** PKJWT-E-001 contains `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID), `kid` (UUID), `event_type` (enum), `algorithm` (RS256/ES256 enum), timestamps, and `rotation_reason` (enum or null). No individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching transcript, body-composition data, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. No private key material or `pkjwt_private_key_encrypted` column value appears in any DEC-030 chain event payload (Zod v2 schema constraint enforced at `emit-audit-event` Worker layer). `r2:form-api` has NO ACCESS to `compliance/evidence/sso/` (§80.3 bucket policy invariant). Vanta access: compliance-officer only.

### §145.3 §80.3 R2 Subfolder Note

**No new R2 subfolder required.** `compliance/evidence/sso/` already exists (created by §103, v3.28.0, 2026-06-23) with WORM Object Lock Governance, `r2:form-api` NO ACCESS invariant, and `compliance_reviewer` + `r2:compliance-officer` read access. PKJWT-E-001 artefacts are stored at `compliance/evidence/sso/PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` within this existing folder structure.

The §80.3 `sso/` folder listing has been updated inline in this document version to include `PKJWT-E-001 [quarterly + per-incident; 7yr] — added §145 (2026-07-02)`.

Filename conventions:
- Quarterly primary: `PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (e.g. `PKJWT-E-001_2026-Q3.csv` for first active-PKJWT-tenant quarter)
- Per-incident supplement: `PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>_sup-<YYYYMMDD>.csv`
- Nil attestation: `PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>-nil.json`

### §145.4 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Entry

PKJWT-E-001 added to Vanta mirror schedule (§80.4):

| Evidence ID | Upload cadence | TSC criteria | What Vanta receives |
|---|---|---|---|
| PKJWT-E-001 | Within 48h of quarterly collection; within 48h of each per-incident supplement; nil attestation within 48h of quarter-end for zero-PKJWT-tenant quarters | CC6.6 | `tenant_id` FORM-internal UUID, `kid` UUID, `event_type` enum, `algorithm` enum, timestamps, `rotation_reason` enum — no private key material, no employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data |

### §145.5 §79.4 Master Evidence Table Row

One row added to the §79.4 master evidence table (count 112 → 113), after GEO-E-001 (§144). See §142.5 above — the PKJWT-E-001 row has been inserted inline at that position in this document version.

If absent: create before first PKJWT tenant is activated. | devops-lead | **P1** | M5 (before first PKJWT tenant onboarding) | [ ] |
| 2 | Execute first PKJWT-E-001 quarterly collection: at end of first quarter containing at least one active PKJWT-enabled tenant — query `audit_log_events` for `sso.pkjwt_key_generated` and `sso.pkjwt_key_rotated` events where `tenant_sso_configs.oidc_client_auth_method = 'private_key_jwt'`; export six-column CSV (`tenant_id`, `kid`, `event_type`, `algorithm`, `key_generated_at`/`rotated_at`, `rotation_reason`, `key_expires_at`); include rotation-policy assertion and payload-privacy assertion as comment headers; file at `compliance/evidence/sso/PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv`; SHA-256 hash; upload to R2 (7yr WORM); upload to Vanta within 48h; add to MASTER-INDEX. Treat any PKJWT key rotation incident as a P2 SSO operational event per SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION §42.8 security controls. | compliance-officer + security-engineer | **P2** | On each incident-triggered rotation | [ ] |

this patch closes it ahead of schedule). §145.2 artefact description: six-column CSV schema (`tenant_id` UUID, `kid` UUID, `event_type` enum, `algorithm` RS256/ES256, timestamps, `rotation_reason` enum/null, `key_expires_at` ISO 8601); rotation-policy assertion (every generated key has subsequent rotation or > 11 months remaining at collection date); payload-privacy assertion (no `private_key` field in any row — Zod constraint); per-incident supplement path (`rotation_reason: 'incident'/'customer_request'` → `_sup-<YYYYMMDD>.csv` within 48h); nil attestation path for zero-PKJWT-tenant quarters; CC6.6 auditor narrative (three assurances: key existence, rotation compliance, payload privacy). §145.3 §80.3 note: no new R2 subfolder (`compliance/evidence/sso/` already exists from §103 v3.28.0); §80.3 `sso/` inline listing updated to include `PKJWT-E-001 [quarterly + per-incident; 7yr] — added §145 (2026-07-02)`. §145.4 §80.4 Vanta mirror entry (quarterly + per-incident within 48h; CC6.6; commercial metadata only — no private key material, no employee `user_id`, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 data; compliance-officer only). §145.5 §79.4 master evidence table row (count 112 → 113; inserted inline after GEO-E-001 (§144) in §142.5 table). §145.6 one cross-reference obligation closed (SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION §42.12 row 5 → 🟢 Done 2026-07-02). §145.7 four-item implementation checklist (1× P1 R2 subfolder verification M5; 1× P2 first quarterly collection post M5; 1× P2 per-incident supplement process; 1× P2 §15 compliance calendar). Privacy floor: PKJWT-E-001 contains `tenant_id` FORM-internal UUID + `kid` UUID + `event_type` enum + `algorithm` enum + timestamps + `rotation_reason` enum/null only — no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, body-composition data, coaching transcript, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; private key material structurally excluded from all chain event payloads by Zod v2 schema constraint (enforced at `emit-audit-event` Worker layer); `r2:form-api` REVOKED from `compliance/evidence/sso/`; Vanta access: compliance-officer only. Document header v3.70.0 → v3.71.0. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

## §146 · Cross-Reference Patch — §15 Compliance Calendar: FIDO2-E-001 / GEO-E-001 / PKJWT-E-001 (CC6.7 / CC1.3 / CC3.1 / CC6.6)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** Three P2 implementation checklist items deferred from §142, §144, and §145 — all requesting §15.1 compliance calendar additions for their respective artefacts. **Owner:** compliance-officer.

### §146.1 Purpose

Three cross-reference patches — §142 (FIDO2-E-001, CC6.7, 2026-07-02), §144 (GEO-E-001, CC1.3/CC3.1, 2026-07-02), and §145 (PKJWT-E-001, CC6.6, 2026-07-02) — each registered a new SOC 2 evidence artefact in the §79.4 master evidence table and each deferred one P2 task: adding the artefact's collection cadence to the §15.1 Master Compliance Calendar.

The three deferred items:
- `§142.7 item 4 [ ]` — "Add FIDO2-E-001 annual collection to §15 compliance calendar alongside CC6-E-PAM-004 annual policy refresh; add per-change trigger to PAM offboarding checklist (parallel to `pam-db-proxy` key rotation)"
- `§144.7 item 3 [ ]` — "Add GEO-E-001 to §15 compliance calendar: annual Q4 collection slot with compliance-officer as owner"
- `§145.7 item 4 [ ]` — "Add PKJWT-E-001 to §15 compliance calendar: quarterly collection slot (Q1 Jan 5 / Q2 Apr 5 / Q3 Jul 5 / Q4 Oct 5) with compliance-officer as owner; note nil-attestation path for zero-PKJWT-tenant quarters; add per-incident supplement reminder to P2 incident response checklist for SSO key rotation events"

This §146 closes all three by adding the required calendar rows inline to §15.1 and marking the checklist items done.

### §146.2 §15.1 Calendar Additions (Inline)

Four rows added to the §15.1 Master Compliance Calendar table (inline in this document version):

#### §146.2.1 FIDO2-E-001 Annual (CC6.7)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Month slot** | Q1 (Jan) — batched alongside CC6-E-PAM-004 annual policy snapshot refresh |
| **Activity** | Annual FIDO2-E-001 collection: four-component Cloudflare Access FIDO2 enrollment proof |
| **Cadence** | Annual (alongside CC6-E-PAM-004 annual policy refresh) |
| **Owner** | compliance-officer + security-engineer |
| **Evidence** | `compliance/evidence/pam/FIDO2-E-001_<YYYY-MM>.zip` (7yr WORM) |
| **SOC 2** | CC6.7 |

**Rationale for Q1 / January slot:** FIDO2-E-001 re-collects the same four-component enrollment proof on an annual cadence. Batching it alongside CC6-E-PAM-004 (annual break-glass policy Terraform snapshot) minimises compliance-officer overhead — both artefacts reference the `form-break-glass` Cloudflare Access policy and its ≤5 enrolled engineers. A January collection also ensures the annual FIDO2 evidence covers the prior SOC 2 observation year end.

#### §146.2.2 FIDO2-E-001 Per-Change Event-Triggered (CC6.7)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Month slot** | Event-triggered (roster change or hardware key replacement) |
| **Activity** | Per-change FIDO2-E-001 supplement: re-collect enrollment screenshots + `break-glass-policy.tf` diff for affected engineers |
| **Cadence** | Per-change; fires on every PAM offboarding (parallel to `pam-db-proxy` key rotation in offboarding checklist) |
| **Owner** | compliance-officer + security-engineer |
| **Evidence** | `compliance/evidence/pam/FIDO2-E-001_<YYYY-MM>_change-<YYYYMMDD>.zip` (7yr WORM) |
| **SOC 2** | CC6.7 |

**PAM offboarding checklist note:** The per-change trigger has been added to the PAM offboarding checklist as a parallel step alongside `pam-db-proxy` key rotation. This satisfies the specific obligation in §142.7 item 4.

#### §146.2.3 GEO-E-001 Annual Q4 (CC1.3 / CC3.1)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Month slot** | Q4 (Oct) — alongside Q4 control effectiveness review; complete before year-end |
| **Activity** | Annual GEO-E-001 collection: `geo.contract_currency_set` DEC-030 HMAC-chain export + GEO-CHAIN-01 predecessor coverage assertion |
| **Cadence** | Annual (Q4 of each SOC 2 observation year) |
| **Owner** | compliance-officer |
| **Evidence** | `compliance/evidence/geo/GEO-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` (7yr WORM); nil attestation at `GEO-E-001_<YYYY>-nil.json` for zero-onboarding years |
| **SOC 2** | CC1.3, CC3.1 |

**Nil-attestation note:** In any SOC 2 observation year where zero enterprise tenants were onboarded, the nil attestation is a positive control execution — not a gap. File `GEO-E-001_<YYYY>-nil.json` within 48h of year-end; upload to Vanta with the same timing as the primary artefact. CC1.3 and CC3.1 auditors accept the nil attestation as evidence that the GEO-CHAIN-01 invariant was monitored and that zero coverage events occurred in the period.

#### §146.2.4 PKJWT-E-001 Quarterly + Per-Incident (CC6.6)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Month slot** | Quarterly: Q1 Jan 5 / Q2 Apr 5 / Q3 Jul 5 / Q4 Oct 5 |
| **Activity** | Quarterly PKJWT-E-001 collection: `sso.pkjwt_key_generated` + `sso.pkjwt_key_rotated` DEC-030 chain export with rotation-policy and payload-privacy assertions |
| **Cadence** | Quarterly (fixed dates: Jan 5 / Apr 5 / Jul 5 / Oct 5); per-incident supplement on each `rotation_reason: 'incident'/'customer_request'` event |
| **Owner** | compliance-officer |
| **Evidence** | `compliance/evidence/sso/PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (7yr WORM); nil attestation at `PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>-nil.json`; supplement at `PKJWT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>_sup-<YYYYMMDD>.csv` |
| **SOC 2** | CC6.6 |

**Fixed-date rationale:** Jan 5 / Apr 5 / Jul 5 / Oct 5 provides a 5-day buffer from quarter-end to allow the `audit_log_events` query to cover the final days of the prior quarter without racing a midnight boundary. The 5th-of-month cadence avoids New Year's Day and aligns with the monthly evidence-checkpoint memo schedule.

**Per-incident supplement P2 reminder:** Any `sso.pkjwt_key_rotated` event with `rotation_reason: 'incident'` or `rotation_reason: 'customer_request'` triggers an out-of-band supplement within 48h regardless of calendar date. The P2 SSO key rotation incident response checklist (SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION §42.8) should reference this supplement obligation. This satisfies the specific requirement in §145.7 item 4.

## §147 · Cross-Reference Patch — PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC6.6 · AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.74 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-57)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-57.11 item 3 (P0) — "Register PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` evidence artefact registry (per-activation cadence, CC6.6, 7yr, owner: security-engineer + compliance-officer)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + security-engineer.

### §147.1 Background

`docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md` v3.22.0 (2026-07-03) added runbook R-57 for `pkjwt_key_expiry_sweep` (pg_cron job 58, schedule `0 9 * * *`, 26h freshness). R-57 is the companion stale-recovery runbook for the PKJWT key expiry sentinel introduced by OBSERVABILITY.md v5.14.0 (2026-07-02, §12.6 job 58 registration).

R-57.11 implementation checklist item 3 (P0) required registering the per-activation incident evidence artefact PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 in this document's §79.4 master evidence artefact registry, and updating §80.3 (R2 bucket folder structure) and §80.4 (Vanta mirror list).

Two companion events support this artefact:
- `system.pkjwt_expiry_sweep_stale_declared` (HIGH, 7yr, CC6.6) — IC PAM-elevated declaration at R-57 T+0
- `system.pkjwt_expiry_sweep_restored` (LOW, 3yr, CC6.6) — IC declaration after recovery (R-57 Step 5)

Both events are DEC-030 HMAC-chained. The PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant enforced by the `emit-audit-event` Worker ensures the declared event precedes the restored event for the same `incident_id` (HTTP 422 `PKJWT_SWEEP_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion). Registered in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md` v2.74 (R-57.11 item 1, 2026-07-03).

### §147.2 §79.4 Registration

PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 has been added to the §79.4 master evidence table (row 114) immediately before PKJWT-E-001 (row 113). See §79.4 for the complete artefact row.

**Summary:**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Evidence ID** | PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 |
| **TSC Domain** | CC6 |
| **Criterion** | CC6.6 |
| **Class** | Manual-event (incident-triggered) |
| **Cadence** | Per-activation (one artefact per R-57 stale incident) |
| **Retention** | 7yr WORM Object Lock (CC6.6 cryptographic monitoring gap) |
| **Owner** | security-engineer + compliance-officer |
| **Path** | `compliance/evidence/ir/pkjwt-sweep-stale/PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001-{incident_id}.txt` |
| **Privacy floor** | `incident_id` UUID + operational metadata + aggregate counts + H1–H4 enum only — no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data |

## §148 · Cross-Reference Patch — INVITE-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2 · AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.75 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-60)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-60.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register INVITE-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` evidence artefact registry (per-activation cadence, CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2, 7yr, owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + security-engineer.

### §148.1 Background

`docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md` v3.26.0 (2026-07-03) added runbook R-60 for `invite_expiry_sweep` (pg_cron job 10, schedule `30 2 * * *`, 26h freshness) and `invite_email_expiry_cleanup` (pg_cron job 12, schedule `0 4 * * *`, 26h freshness). R-60 is a combined stale-recovery runbook because job 12 depends on job 10 having set `tenant_invitations.status = 'expired'` before it can execute the 30-day GDPR Art. 17 email erasure (`invited_email = '[erased]'`).

R-60.12 implementation checklist item 3 (P1) required registering the per-activation incident evidence artefact INVITE-STALE-E-001 in this document's §79.4 master evidence artefact registry, and updating §80.3 (R2 bucket folder structure) and §80.4 (Vanta mirror list).

Two companion events support this artefact:
- `system.invite_expiry_sweep_stale_declared` (HIGH, 7yr, CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2) — IC PAM-elevated declaration at R-60 T+0; anchor event for INVITE-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant
- `system.invite_expiry_sweep_restored` (LOW, 3yr, CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2 advisory) — IC declaration after recovery (R-60 Step 5); terminal event for INVITE-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01

Both events are DEC-030 HMAC-chained. The INVITE-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant enforced by the `emit-audit-event` Worker ensures the declared event precedes the restored event for the same `incident_id` (HTTP 422 `INVITE_SWEEP_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion; co-activates R-05 HMAC chain inversion runbook). Registered in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md` v2.75 (R-60.12 item 1, 2026-07-03).

**P0 upgrade gate:** When `pii_at_risk_count > 0` (job 12 stale ≥ 37 days — at least one `invited_email` value has passed the 30-day erasure window without erasure), the incident severity upgrades to P0. `pii_at_risk_count > 0` constitutes a GDPR Art. 17 active breach → GDPR Art. 33 72h supervisory authority notification obligation. The `pii_at_risk_count` field is an aggregate INT count only — no `tenant_id`, `user_id`, `invited_email`, `invited_email_hash`, or GDPR Art. 9 data.

§80.3 `invite/` subfolder added to R2 `form-soc2-evidence` bucket structure (new path: `compliance/evidence/invite/invite-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`; WORM Object Lock Governance 7yr; `r2:form-api` NO ACCESS invariant). §80.4 Vanta mirror list updated: INVITE-STALE-E-001 upload within 48h of `system.invite_expiry_sweep_restored` emission; compliance-officer access only.

### §148.2 §79.4 Registration

INVITE-STALE-E-001 has been added to the §79.4 master evidence table (row 115) after PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 (row 114). See §79.4 for the complete artefact row.

**Summary:**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Evidence ID** | INVITE-STALE-E-001 |
| **TSC Domain** | CC6, P5 |
| **Criterion** | CC6.3, P5.1, P5.2 |
| **Class** | Manual-event (incident-triggered, IC PAM-elevated) |
| **Cadence** | Per-activation (one artefact per R-60 stale incident) |
| **Retention** | 7yr WORM Object Lock (CC6.3 timely access revocation + P5.1 PII access restriction + P5.2 PII disposal monitoring gap) |
| **Owner** | compliance-officer + security-engineer |
| **Path** | `compliance/evidence/invite/invite-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` |
| **Privacy floor** | `incident_id` UUID + `seat_inflation_count` INT ≥ 0 + `pii_at_risk_count` INT ≥ 0 + operational metadata + H1–H4 root-cause enum only — no `tenant_id`, `user_id`, `invited_email`, `invited_email_hash`, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data |

**CC6.3 auditor narrative:** CC6.3 requires timely removal of access when no longer needed. FORM's `invite_expiry_sweep` (pg_cron job 10) marks `tenant_invitations.status = 'expired'` and revokes `enterprise_seat_assignments.revoked_at` daily at 02:30 UTC. When job 10 becomes stale, expired invitations and their associated seat revocations are delayed — directly impairing FORM's CC6.3 timely-access-revocation control. INVITE-STALE-E-001 provides the incident anchor: the declaration event (`system.invite_expiry_sweep_stale_declared`) records the stale duration (`stale_hours_job10`), the seat-revocation backlog (`seat_inflation_count`), and the IC declaration timestamp. The recovery event (`system.invite_expiry_sweep_restored`) records the resolution method (root cause H1–H4) and the count of invites manually expired during the incident. Together they close the CC6.3 evidence gap for the stale period.

**P5.1 auditor narrative:** P5.1 requires restricting access to personal information to individuals with a legitimate need. Pending-invitation `invited_email` values in `tenant_invitations` remain accessible (not yet erased by job 12) after the 30-day post-expiry window. A stale job 10 prevents job 12 from executing its GDPR Art. 17 erasure, prolonging PII access beyond the retention window. `pii_at_risk_count` in the declaration event quantifies this risk as an aggregate INT count (no individual email values in any payload). INVITE-STALE-E-001 provides P5.1 evidence that FORM monitored and responded to every instance where PII access was prolonged beyond policy, and that the IC declared and resolved each incident under PAM elevation.

**P5.2 auditor narrative:** P5.2 requires disposing of personal data in accordance with the organisation's retention schedule. FORM's GDPR Art. 17 erasure schedule for `invited_email` is 30 days post-expiry (enforced by pg_cron job 12). A stale job 12 directly delays disposal. INVITE-STALE-E-001 provides P5.2 evidence that every disposal-delay incident was declared by an IC under PAM elevation, the aggregate `pii_at_risk_count` was recorded (privacy floor respected), and recovery was completed with `emails_erased_manually` count documented. The 7yr WORM retention for the declaration event ensures P5.2 monitoring evidence survives the full SOC 2 observation window and any subsequent litigation hold.

### §148.3 Cross-Reference Obligations Closed

| Obligation | Source | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-60.12 item 3 [ ]` — "Register INVITE-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` evidence artefact registry (per-activation cadence, CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2, 7yr, owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer)" | §R-60.12 item 3 (v3.26.0, 2026-07-03) | 🟢 **Done — 2026-07-03 (SOC2_READINESS.md v3.74.0, §148)** |

### §148.4 Implementation Checklist

## §149 · APIKEY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 Registration + INVITE-STALE-E-001 Gap Fix (CC6.4/CC7.2 · AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.76 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-61)

### §149.1 Background

`api_key_chain_monitor` (job 13, daily 03:00 UTC, 26h freshness, PagerDuty P1 `form-security` → security-engineer) was identified as the only remaining daily P1-alert pg_cron job without a companion stale recovery runbook in `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md`. R-61 closes this gap (v3.27.0, 2026-07-03). R-61 covers two security monitoring checks:

- **APIKEY-CHAIN-01** (`docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §31.6`): detects live `tenant_api_keys` rows with no DEC-030 audit event (`api_key.created` or `api_key.rotated`) in 90 days — potential DEC-030 audit trail bypass.
- **APIKEY-CHAIN-02** (`docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §31.6`): detects rotation-overlap without revocation — old key not revoked within 26h of rotation (APIKEY-SLO-03 breach, `ENTERPRISE_SLA.md §17.2`).

P0 upgrade conditions: `rotation_gap_count_at_declared > 0` (active CC6.4 credential exposure) or `bypass_check_count_at_declared > 0` (potential DEC-030 bypass) — both trigger co-active R-01 (security incident) and dual security-engineer + compliance-officer sign-off.

**INVITE-STALE-E-001 gap fix:** §148 (v3.74.0, 2026-07-03) documented INVITE-STALE-E-001 registration in the `*v3.26.1` INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md changelog (R-60.12 item 3), but the actual §79.4 table row was not inserted. Gap confirmed by inspecting lines 34324–34325: table went directly from PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 to PKJWT-E-001 with no INVITE row. Both rows are inserted by §149.2 (this section).

---

### §149.2 §79.4 Master Evidence Table Registration

Two new rows added to the §79.4 master evidence table, inserted after PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001:

1. **INVITE-STALE-E-001** (CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2) — per-activation artefact for R-60 stale incidents; `compliance/evidence/invite/invite-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`; 7yr WORM; compliance-officer + devops-lead; gap fix from §148.
2. **APIKEY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001** (CC6.4/CC7.2) — per-activation artefact for R-61 stale incidents; `compliance/evidence/api-key/apikey-chain-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`; 7yr WORM; security-engineer + compliance-officer; new this pass.

See §79.4 table rows for full description of each artefact's content, auditor narratives, and privacy floor.

---

### §149.3 §80.3 R2 Storage Addition

`api-key/` subfolder added to R2 primary store (`r2:form-compliance`). Access policy: `r2:form-api` NO ACCESS (same invariant as `ir/`, `sso/`, `pam/`, `geo/`, `invite/`). WORM Object Lock: 7yr minimum (CC6.4/CC7.2 evidence retention for APIKEY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001).

```
compliance/evidence/
├── ...
├── api-key/                                        ← NEW (v3.75.0, 2026-07-03)
│   └── apikey-chain-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/
│       ├── stale_declared.json          (system.apikey_chain_monitor_stale_declared event)
│       ├── r61_c1_run_history.csv       (pg_cron run history — job 13; no user data)
│       ├── r61_c2_rotation_gaps.json    (aggregate counts — no key_id, no key material)
│       ├── r61_c3_bypass_check.json     (aggregate unmatched_live_key_count — no key_id)
│       ├── compensating_sql.log         (P0 only — execution log; no key material logged)
│       ├── restoration_confirm.json     (R-61-C4 confirmation query result)
│       ├── restored.json               (system.apikey_chain_monitor_restored event)
│       └── sign_off.txt                (dual: security-engineer + compliance-officer)
└── ...
```

Access invariant (§80.3): `r2:form-api` is REVOKED from `compliance/evidence/api-key/` — no API key material can flow from the application layer into the evidence store.

## §150 · VBASE-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC7.2/A1.2 · AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.77 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-62)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-62.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register VBASE-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table (CC7.2/A1.2; per-activation; 7yr; `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vbase-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + security-engineer.

### §150.1 Background

`docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md` v3.28.0 (2026-07-03) added runbook R-62 for `victor_safety_baseline_refresh` (pg_cron job 14, schedule `0 1 * * *`, 26h freshness). Job 14 computes the per-trigger-category 30-day rolling safety flag rates and writes them to the `system.victor_safety_baselines` KV namespace (`vsafety:baseline:{trigger_category}:{date}`); these baselines are the denominators for FORM-VICTOR-002 (P1, 3× baseline), FORM-VICTOR-003 (P2, 5× baseline), and FORM-VICTOR-004 (P3, 10× baseline) PagerDuty alert thresholds.

R-62.12 implementation checklist item 3 (P1) required registering the per-activation incident evidence artefact VBASE-STALE-E-001 in this document's §79.4 master evidence artefact registry, and updating §80.3 (R2 bucket folder structure) and §80.4 (Vanta mirror list).

**Clinical safety significance:** A stale baseline during a period of elevated Victor safety flags risks inflating the 30-day rolling rate used as the threshold denominator — causing subsequent VICTOR-002/003/004 thresholds to be too permissive and creating a false-negative risk where a genuine dangerous-advice spike goes undetected. FORM-VICTOR-001 (P0, threshold-independent — any VT-03/04/05/06 trigger) is unaffected by baseline staleness. The `clinical_safety_review_required` field in `system.victor_baseline_refresh_restored` flags whether the IC must include a victor-safety-lead review attestation before P1 closure.

Two companion events registered in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md` v2.77 (2026-07-03):
- `system.victor_baseline_refresh_stale_declared` — HIGH / 7yr; DEC-030 HMAC-chained; emitted by IC (devops-lead, PAM-elevated) at R-62 T+0
- `system.victor_baseline_refresh_restored` — LOW / 3yr; DEC-030 HMAC-chained; emitted by IC after R-62 restoration confirmed

VBASE-REFRESH-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `system.victor_baseline_refresh_restored` blocked (HTTP 422 `VBASE_REFRESH_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) without prior `system.victor_baseline_refresh_stale_declared` for same `incident_id`. Enforced by `emit-audit-event` Worker.

H5 root cause (`kv_write_failure`) is unique to R-62: pg_cron shows `succeeded` (job ran) but the KV write to `system.victor_safety_baselines` failed silently. R-62-C2 (Wrangler KV probe) is required in addition to R-62-C1 (pg_cron run history) to discriminate H5 from H1–H4.

`compliance/evidence/victor-safety/` subfolder already registered in §80.3 by §106 (2026-06-25). Updated to include VBASE-STALE-E-001 at path `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vbase-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`. `r2:form-api` NO ACCESS invariant inherited. §80.4 Vanta mirror list updated: VBASE-STALE-E-001 upload within 48h of `system.victor_baseline_refresh_restored` emission.

### §150.2 §79.4 Registration

One new row added to the §79.4 master evidence table after APIKEY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 (row 116 → row 117). See §79.4 table row inserted above for the full inline artefact entry.

**Summary:**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Evidence ID** | VBASE-STALE-E-001 |
| **TSC Domain** | CC7, A1 |
| **Criterion** | CC7.2, A1.2 |
| **Class** | Manual-event |
| **Cadence** | Per-activation (one artefact per R-62 stale incident) |
| **Retention** | 7 years (WORM Object Lock) |
| **Owner** | security-engineer + compliance-officer |
| **Path** | `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vbase-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` |
| **Privacy floor** | `incident_id` UUID + `safety_incident_count_at_declared` INT + `missing_categories` string[] + `stale_hours` INT + operational metadata + H1–H5 root-cause enum only — no `user_id`, `session_id`, coaching content, body composition, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data |

### §150.3 §80.3 R2 Storage Update

`victor-safety/` subfolder already registered in §80.3 by §106 (2026-06-25) containing `VSAFETY-E-001/002/003/004 [periodic]`. No new subfolder required — VBASE-STALE-E-001 lives under the existing parent at `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vbase-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`. Updated §80.3 entry (see inline update above):

```
  victor-safety/ (Victor AI clinical safety observability — VSAFETY-E-001/002/003/004 [periodic]; VBASE-STALE-E-001 [per-incident; R-62 activation-triggered; 7yr WORM]; VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 [per-incident; R-63 activation-triggered; 7yr WORM; clinical-safety sign-off required if R-23 co-activated]; form-api NO ACCESS) — §105 reference; §80.3 entry added retroactively by §106 (2026-06-25); VBASE-STALE-E-001 added by §150 (2026-07-03); VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 added by §151 (2026-07-03)
```

WORM Object Lock Governance 7yr minimum for VBASE-STALE-E-001 (CC7.2/A1.2 monitoring calibration gap record). `r2:form-api` NO ACCESS invariant applies — `form_api` credential cannot read or write any file in `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/`. IC PAM-elevated access only for `emit-audit-event` Worker calls during R-62 incident lifecycle.

### §150.4 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Update

Upload label convention: `VBASE-STALE-E-001_<YYYY-MM-DD>_<incident_id_short>` — same format as INVITE-STALE-E-001 and APIKEY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 (§149.4 convention).

## §151 · VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC7.4/A1.2 · AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.78 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-63)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-63.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table (CC7.4/A1.2; per-activation; 7yr; `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vsafety-chain-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + devops-lead + clinical-safety.

### §151.1 Background

`victor_safety_chain_monitor` (job 15, `30 0 * * *`, daily 00:30 UTC) is the pg_cron automated daily check that two clinical-safety audit chain invariants hold:

- **VSAFETY-CHAIN-01** — no P0 Victor AI safety incident (`ai.safety_incident_opened`, severity=P0) remains open for > 60 min without a matching `ai.safety_incident_contained` event for the same `incident_id`.
- **VSAFETY-CHAIN-02** — Victor AI coach is not left disabled (`ai.victor_disabled`) for > 48 h without a matching `ai.victor_reenabled` event.

When job 15 goes stale (past its 26h freshness window), FORM has no automated chain-violation detection for these two conditions. R-63.12 implementation checklist item 3 (P1) required registering the per-activation incident evidence artefact VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 in this document's §79.4 master evidence artefact registry, and updating §80.3 (R2 bucket folder structure) and §80.4 (Vanta mirror list).

### §151.2 §79.4 Registration

> See §79.4 master evidence table — VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 row inserted after VBASE-STALE-E-001 (inline update above).

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Evidence ID** | VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 |
| **SOC 2 Control families** | CC7, A1 |
| **Specific criteria** | CC7.4, A1.2 |
| **Cadence** | Per-activation (one artefact per R-63 stale incident) |
| **Retention** | 7 years WORM — Cloudflare R2 `form-soc2-evidence` |
| **R2 path** | `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vsafety-chain-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` |
| **Owner** | devops-lead + compliance-officer + clinical-safety (sign-off if R-23 co-activated) |
| **Clinical-safety sign-off** | **Required** if R-23 (Victor AI safety incident) was co-activated during the stale incident |
| **Source documents** | AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.78; INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-63 (v3.29.0) |

### §151.3 §80.3 R2 Storage Update

`victor-safety/` subfolder already registered in §80.3 by §106 (2026-06-25) containing `VSAFETY-E-001/002/003/004 [periodic]`, and updated by §150 (2026-07-03) to include `VBASE-STALE-E-001`. No new subfolder required — VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 lives under the existing parent at `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vsafety-chain-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`. Updated §80.3 entry (see inline update above in §80.3):

```
  victor-safety/ (...; VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 [per-incident; R-63 activation-triggered; 7yr WORM; clinical-safety sign-off required if R-23 co-activated]; form-api NO ACCESS)
  — §151 (2026-07-03)
```

WORM Object Lock Governance 7yr minimum for VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 (CC7.4/A1.2 clinical-safety chain integrity monitoring gap record). `r2:form-api` NO ACCESS invariant applies — `form_api` credential cannot read or write any file in `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/`. IC PAM-elevated access only for `emit-audit-event` Worker calls during R-63 incident lifecycle.

### §151.4 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Update

| Artefact ID | Upload trigger | Retention | Criteria | Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 | `system.victor_safety_chain_monitor_restored` emission | 7yr | CC7.4/A1.2 | New — R-63 per-activation artefact; `compliance/evidence/victor-safety/vsafety-chain-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` R2 path; upload within 48h of restored event; include clinical-safety sign-off attestation if R-23 was co-activated during the stale incident; upload blocked until sign-off obtained when `r23_co_activated = true` |

Upload label convention: `VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001_<YYYY-MM-DD>_<incident_id_short>` — consistent with VBASE-STALE-E-001 and APIKEY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 format conventions (§150.4 / §149.4).

## §152 · PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC6.6 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-64)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-64.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table (CC6.6; per-activation; 7yr; `compliance/evidence/pam/pam-bg-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + devops-lead.

### §152.1 Background

`pam_bg_review_alert` (job 21, `0 8 * * *`, daily 08:00 UTC) is the pg_cron automated daily check that enforces FORM's 72-hour break-glass post-hoc review SLA: any `pam_break_glass_reviews` row with `review_due_at < now() AND reviewed_at IS NULL` triggers PagerDuty P1 AL-PAM-BG-01 → compliance-officer.

When job 21 goes stale (past its 26h freshness window), FORM has no automated detection of overdue break-glass review SLA violations. R-64.12 implementation checklist item 3 (P1) required registering the per-activation incident evidence artefact PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 in this document's §79.4 master evidence artefact registry, and updating §80.3 (R2 bucket folder structure) and §80.4 (Vanta mirror list).

### §152.2 §79.4 Registration

> See §79.4 master evidence table — PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 row inserted after VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 (count 118 → 119; inline update above).

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Evidence ID** | PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 |
| **SOC 2 Control families** | CC6 |
| **Specific criteria** | CC6.6 |
| **Cadence** | Per-activation (one artefact per R-64 stale incident) |
| **Retention** | 7 years WORM — Cloudflare R2 `form-soc2-evidence` |
| **R2 path** | `compliance/evidence/pam/pam-bg-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` |
| **Owner** | compliance-officer + devops-lead |
| **Compliance-officer sign-off** | **Required** if R-64-C2 returned ≥ 1 row (overdue break-glass reviews existed during stale window) |
| **Source document** | INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-64 (v3.30.0, 2026-07-03) |

### §152.3 §80.3 R2 Storage Update

`pam/` subfolder already registered in §80.3 by §107 (2026-06-25) containing `CC6-E-PAM-001/002/003/004 + CC7-E-PAM-001`, and updated by §142 (2026-07-02) to include `FIDO2-E-001`. No new subfolder required — PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 lives under the existing parent at `compliance/evidence/pam/pam-bg-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`. Updated §80.3 entry (see inline update above in §80.3):

```
  pam/ (...; PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 [per-incident; R-64 activation-triggered; 7yr WORM; compliance-officer sign-off required if overdue reviews confirmed]; form-api NO ACCESS)
  — §152 (2026-07-03)
```

WORM Object Lock Governance 7yr minimum for PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 (CC6.6 PAM break-glass post-hoc review SLA monitoring gap record). `r2:form-api` NO ACCESS invariant applies — consistent with all PAM evidence subfolders per §80.3 and §107.4.

Upload label convention: `PAM-BG-STALE-E-001_<YYYY-MM-DD>_<incident_id_short>` — consistent with VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 and VBASE-STALE-E-001 format conventions (§151.4 / §150.4).

## §153 · SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC7.2 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-65)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-65.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table (CC7.2; per-activation; 7yr; `compliance/evidence/siem-bridge-stale/siem-cr02-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + security-engineer.

### §153.1 Background

`docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-65` (v3.31.0, 2026-07-03) introduced the companion stale recovery runbook for pg_cron job 22 (`siem_bridge_cr02_impossible_travel`, `*/5 * * * *`). Job 22 runs the Supabase SECURITY DEFINER function `fn_siem_bridge_cr02()`, which self-JOINs `audit_log_events` for intercontinental login pairs (SSO login + auth token events) within 2-hour windows and emits `siem.correlation_rule_matched` HIGH events via pg_net to the `emit-audit-event` Worker. Actor identity is SHA-256 pseudonymized. The static reference table `siem_country_continent` (249 countries, 7 continents) is used for continent mapping; `app.emit_audit_event_worker_url` GUC is required.

When job 22 goes stale (past its 6-min freshness window), FORM has no automated near-real-time detection of impossible travel login events. This is a SOC 2 CC7.2 (Anomalous Activity Monitoring) gap — the 5-min cadence exists specifically to minimize the window between an impossible-travel event occurring and FORM detecting it. R-65.12 implementation checklist item 3 (P1) required registering the per-activation incident evidence artefact SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001 in this document's §79.4 master evidence artefact registry, and updating §80.3 (R2 bucket folder structure) and §80.4 (Vanta mirror list).

### §153.2 §79.4 Registration

> See §79.4 master evidence table — SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001 row inserted after PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 (count 119 → 120; inline update above).

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Evidence ID** | SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001 |
| **SOC 2 Control families** | CC7 |
| **Specific criteria** | CC7.2 |
| **Cadence** | Per-activation (one artefact per R-65 stale incident) |
| **Retention** | 7 years WORM — Cloudflare R2 `form-soc2-evidence` |
| **R2 path** | `compliance/evidence/siem-bridge-stale/siem-cr02-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` |
| **Owner** | compliance-officer + security-engineer |
| **Security-engineer sign-off** | **Required** if R-65-C2 returned `manual_cr02_match_count > 0` (impossible travel events occurred during the stale window) |
| **Source document** | INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-65 (v3.31.0, 2026-07-03) |

New `siem-bridge-stale/` subfolder required — no prior parent existed under `compliance/evidence/` for SIEM correlation rule stale evidence. New subfolder registered in §80.3 (see inline update above in §80.3):

```
  siem-bridge-stale/  (SIEM Bridge CR-02 impossible travel monitor stale-incident evidence —
    SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001 [per-incident; R-65 activation-triggered; 7yr WORM; security-engineer
    sign-off required if manual_cr02_match_count > 0]; form-api NO ACCESS)
  — §153 (2026-07-03)
```

WORM Object Lock Governance 7yr minimum for SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001 (CC7.2 SIEM Bridge impossible-travel detection monitoring gap record). `r2:form-api` NO ACCESS invariant applies — consistent with all security monitoring evidence subfolders per §80.3 and the established pattern for stale-incident evidence folders (§147, §150, §151, §152).

Upload label convention: `SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001_<YYYY-MM-DD>_<incident_id_short>` — consistent with PAM-BG-STALE-E-001 and VSAFETY-CHAIN-STALE-E-001 format conventions (§152.4 / §151.4).

## §154 · SIEM-CR03-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC7.2 / CC6.6 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-66)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-66.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register SIEM-CR03-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table (CC7.2/CC6.6; per-activation; 7yr; `compliance/evidence/siem-bridge-stale/siem-cr03-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + security-engineer.

### §154.1 Background

Job 23 (`siem_bridge_cr03_priv_escalation`, `*/5 * * * *`) is FORM's near-real-time CR-03 detector: it JOINs `audit_log_events` for `pam.elevation_denied` → `pam.session_started` for the same actor within 30 minutes and emits `siem.correlation_rule_matched` HIGH via pg_net. When job 23 goes stale, FORM's CC7.2 anomalous-activity detection and CC6.6 privileged-access monitoring for this pattern are blind. INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-66 is the companion stale recovery runbook for job 23; it requires a per-activation SIEM-CR03-STALE-E-001 evidence artefact capturing the DEC-030 HMAC chain (`system.siem_cr03_stale_declared` HIGH + `system.siem_cr03_restored` LOW), R-66-C2 manual detection output (`actor_user_sha256` pseudonym counts only), and (where `manual_cr03_match_count > 0`) security-engineer sign-off. SIEM-CR03-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `siem_cr03_restored` blocked (HTTP 422 `SIEM_CR03_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) without prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; co-activates R-05.

### §154.2 §79.4 Registration

SIEM-CR03-STALE-E-001 registered in §79.4 master evidence table (count 120 → 121). Evidence class: per-activation (one artefact per R-66 stale incident). Retention: 7yr WORM. TSC: CC7.2, CC6.6. R2 path: `compliance/evidence/siem-bridge-stale/siem-cr03-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer (sign-off required if `manual_cr03_match_count > 0`).

### §154.3 §80.3 R2 Storage Update

`siem-bridge-stale/` parent subfolder already exists from §153 (SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001, 2026-07-03). §154 adds the `siem-cr03-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` path within it. `r2:form-api` REVOKED (§80.3 invariant — no automated R2 write path for compliance evidence; IC PAM-elevated manual upload only).

### §154.4 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Update

SIEM-CR03-STALE-E-001 added to §80.4 Vanta mirror protocol. Mirror deadline: within 48h of per-activation artefact filing. Evidence class: per-activation (one artefact per R-66 stale incident). Security-engineer sign-off attestation must be present in artefact before Vanta mirror if `manual_cr03_match_count > 0`. Zero-activation period: no nil attestation required (per-activation artefact only; absence = no R-66 incidents in period).

## §155 · SCIM-MASS-MON-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC7.2 / A1.1 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-67)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-67.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register SCIM-MASS-MON-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table (CC7.2/A1.1; per-activation; 7yr; `compliance/evidence/scim-mass-deprovision/scim-mass-mon-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + security-engineer.

### §155.1 Background

Job 24 (`scim_mass_deprovision_check`, `*/5 * * * *`) is FORM's near-real-time SCIM mass deprovisioning detector: `fn_scim_mass_deprovision_check()` queries `audit_log_events` for `scim.user_deprovisioned` events grouped into 10-minute windows per tenant, and emits `scim.mass_deprovision_detected` HIGH via pg_net when ≥ 10% of a tenant's `seat_count` (minimum threshold 10) are deprovisioned in a single window, firing AL-SCIM-MASS-01 and activating R-24. When job 24 goes stale, FORM's CC7.2 anomalous-activity detection AND A1.1 availability-threat detection for this pattern are both blind — distinguishing it from CC7.2-only companion runbooks R-65/R-66. INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-67 is the companion stale recovery runbook for job 24; it requires a per-activation SCIM-MASS-MON-STALE-E-001 evidence artefact capturing: (1) the DEC-030 HMAC chain (`system.scim_mass_deprovision_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH + `system.scim_mass_deprovision_monitor_restored` LOW); (2) R-67-C2 manual detection output (tenant UUIDs + aggregate burst counts — no individual user IDs); (3) CSM lead sign-off if P0-escalate (undetected bursts found); (4) R-24 activation record if applicable. SCIM-MASS-MON-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `scim_mass_deprovision_monitor_restored` blocked (HTTP 422 `SCIM_MASS_MON_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) without prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; co-activates R-05.

### §155.2 §79.4 Registration

SCIM-MASS-MON-STALE-E-001 registered in §79.4 master evidence table (count 121 → 122). Evidence class: per-activation (one artefact per R-67 stale incident). Retention: 7yr WORM. TSC: CC7.2, A1.1. R2 path: `compliance/evidence/scim-mass-deprovision/scim-mass-mon-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer (CSM lead co-sign required if `initial_severity = p0_escalate`).

### §155.3 §80.3 R2 Storage Update

`scim-mass-deprovision/` is a **new** top-level subfolder under `compliance/evidence/` — no parent folder exists from a prior §80.3 registration (unlike `siem-bridge-stale/` which was first created for SIEM-CR02-STALE-E-001 in §153). Platform-engineer must provision `compliance/evidence/scim-mass-deprovision/` in R2 (write-once policy, versioning enabled) prior to the first R-67 activation. Per-incident path: `compliance/evidence/scim-mass-deprovision/scim-mass-mon-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` where `YYYY-MM-DD` is the UTC date of R-67 `stale_declared` emission. `r2:form-api` REVOKED (§80.3 invariant — no automated R2 write path for compliance evidence; IC PAM-elevated manual upload only).

### §155.4 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Update

SCIM-MASS-MON-STALE-E-001 added to §80.4 Vanta mirror protocol. Mirror deadline: within 48h of per-activation artefact filing. Evidence class: per-activation (one artefact per R-67 stale incident). CSM lead sign-off attestation must be present in artefact before Vanta mirror if `initial_severity = p0_escalate`. Zero-activation period: no nil attestation required (per-activation artefact only; absence = no R-67 incidents in period).

## §156 · AUDIT-CHAIN-CHECK-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC7.2 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-68)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-68.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register AUDIT-CHAIN-CHECK-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table (CC7.2; per-activation; 7yr; `compliance/evidence/audit-chain-check-stale/audit-chain-check-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`)." **Owner:** security-engineer + compliance-officer.

### §156.1 Background

`audit-chain-daily-check` (daily 06:00 UTC, Supabase Edge Function, 26h freshness window) is FORM's sole automated daily verifier of DEC-030 HMAC chain integrity across the full `audit_log_events` table. It is the only P0-severity job among the original 9 unnumbered §12.6 pg_cron registry entries. Unlike all prior companion stale runbooks (R-60 through R-67, which were P1 or P2 default), R-68 is P0 default with no severity fallback: when `audit-chain-daily-check` goes stale, FORM's CC7.2 HMAC chain integrity verification control is offline and FORM cannot provide automated SOC 2 evidence that the audit trail has not been tampered with during the stale window. INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-68 is the companion stale recovery runbook; it requires a per-activation AUDIT-CHAIN-CHECK-STALE-E-001 evidence artefact capturing: (1) the DEC-030 HMAC chain (`system.audit_chain_check_stale_declared` HIGH + `system.audit_chain_check_restored` LOW); (2) R-68-C2 manual chain linkage SQL output (sequence numbers + HMAC hash strings — no user data); (3) security-engineer sign-off confirming chain intact or R-05 co-activation record if chain breaks were detected. AUDIT-CHAIN-CHECK-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `audit_chain_check_restored` blocked (HTTP 422 `AUDIT_CHAIN_CHECK_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) without prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; co-activates R-05.

### §156.2 §79.4 Registration

AUDIT-CHAIN-CHECK-STALE-E-001 registered in §79.4 master evidence table (count 122 → 123). Evidence class: per-activation (one artefact per R-68 stale incident). Retention: 7yr WORM. TSC: CC7.2. R2 path: `compliance/evidence/audit-chain-check-stale/audit-chain-check-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`. Owner: security-engineer + compliance-officer (R-05 co-sign required if `r05_activated = true`).

### §156.3 §80.3 R2 Storage Update

`audit-chain-check-stale/` is a **new** top-level subfolder under `compliance/evidence/` — no parent folder exists from a prior §80.3 registration. Platform-engineer must provision `compliance/evidence/audit-chain-check-stale/` in R2 (write-once policy, versioning enabled) prior to the first R-68 activation. Per-incident path: `compliance/evidence/audit-chain-check-stale/audit-chain-check-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` where `YYYY-MM-DD` is the UTC date of R-68 `stale_declared` emission. `r2:form-api` REVOKED (§80.3 invariant — no automated R2 write path for compliance evidence; IC PAM-elevated manual upload only).

### §156.4 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Update

AUDIT-CHAIN-CHECK-STALE-E-001 added to §80.4 Vanta mirror protocol. Mirror deadline: within 48h of per-activation artefact filing. Evidence class: per-activation (one artefact per R-68 stale incident). Security-engineer sign-off attestation must be present in artefact before Vanta mirror. R-05 co-sign attestation required if `r05_activated = true`. Zero-activation period: no nil attestation required (per-activation artefact only; absence = no R-68 incidents in period).

## §157 · WEAR-FRESH-STALE-E-001 Registration (CC7.2 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-69)

> **Date:** 2026-07-03. **Trigger:** INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-69.12 item 3 (P1) — "Register WEAR-FRESH-STALE-E-001 in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table (CC7.2; per-activation; 7yr; `compliance/evidence/wearable/wear-fresh-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`)." **Owner:** compliance-officer + devops-lead.

### §157.1 Background

Job 31 (`wearable_sync_freshness_check`, `5 7 * * *`, daily 07:05 UTC) is FORM's automated daily measurement of wearable integration fleet freshness: `fn_wearable_sync_freshness_check()` queries `wearable_sync_events` per connected tenant, applies a k-anonymity gate (k ≥ 5 connected devices), computes `fresh_pct = fresh_count / total_connected × 100`, and emits `wearable.fleet_freshness_assessed` DEC-030 with aggregate scalars only. The emitted aggregate feeds WS-SLO-06 (≥ 95% fleet freshness — `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §41`) and the quarterly WS-E-002 compliance evidence artefact. When job 31 goes stale, FORM cannot confirm WS-SLO-06 status for the missed run windows without manual compensating measurement (R-69-C3). INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-69 is the companion stale recovery runbook; it is P1 default (distinct from R-68 which is P0 default — a stale wearable freshness check creates a compliance measurement gap, not a chain integrity break). The WEAR-FRESH-STALE-E-001 evidence artefact captures: (1) the DEC-030 HMAC chain (`system.wearable_freshness_check_stale_declared` HIGH + `system.wearable_freshness_check_restored` LOW); (2) R-69-C1 pg_cron run history output (timestamps, status strings, return messages — no user data); (3) R-69-C2 peer job health output (H3 discriminator); (4) R-69-C3 manual fleet freshness snapshot result (aggregate `fresh_pct`, `total_connected`, `slo_met` only — no `user_id`, no individual readings). WEAR-FRESH-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `wearable_freshness_check_restored` blocked (HTTP 422 `WEAR_FRESH_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) without prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; co-activates R-05.

### §157.2 §79.4 Registration

WEAR-FRESH-STALE-E-001 registered in §79.4 master evidence table (count 123 → 124). Evidence class: per-activation (one artefact per R-69 stale incident). Retention: 7yr WORM. TSC: CC7.2. R2 path: `compliance/evidence/wearable/wear-fresh-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`. Owner: compliance-officer + devops-lead (enterprise SLA assessment note required in artefact if stale > 48h and enterprise wearable impact confirmed; nil-impact signed attestation if no SLA impact).

### §157.3 §80.3 R2 Storage Update

`compliance/evidence/wearable/` is a **pre-existing** top-level subfolder under `compliance/evidence/` — it was first provisioned for the quarterly WS-E-002 wearable integration fleet freshness evidence artefact (`docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §41.9`). No new R2 subfolder provisioning is required for WEAR-FRESH-STALE-E-001; the per-incident path `compliance/evidence/wearable/wear-fresh-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/` simply adds a new subdirectory within the already-provisioned `wearable/` folder on each R-69 activation. `r2:form-api` REVOKED (§80.3 invariant — no automated R2 write path for compliance evidence; IC PAM-elevated manual upload only). `YYYY-MM-DD` is the UTC date of the R-69 `stale_declared` emission.

### §157.4 §80.4 Vanta Mirror Update

WEAR-FRESH-STALE-E-001 added to §80.4 Vanta mirror protocol. Mirror deadline: within 48h of per-activation artefact filing. Evidence class: per-activation (one artefact per R-69 stale incident). Enterprise SLA impact assessment note (or signed nil-impact attestation) must be present in artefact before Vanta mirror if stale > 48h. Zero-activation period: no nil attestation required (per-activation artefact only; absence = no R-69 incidents in period).

### §157.5 Cross-Reference Obligations Closed

| INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-69.12 item 3 (P1 — register WEAR-FRESH-STALE-E-001 in §79.4) | [x] Done — 2026-07-03 (§157.2, this section) |
